I_kwDOEYAvFc58nQLY.Title: Date plugin: Format the date from frontmatter exactly as I specified it
I_kwDOEYAvFc58nQLY.Author: brookback
I_kwDOEYAvFc58nQLY.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Hi! Not sure I'm holding this wrong, but I've got a (deceivingly simple) date problem.

## Request

I want the specified date and time from my Markdown frontmatter to show in the built site. Currently, all dates and times are formatted in UTC (or whatever `TZ` is on the machine building the site).

## Use case

For the `date` field in my posts' frontmatter, I use my **current local time with time zone offset**. Like this:

```
---
date: 2024-01-18 20:44:58 +7
location: Koh Lanta, Thailand
---
```

Note the included timezone offset.

When formatting that in the templates, I want that date and time to show. It works on my machine (classic!), but when building on CI for deploying, dates are showing in UTC.

Using the format string `PPP — HH:mm`:

```
January 18th, 2024 — 13:44
```
when I want:
```
January 18th, 2024 — 20:44
```

I just want whatever I input as the formatted output 😄

I've read about setting the `TZ` var when building with `lume`, but I can't do that, since the local time for each post might vary (I might post across timezones).

Also, I can't do my own hacky date parsing and formatting, since I don't have access to the raw frontmatter record on the `Page` data: I only have the already parsed `date: Date`.

Maybe this is out of scope for Lume, but I thought I'd ask. Thanks for a never ending great job ❤️

***

**Info**

- Lume version:  v1.13.1
- OS: macOS 14.2.1

**Related:**

- https://blog.jim-nielsen.com/2023/date-and-time-in-ssg/. Known problem for other SSGs 🤷 
- https://github.com/lumeland/lume/issues/391. Similar issue, but not exactly the same.

Comments.Body:
As an idea, you can preprocess the pages to change the `Date`. Let's say you create a new variable with the time zone:

```yml
date: 2024-01-18 20:44:58
timezone: Asia/Bangkok
```

And then process the pages assigning the correct timezone.

Lume 2 uses [Temporal polyfill](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@js-temporal/polyfill) (in `/deps/temporal.ts`) which has functions to work with timezones ([see docs here](https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/docs/#Temporal-ZonedDateTime)).

Let me know if you can solve it with this. If it works fine, maybe this could be a Lume plugin (Some people ask me about using different timezones in multilanguage sites).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
@oscarotero Thanks for replying.

Yes, that'd work too, with the added benefit of documentation in the frontmatter.

But if not, would it be improper to suggest that Lume provides the frontmatter as a part of the `Page` interface somewhere? As I touched upon in:

> Also, I can't do my own hacky date parsing and formatting, since I don't have access to the raw frontmatter record on the Page data: I only have the already parsed date: Date.

As "raw" as possible, meaning, no `string → Date` parsing whatsoever. Just raw strings (and maybe `number | boolean` :)).

Or do you consider it leaky?
Comments.Author: brookback

Comments.Body: That's no easy. The date parsing is not performed by Lume, but by yaml parsed https://deno.land/std@0.212.0/front_matter/mod.ts
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I have only just started using Lume an  ran into the same challenge that original timezone offsets do not make it into the page's `date` object, which is to be expected with JavaScript `Date` objects.  I would have hoped the raw string would still be there somewhere, but I could not find it.

I believe it happens in: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/f973940e5d3714074cec055aed9328eb2c863815/core/utils/date.ts#L30

`front_matter` appears to just return the raw string, from what I can tell:

```js
> extractYaml(`---
date: '2016-01-01T15:42:00.001+11:00'
---
`)
{
  frontMatter: "date: '2016-01-01T15:42:00.001+11:00'",
  body: "",
  attrs: { date: "2016-01-01T15:42:00.001+11:00" }
}
```

Lume is using `Temporal` internally to parse the string, but is converting it to a `Date` object, rather than returning  e.g. a `ZonedDateTime`, which would preserve all info.
```js 
Temporal.ZonedDateTime.from("2016-01-01T15:42:00+11:00[Australia/Sydney]")
```
A date with offset `"2016-01-01T15:42:00+11:00` but without timezone  info (e.g. `[Australia/Sydney]`) would not work, if I read the [docs](https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/docs/zoneddatetime.html#static-methods) correctly, though.  
So, all your date strings would need to include the timezone. Otherwise Lume would need to use `Temporal.Instant` which would also loose the parsed offset information. 

It would be great if Lume either preserved the original unparsed date (or whole plain front_matter object) in the page data or provide a hook for parsing it. A`site.prepprocess` hook already is already too late, as the date has already been parsed.
Comments.Author: kollhof

Comments.Body:
@kollhof Nice find, I didn't know Lume was using Temporal internally.

Out of curiosity, how do you plan on formatting the timezone-aware dates in your markup?

I'd love for this end-to-end flow to work:

1. Have an ISO date with timezone `date: "2016-01-01T15:42:00+11:00[Australia/Sydney]"` in the frontmatter.
2. Lume keeps this exact ISO string somewhere on the page's data object for me to do things with, if needed. Also parse it to a `Temporal.ZonedDateTime` and keep on the page data.
3. When formatting — maybe with the `date` filter — the timezone is carried through and taken into consideration when formatting according to some string or with the `Intl` API.
Comments.Author: brookback

Comments.Body:
Lume uses Temporal internally to parse the `date` if it wasn't already parsed by the yaml parser. If the date is unquoted, the YAML package (from std) parse it automatically ([here's the code](https://jsr.io/@std/yaml/1.0.2/_type/timestamp.ts)):

```yaml
date: 2016-01-01T15:42:00.001+11:00   # parsed by @std/yaml
date: '2016-01-01T15:42:00.001+11:00' # parsed by lume
```

What Lume does is to normalize the Date parsing to match yaml behavior in those cases in which the date is not parsed by yaml: i.e. if it's exported as a quoted date, if has a special value like `Git Created` or if it's included in the filename (`2016-01-01-example.md`).

As an idea, you can use a different named variable to store the date as you want. For example, `tz_date` to store the time zoned date (must be quoted, to prevent being parsed by YAML parser):

```yaml
tz_date: '2016-01-01T15:42:00.001+11:00'
```

Then, you can create a preprocessor to parse the date as you want and even modify the `date` variable if you need to.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Interesting, I was not aware of the yaml parser doing that, thanks.
Comments.Author: kollhof

I_kwDOEYAvFc5lW-CH.Title: File deleted from _site/ during watch if not changed
I_kwDOEYAvFc5lW-CH.Author: fserb
I_kwDOEYAvFc5lW-CH.Body:
### Version

1.17.0

### Platform

mac

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

This is my minimal repro. `_config.js`:

```js
import lume from "lume/mod.ts";

const site = lume();
site.copy([".js"]); // it also works if you do terser, for example, and also with css copy or css postcss.
export default site;
```

create a file `a.js` with a anything on it and run `lume -w`.

If you check, there will be a `_site/a.js` file with the correct content.

Now in a separate terminal, run `touch a.js`. Lume will show: 
```
Changes detected:
- /a.js
```

but now the file disappears from `_site`. If you actually change `a.js` content, then everything works as expected (the file gets updated).



### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

I tried to look into the code and I have no idea how this can be happening, so maybe is some weird OS-specific, thing? I've created a new project from scratch and I'm always able to repro.

### What is the expected behavior?

The file to stay on `_site` when it changed but the content hasn't changed.

### What do you see instead?

The file disappears from `_site` until I re-run lume.

### Additional information

_No response_

Comments.Body:
Thanks for opening this issue. I can confirm it's a bug on Lume 1.17, sorry for the trouble.
I will fix it ASAP.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Fixed in v1.17.1.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Btw, I'm still getting errors like this, where the file disappears from _site if it's saved again but was supposed to generate the same output.
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body: Is there any way to reproduce the error?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Use the exact same set up as before, but instead of `a.js`, do it with `index.md`. 
If it's any other `.md` it works fine, but if it's called `index.md` (and becomes `index.html`), then the same problem happens.
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body: (btw, I can't re-open the bug)
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body:
Fixed in 1.17.4
Sorry for the trouble.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc55PI9P.Title: Lightning CSS can’t be configured to use a different includes folder
I_kwDOEYAvFc55PI9P.Author: paulrobertlloyd
I_kwDOEYAvFc55PI9P.Body:
### Version

2.0.0

### Platform

Deno 1.38.5

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Lume options:

```js
const site = lume({
  src: "./src",
  includes: "_layouts",
});
```

LigntingCSS options:

```js
site.use(lightningCss({
  includes: "_styles",
  drafts: {
    customMedia: true,
    nesting: true,
  },
}));
```

CSS file:

`src/app.css` trying to import from `src/_styles/config/_custom-properties.css`:

```css
@import url("config/_custom-properties.css");
```

### What is the expected behavior?

CSS file gets bundled.

### What do you see instead?

Results in the following error:

```sh
Error: No result
    at napi_create_error (file:///Users/paulrobertlloyd/Library/Caches/deno/npm/registry.npmjs.org/napi-wasm/1.0.1/index.mjs:866:15)
    at <anonymous> (wasm://wasm/02a4df8e:1:7124758)
    at <anonymous> (wasm://wasm/02a4df8e:1:7118080)
    at <anonymous> (wasm://wasm/02a4df8e:1:7110732)
    at Object.bundle (file:///Users/paulrobertlloyd/Library/Caches/deno/npm/registry.npmjs.org/napi-wasm/1.0.1/index.mjs:248:19)
    at bundleAsync (file:///Users/paulrobertlloyd/Library/Caches/deno/npm/registry.npmjs.org/lightningcss-wasm/1.22.1/async.mjs:68:21)
    at async lightningCSSBundler (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.0/plugins/lightningcss.ts:143:24)
    at async Processors.run (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.0/core/processors.ts:31:7)
    at async Site.#buildPages (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.0/core/site.ts:638:5)
    at async Site.build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.0/core/site.ts:506:9) {
  code: "GenericFailure",
  fileName: "/Users/paulrobertlloyd/Sites/paulrobertlloyd-v5/src/app.css",
  loc: { line: 2, column: 1 },
  data: "ResolverError"
}
```

### Additional information

Moving `config/_custom-properties.css` to `src/_layouts` folder (which is set as `site.options.includes`), the error disappears.

From a quick glance, it looks the `includes` option gets overridden by `site.options.includes` here, and perhaps `userOptions` isn’t taken? https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.0/plugins/lightningcss.ts?source#L57

Comments.Body:
Hi. Thanks for this. It's indeed a bug here: https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.0.0/plugins/lightningcss.ts?source#L112
The plugin is getting the includes value from `site.options` instead of the plugin options.
I'll fix it.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Fixed in Lume v2.0.1
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Brill, thanks for the quick fix!
Comments.Author: paulrobertlloyd

MDU6SXNzdWU3NzYyMDE5NjA=.Title: Benchmarks
MDU6SXNzdWU3NzYyMDE5NjA=.Author: shadowtime2000
MDU6SXNzdWU3NzYyMDE5NjA=.Body: It would be great to see some build time benchmarks compared to popular SSGs such as Jekyll and 11ty. @probins did share the speed increase in his sites build time compared to Jeykll.

Comments.Body:
The times I posted were the total time setting up GitLab Pages, where much of the time is spent loading the Docker image and deploying the pages, not just running Lume. Deno programs are slim by design. To run Lume, you just need Deno and not much else. So you can use a small Docker image such as Alpine Linux with Deno already included. For Jekyll, you have to use the Ruby image, which is a lot larger and takes a lot longer to load. Jekyll also has a lot more baggage. Node is much the same: you have to spend a lot of time installing a large number of 'required' packages, much of which you probably don't need. You could of course create your own custom Docker image with everything you need already loaded, which would be faster to run.

I suppose it depends on what you want to compare. In my case, I'm interested in the total time from `git push` to deployment. If you just want comparisons of actual run time, you would install everything first, and then run the different systems one after another.
Comments.Author: probins

Comments.Body:
I did some tests locally using a clone of this repo: https://github.com/seancdavis/ssg-build-performance-tests (disabling nuxt, next and gatsby due they are incredible slow).
I'll add the repo to this organization, if you want to take a look and play with it.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I've created this repository with the code to run benchmarks and the first results:
https://github.com/lumeland/benchmark

(I wasn't able to make work the [other repository](https://github.com/seancdavis/ssg-build-performance-tests))

Hugo is unbeatable, but lume is in second position, except with big sites (10,000 pages) that jekyll seems more performant.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5n3WPr.Title: Dots in filenames lead to missing `.html` extension
I_kwDOEYAvFc5n3WPr.Author: not-my-profile
I_kwDOEYAvFc5n3WPr.Body:
Like any other static-site generator Lume by default processes `foo.md` to `foo/index.html`. However if the filename contains a dot, e.g. `foo.bar.md` then Lume currently writes the HTML to `foo.bar` ... rather than writing to `foo.bar/index.html` as you would expect (case in point Jekyll and Eleventy and I'd assume most other static-site generators behave such by default).

It's worth noting that setting `prettyUrls: false` doesn't change the Lume behavior: it still writes the HTML to `foo.bar` rather than `foo.bar.html`, as you would expect. The relevant code appears to be the `getUrl` function in `core/source.ts`.

While changing the Lume default behavior regarding this would be a breaking change, I'd consider conforming to user expectations and common practice to be enough justification.

As a workaround you can currently [specify a relative URL](https://lume.land/docs/creating-pages/urls/#relative-urls) in the page frontmatter, such as `url: ./foo.bar/`.

Comments.Body:
This behavior is intentional. In Lume you can create other files than HTML pages. For example, you can create a css file from nunjucks simply by setting the name to `/styles.css.njk`. Lume removes the template extension and, if the remaining name contains an extension, it's keept.

Related: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/discussions/113
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Right that makes sense if the last extension is a template extension such as `.njk` but it doesn't make sense for `.md` as the last extension as far as I can tell. So perhaps we could introduce some distinction?

Conceptually `.md$` maps to `.html` and `.njk$` maps to the empty string.
Comments.Author: not-my-profile

Comments.Body: It's true that `md` is intended for HTML, but I think making Lume so clever can be confusing because similar things works differently. In theory, you can use markdown to build anything. Imagine, for example, the md file uses a layout that converts this html code into a svg file (or even render it to a PNG file). You may want to name this file `filename.png.md`. :)
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: For the vary rare case that you actually want an `.md` file to produce a non-HTML file you can just specify the `url` in the YAML front matter. I think it's much more common to have a regular `.md` file with another dot in the filename.
Comments.Author: not-my-profile
oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> so you can create this page with `404.html.md`

I believe file extensions should describe the content of the file. I'd consider conflating actual file extensions describing the content of the file with file extensions describing the output format of some conversion process to be an anti-pattern. I'd consider any `.html.md` file to be confusing.

> this introduces some cognitive load in how Lume works and make its behavior less predictable

What is more predictable?

(A)

* `foo.md` -> `foo/index.html`
* `foo.bar.md` -> `foo.bar`
* `foo.bar.baz.md` -> `foo.baz`

or (B)?

* `foo.md` -> `foo/index.html`
* `foo.bar.md` -> `foo.bar/index.html`
* `foo.bar.baz.md` -> `foo.bar.baz/index.html`

A) is the current default behavior of Lume and B) is the default behavior of Jekyll and Eleventy. I find B) to be much more predictable.  (Granted that this is particularly true because in A3 Lume currently omits the `.bar` from the output filename, which is caused by the slug regex.)

> To me, a file shouldn't have a dot in the name, because this is introduces some ambiguity. You can request the url `/foo/bar.png` expecting a png file but it opens the html page `/foo/bar.png/index.html`.

Correct, you cannot assume a file type just from a URL ... this however is absolutely not a problem because web browsers determine the file type via the `Content-Type` HTTP header. Note that even if the default Lume behavior is changed, there is nothing stopping you from still avoiding dots in your filenames, if you prefer it that way.

On the contrary the current Lume default behavior poses an inconvenience to any user that wants to e.g. mention a version number in a file name, something even the Lume website does: https://lume.land/blog/posts/lume-1.17.0-release-notes
Comments.Author: not-my-profile

Comments.Body:
Okay, let's mimic the Jekyll / Eleventy behavior but I don't want to introduce a breaking change now. 
I'm going to put this on hold until Lume 2.0 that will introduce many breaking changes.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Fixed in the v2 branch.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5SO8vG.Title: Use minimum system permissions necessary
I_kwDOEYAvFc5SO8vG.Author: cmoog
I_kwDOEYAvFc5SO8vG.Body:
I noticed that the default dev script invokes itself with full permissions over the entire system. Ideally, Lume should function fine if invoked with the following minimum permissions.

```console
$ deno run --allow-net=localhost:3000 --allow-read=./ --allow-write=./_site lume/task.ts
```

Comments.Body:
It's not so easy, for several reasons:

- This depends on the site configuration. For example, if the site is configured to use a different `dest` folder or if the server port is different (like `8080` instead of `3000`), the command should change also the permissions configuration.
- `deno run lume/task.ts` doesn't work, because the `lume/task.ts` file is not resolved with the import map, so you have to include the full file url: `deno run https://deno.land/x/lume@1.11.4/task.ts`. But this introduce another url to maintain and keep up to date on update Lume, in addition to the Lume url in the import_map.json file. This is why the lume task is `deno eval "import 'lume/task.ts'"` because `deno eval` takes the import_map.json file into account to resolve the imports, making this file a single source of truth. More info about `deno run` and `deno eval` here: https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/14760
- `deno eval` has implicit all permissions (it's like `--allow-all`).
- `lume/task.ts` file just run https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/ci.ts and this file run the real deno command to run Lume with all required parameters. More info: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/ci.ts#L107-L115

I'd like to simplify this in the near future, when this proposal is implemented: https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/12763 so you can configure the permissions in the Deno config file.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> This depends on the site configuration.

Point taken. Then the minimum permissions for most projects would be `--allow-net=localhost --allow-read=./ --allow-write=./`. If some plugin needs to make other network requests, the user should need to explicitly allow that.

> deno eval has implicit all permissions (it's like --allow-all).

After reading [this issue](https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/14760), I can't help but feel the current behavior is a big mistake. If my understanding is correct, the lack of support for import maps in `deno run` means the only ergonomic way to invoke "framework" CLIs is without any sandboxing. This erases a major benefit of Deno's security model.

And to preempt, I don't think this is bikeshedding. Given that Lume is plugin-based, it's plausible that there will be a long tail of unaudited, untrusted plugins. Why should plugins have exec permissions?

### Potential (temporary) solution

I haven't thought about this too much... but this came across my mind as a potential solution. This way, only one line of code runs with global permissions, then the `task.ts` process itself is sandboxed.

```json
{
  "importMap": "import_map.json",
  "tasks": {
    "lume": "deno run --allow-read=./ --allow-write=./ --allow-net=localhost $(deno eval \"console.log(JSON.parse(Deno.readTextFileSync('import_map.json')).imports['lume/'])\")task.ts",
    "build": "deno task lume",
    "serve": "deno task lume -s"
  }
}
```

Of course this would also require changes to `ci.ts` and `task.ts` to remove the unnecessary self-invocation.
Comments.Author: cmoog

Comments.Body:
`task.ts` and `ci.ts` works as wrappers to execute the real CLI script (`cli.ts`). This wrapper [fixes different issues](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/ci.ts#L12-L19). So, restricting permissions to these files has no effect to `cli.ts`(AFAIK, permissions are not propagated between childprocesses: a file with only `--allow-run` could run a script passing the `--allow-all` argument).

The use cases are so variable that this is the reason I've decided to run `cli.ts` with `--allow-all`, for example:
- Sites that fetch data from an external API, needs `allow-net` or `allow-net=api-url`.
- Sites that have different configurations depending on the environment variables (like `ENV=dev`) needs `--allow-env`.
- Sites that need to run some scripts during the build needs `--allow-run`
- Sites that use some binary libraries connected with ffi needs `--allow-ffi`.
- Sites that use WebAssembly libraries needs `--allow-read` to read the WebAssembly file.

Restricting the permissions right now can break many sites. I think it's better to let the users configure the permisisions once Deno team implements permission configuration in the deno.json file. In Lume 2.0 I'm planning to remove these wrapper files and depend only on deno.json that will be mandatory (unlike now, that both deno.json and import_map.json files are optional).

If you're concerned about that, the correct way to apply permissions is by running the `cli.ts` file directly. You can create a task like this:

```json
{
  "importMap": "import_map.json",
  "tasks": {
    "lume": "deno run --unstable --allow-read=./ --allow-write=./ --allow-net=localhost https://lume.land/x/lume@1.11.4/cli.ts",
    "build": "deno task lume",
    "serve": "deno task lume -s"
  }
}
```

This conversation makes me think that a Permissions section in the documentation website would be really useful for users that want to restrict these permissions.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
>This conversation makes me think that a Permissions section in the documentation website would be really useful for users that want to restrict these permissions.

This sounds like a great idea! It will also be useful for raising awareness of permissions considerations among users who wouldn't otherwise be thinking about it.

I don't think I know enough about Deno's permissions API or Lume's requirements to write this myself, but I'd be very happy to help with writing this documentation if anyone feels like collaborating on it.
Comments.Author: bryophyta

Comments.Body:
Permissions are explained here: https://lume.land/docs/advanced/permissions/
Closing this.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc49SC0T.Title: Lume (local) server always warns "Unable to detect your local IP address"
I_kwDOEYAvFc49SC0T.Author: x80486
I_kwDOEYAvFc49SC0T.Body:
Every time I start the (local) server I'm getting this warning: `Unable to detect your local IP address`.

```shell
[x80486@uplink:~/lume-template]$ lume --serve
Loading config file /home/x80486/lume-template/_config.ts

🔥 /index.html /index.njk
🔥 /global.css /global.css

🍾 Site built into _site/
No such file or directory (os error 2)

  Server started at:
  http://localhost:3000/ (local)
Warning Unable to detect your local IP address
If you're on an Ubuntu machine, try installing net-tools with 'apt install net-tools'

200 http://localhost:3000/
```

I'm running Arch Linux, Deno `1.15.1`, and Lume `v1.1.1`:

```shell
[x80486@uplink:~/lume-template]$ uname -a 
Linux uplink 5.10.72-1-lts #1 SMP Sat, 09 Oct 2021 17:34:39 +0000 x86_64 GNU/Linux
[x80486@uplink:~/lume-template]$ deno --version 
deno 1.15.1 (release, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu)
v8 9.5.172.19
typescript 4.4.2
[x80486@uplink:~/lume-template]$ lume --version 
v1.1.1
```

Is this something that could be solve changing something in Lume, or is it like that because of Deno? I would really like that warning to disappear, even though it doesn't do any harm 😆

Comments.Body: This is because Lume uses [deno-local-ip](https://github.com/joakimunge/deno-local-ip) to detect the local-ip of the local server and this package depends on `net-tools` installed in your computer. I'm not familiarized with Arch Linux, so don't know if you can install it. Perhaps `pacman -S net-tools`??
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Indeed, it's looking for `ifconfig` on non-Windows systems...and yes, I don't have it 😏

Feel free to close this one, since there is nothing you can do, if the IP is certainly needed.
Comments.Author: x80486

I_kwDOEYAvFc6056Bd.Title: Transform Images plugin: Animation Lost When Input/Output Formats Are Not Both GIF
I_kwDOEYAvFc6056Bd.Author: akimon658
I_kwDOEYAvFc6056Bd.Body:
### Version

v2.5.2

### Platform

_No response_

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Process an animated image (GIF or WebP) with input and output formats that are not both GIF. Animation is lost during this processing.

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

Always, when processing an animated GIF or WebP image and the input and output formats are not both GIF.

### What is the expected behavior?

Animated images should remain animated after processing, regardless of the output format (unless a static output is explicitly intended).

### What do you see instead?

The output image is a static GIF or WebP, losing its animation.

### Additional information

Commit [62c5e0c](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/commit/62c5e0cdf6d61235805fb21979c1c83cb00f9a45) seems to have introduced a regression, limiting animation preservation to only GIF-to-GIF processing. This causes animated GIFs and WebP images to become static in all other conversion scenarios.

Comments.Body:
The commit you mentioned fixed another issue: if an animated gif image is loaded and converted to a not animated format like jpeg, it converts all frames, creating a huge image with all frames that can cause the error `VipsJpeg: Maximum supported image dimension is 65500 pixels` if the image is too big.

(The error was reported in [a Discord conversation](https://discord.com/channels/794537085641818124/794537086278828094/1324884811696832727))

This is why only load all frames if the intention is maintain the animation. What other formats should support animations?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Given that Sharp's animation support is likely limited to GIF and WebP, focusing on preserving animation for these two formats would be sufficient to address the current issue.
Comments.Author: akimon658

Comments.Body:
Okay. I just made a change to preserve the animation between gif and webp formats.
It will be available in the next version
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5RAQIe.Title: `search.pages()` and `search.page()` should return the `data` object instead of the full `Page` instance.
I_kwDOEYAvFc5RAQIe.Author: oscarotero
I_kwDOEYAvFc5RAQIe.Body:
This makes working with pages easier. For example, instead of this: ```njk {% for article in search.pages("type=article", "date=desc") %}

{{ article.data.title }}

 {% endfor %} ``` It would become to this: ```njk {% for article in search.pages("type=article", "date=desc") %}

{{ article.title }}

 {% endfor %} ``` If it's necesary to access to the `Page` instance (to get the `src` data, for example) it's available at `data.page`. Example: ```njk {% for article in search.pages("type=article", "date=desc") %}

{{ article.title }}

Loaded from {{ article.page.src.path }} {% endfor %} ``` Due this is a breaking change, it will be in Lume 2.0.

Comments.Body:
I like this but aren't the functions `search.pages()` and `search.page()` signalling that the return type would be a `Page` (at least from naming perspective)? 

For data, I'd say, there should be `search.data()` which can return `Array<Data> | Data` depending on filter. If we choose to add `search.data()`, we can bring it in 1.x by deprecating `search.pages()` and `search.page()`.
Comments.Author: naiyerasif

Comments.Body:
`search.data()` already exits and returns the data associated to a file or directory. For example `search.data("/")` returns the data associated to the root directory (the _data files/folders), and `search.data("/post/first-post.md")` the data associated to this specific page. In Lume `1.11`, I've added the `data` filter as a temporary solution, so it's possible to do the following: ```njk {% for article in search.pages("type=article", "date=desc") | data %}

{{ article.title }}

Loaded from {{ article.page.src.path }} {% endfor %} ``` But I think the `Page` instance is something internal, a implementation detail that users working with templates shouldn't need to know.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Might it be possible to enable this behaviour in v1.0 via an option in the plugin’s config?
Comments.Author: paulrobertlloyd

Comments.Body: Yes, it's a good idea.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Implemented in v2 branch https://github.com/lumeland/lume/pull/446
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc6R2QY0.Title: Feeds plugin: item.updated?.toISOString is not a function
I_kwDOEYAvFc6R2QY0.Author: basus
I_kwDOEYAvFc6R2QY0.Body:
### Version

2.2.3

### Platform

macOS Sonoma 14.5, Deno 1.45.2

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

I'm using the feeds plugin to create a RSS feed where I set the `updated` in `items` as `=modified`. The `items` key looks like:

```
items: {
        title: "=title",
        description: "=description",
        published: "=date",
        updated: "=modified",
        image: "=cover"
    }
```

I have a number of Markdown files, some that have a `modified` front matter field, some that don't. The ones that do have them in ISO format like `modified: "2022-05-24T17:00:00"`.

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

_No response_

### What is the expected behavior?

_No response_

### What do you see instead?

I get the following error when running `lume` or `lume --serve`:

```
TypeError: item.updated?.toISOString is not a function                                      
    at https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.2.3/plugins/feed.ts:248:43                                
    at Array.map (<anonymous>)                                                               
    at generateRss (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.2.3/plugins/feed.ts:237:26)                  
    at https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.2.3/plugins/feed.ts:188:53                                
    at Events.dispatchEvent (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.2.3/core/events.ts:46:19)           
    at Site.dispatchEvent (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.2.3/core/site.ts:274:24)              
    at Site.#buildPages (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.2.3/core/site.ts:700:23)                
    at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:168:7)                                             
    at async Site.build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.2.3/core/site.ts:532:9)                 
    at async build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.2.3/cli/build.ts:22:3)
```

### Additional information

Removing the updated key makes everything work fine, of course with no `updated` field in the RSS.

Comments.Body: Yes, this is a bug. The value expects a `Date` instance, but it's getting a `string`.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Hi, I just fixed this bug and will be available in the next Lume version.
For now, you can upgrade Lume to the latest development version with `deno task lume upgrade --dev`.
Feel free to reopen this if it doesn't work.
Thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5OF9g8.Title: Recommended way to add post-`use()` configuration to markdown-it plugins?
I_kwDOEYAvFc5OF9g8.Author: bryophyta

Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: I just added the `rules` option to markdown. It will be available in the next version.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> I just added the rules option to markdown. It will be available in the next version.

wow, thanks for such a quick response!

just to clarify one point from your original response -- is the `site.formats.get()` method that you mentioned a part of lume's external API? Or is it only designed for use within the internals of lume? (I'm partly wondering whether as users we can consider it to be a stable API or not.)
Comments.Author: bryophyta

Comments.Body:
I only consider stable API all `site` functions that allows to configure the site building (`site.copy()`, `site.use()`, `site.ignore()`, etc (all functions documented in the lume.land site).

All internal objects, like `site.formats`, could change in the future, for performance improvements, implementing new features, etc. I think they won't change much in the short term, but I cannot confirm 100%.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5E9l_h.Title: Build only one or some page
I_kwDOEYAvFc5E9l_h.Author: elycheikhsmail
I_kwDOEYAvFc5E9l_h.Body:
Is possible to build only one or some pages ?
something like
lume build post1.njk

Comments.Body:
No, Lume works with directories.
You could do it programaticaly, but only render the page, not save it:

```ts
const page = site.renderPage("post1.njk");
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5ynMua.Title: Allow Lume to run offline
I_kwDOEYAvFc5ynMua.Author: cvburgess
I_kwDOEYAvFc5ynMua.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

When trying to run `deno task lume --serve` lume dies with the following error:

```
TypeError: error sending request for url (https://cdn.deno.land/lume/meta/versions.json): error trying to connect: dns error: failed to lookup address information: nodename nor servname provided, or not known
    at async mainFetch (ext:deno_fetch/26_fetch.js:277:12)
    at async fetch (ext:deno_fetch/26_fetch.js:501:7)
    at async getLatestVersion (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.19.1/core/utils.ts:114:20)
    at async checkUpgrade (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.19.1/core/utils.ts:97:7)
    at async build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.19.1/cli/build.ts:57:5)
    at async Command.execute (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.7/command/command.ts:1794:7)
    at async Command.parseCommand (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.7/command/command.ts:1639:14)
    at async (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.19.1/cli.ts:135:3)
```

Fetching this JSON file should fail gracefully - especially for re-builds - so devs can work from planes or trains with no issue!

Comments.Body:
Good point.
This request is done once per day, just to make sure you're using the latest version of Lume. Then it's saved in the localstorage so you can run Lume offline without problems.
But I'm agree that it shouldn't fail on offline environment. I'll add a try/catch in the next version.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5bEQbW.Title: Access rendered content from outer pages
I_kwDOEYAvFc5bEQbW.Author: valpackett
I_kwDOEYAvFc5bEQbW.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Inside the page layout, `{{ content }}` is the rendered content, already processed with all the `templateEngine`s.

Within `search.pages` results, there is only entirely unprocessed content, and one has to basically redo the entire pipeline manually. And I can't even get the `njk` filter—maybe due to `{% import %}` stuff in the content—to work in that context.

It would be great if the rendered content was cached and available for access in pages with higher `renderOrder`.

Comments.Body:
Hi 👋 

> And I can't even get the njk filter—maybe due to {% import %} stuff in the content—to work in that context.

I don't understand this. The njk filter should be available always, for example:

```
{{ content | njk }}
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I mean, using it just quietly ruins the output in a weird way, even cutting off a lot of things after the usage tag. Templates: ```html {% macro preview(post) -%}

  • {{ post.title }}

    {{ post.date | date('HUMAN_DATE') }} ✦ {{ post.content | md | striptags | reading_time }} min read

    {{ post.desc }} ✦ read more →

    • {%- endmacro %} ``` ```html {% import "blog.njk" as blog %}

    Blog

      {%- for post in search.pages("blog=true", "date=desc") | data %} {{ blog.preview(post) }} {%- endfor %}
    ``` Output: ```html

    • Yet another keyboard post, or, introducing ErgoNICE

      January 9th, 2023 ✦ 20 min read

      I made a custom split mechanical keyboard! Because it's me, this involved things like contributing to a PCB design tool and discovering a ridiculously optimized way to read a keyboard matrix. ✦ read more →

    • FreeBSD and custom firmware on the Google Pixelbook

      October 5th, 2019 ✦ 15 min read

      A search for a new thin-and-light laptop, a journey through the Chromebook firmware trust architecture, some FreeBSD kernel development, and finally, something about actually customizing open source firmware. ✦ read more →

      • ..... ``` Now if we change to `{{ post.content | njk | md | striptags | reading_time }}` or `{{ post.content | njk(post) | md | striptags | reading_time }}` or `{{ post.content | njk }}` or `{{ post.content | njk(post) }}`: ```html

      • Yet another keyboard post, or, introducing ErgoNICE

        January 9th, 2023 ✦

      • FreeBSD and custom firmware on the Google Pixelbook

        October 5th, 2019 ✦

      • ``` The full source repo for the site is [here](https://codeberg.org/valpackett/val.packett.cool).
Comments.Author: valpackett
oscarotero

Comments.Body: Ohh, that's so clever! I hadn't thought to use a js layout for that. Thanks!!
Comments.Author: valpackett

Comments.Body:
Yeah, it's not designed for that, but it's a nice way to edit the page data dynamically from the layouts :)

BTW, I just saw your site and it's great. Do you mind if I include it in the [Showcase section](https://lume.land/showcase/)?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: oh please do include it :3
Comments.Author: valpackett

Comments.Body:
Done!
Thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc64Fbh6.Title: Google font file output to _includes
I_kwDOEYAvFc64Fbh6.Author: RickCogley
I_kwDOEYAvFc64Fbh6.Body: I noticed I can't specify a file in src/_includes as an output path. Is this by design? I was thinking it would help when you are bundling.

Comments.Body: You can configure the plugin to output the code in the final css file. For example, if your CSS is bundled to `/style.css`, you can configure google font to generate the code in this file.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
True. I was trying to avoid updating the name of the target to-be-bundled css file in both the `<head>` and in the google fonts setup. 😸 

I was thinking, if I can specify a generic location under `src/_includes` I could then in turn reference that in an `@import`. 

It could cause weird timing trouble though. At any rate, well noted, thanks!
Comments.Author: RickCogley

I_kwDOEYAvFc65Lir9.Title: Feed plugin does not seem to honor lang
I_kwDOEYAvFc65Lir9.Author: RickCogley
I_kwDOEYAvFc65Lir9.Body:
### Version

3.0.2

### Platform

MacOS

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

I have a multilanguage site at https://github.com/eSolia/blog.esolia.pro, published to https://blog.esolia.pro, and the settings I have made for the feed plugin are not taking effect. 

I have it set up here:
https://github.com/eSolia/blog.esolia.pro/blob/main/_config.ts#L336

The two feeds are being produced and the query filtering by lang is working. 

https://blog.esolia.pro/feed.en.json
https://blog.esolia.pro/feed.ja.json

If you look at both of these, 

* the description is Japanese in both although I have separate sections for both languages in my _data.yml:
   * https://github.com/eSolia/blog.esolia.pro/blob/main/src/_data.yml
* although I've specified the lang in the feed "info" and "items" both, it does not show up in the feeds
* the author in "info" doesn't show at all

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

Consistently. I tried various combinations in the settings, but it's like it is not honoring or picking up the values in my _data.yml. 

### What is the expected behavior?

It should pick up and show fixed strings, and also be able to figure out the language sections of the _data.yml and display those correctly. 

### What do you see instead?

As mentioned, missing values.

### Additional information

this feels a bit like some of the other issues I had before, related to field aliases...

Comments.Body:
The variables for the feed info are taken from root global data. The multilanguage plugin only transform the data of the pages, not general data. The english data is stored in the `en` global property, so you can do this:

```js
site.use(feed({
  output: ["/feed.en.xml", "/feed.en.json"],
  query: "type=post lang=en",
  sort: "date=desc",
  info: {
    title: "=en.site.title",
    description: "=en.site.description",
    published: new Date(),
    lang: "en",
    generator: true,
    authorName: "eSolia Inc.",
  },
  // etc...
```

> although I've specified the lang in the feed "info" and "items" both, it does not show up in the feeds

`lang` is not used in the [JSON feed spec](https://www.jsonfeed.org/version/1/). But there's the `<language>` tag in the xml to set the language of the channel. There isn't a language tag per item, I don't know if there's a standard tag for that. I can add it if it's supported.

> the author in "info" doesn't show at all

I see the author in both xml and json formats.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
thanks for checking and explaining, @oscarotero. Ok, well noted on the lang being present only in the xml. I just wrongly assumed about the lang being in the items. 

Ok, after some further tests, what I found was, since the variables for "info" are taken from root global data as you mention, you need to have the language prefix on only the "sub" languages, not the default one. In other words, if it has a lang prefix in your _data.yml, then add it in the feed plugin config.

My second finding was, when I made these feed changes in my `_config.ts`, my `lume serve` wouldn't pick up the changes. Looking at the feeds on localhost:3000 showed no changes after the `lume serve` process said it was "reloading site" finally completed its load process, even if I force-refreshed the browser once it had restarted. 

It worked only after I did `rm -rf _site _cache deno.lock`. I'm not sure which of those had the impact, but once I did that, I could see the changes I made in the feed, from localhost:3000.

This is the config that worked, with only the secondary language of English having the prefix like `=en.site.title`: 

```ts
site.use(feed({
  output: ["/feed.ja.xml", "/feed.ja.json"],
  query: "type=post lang=ja",
  sort: "date=desc",
  info: {
    title: "=site.title",
    description: "=site.description",
    published: new Date(),
    lang: "ja",
    hubs: undefined,
    generator: true,
    authorName: "=site.author",
  },
  items: {
    title: "=title", // title of the item
    description: "=description", // description 
    published: "=date", // publishing date 
    updated: "=last_modified", // last update 
    lang: "ja", // language 
    image: "=image", // image 
    authorName: "=author", // author 
  },
}));
site.use(feed({
  output: ["/feed.en.xml", "/feed.en.json"],
  query: "type=post lang=en",
  sort: "date=desc",
  info: {
    title: "=en.site.title",
    description: "=en.site.description",
    published: new Date(),
    lang: "en",
    hubs: undefined,
    generator: true,
    authorName: "=en.site.author",
  },
  items: {
    title: "=title", // title of the item
    description: "=description", // description 
    published: "=date", // publishing date 
    updated: "=last_modified", // last update 
    lang: "en", // language 
    image: "=image", // image 
    authorName: "=author", // author 
  },
}));
```
Comments.Author: RickCogley

Comments.Body:
When `_config.ts` is changed, Lume needs to be reloaded entirely. This is automatically done by the lume command hence that's the "reloading site" message you see in the terminal.

The hot reload feature of lume server only works inside HTML pages and can reload the html page itself or other resources loaded by the pages (css, js, images, etc). But a xml/json feed file cannot be reload automatically. Once the build is completed, if you reload manually the browser, you should see the changes.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Thanks. I tried reloading the browser after the Lume serve settled but nothing changed. I'm using Edge so it might be that. I didn't try any others this time.
Comments.Author: RickCogley

I_kwDOEYAvFc6NyVCb.Title: lume -s, Fail to copy file to where the path once was a directory
I_kwDOEYAvFc6NyVCb.Author: iacore
I_kwDOEYAvFc6NyVCb.Body:
### Version

about the latest

### Platform

Linux

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

1. `site.copy('public')`
2.
```
deno task serve &
# wait for server to launch
cd public/
mkdir b.md
touch b.md/b.md
rm -r b.md; touch b.md
```


### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

100% methinks

### What is the expected behavior?

There should be no error.

### What do you see instead?
```
ERROR Failed to copy file: /b.md/b.md: Not a directory (os error 20): readfile '/home/user/__site__/public/b.md/b.md'
```
### Additional information

workaround: run `deno task serve` again.

Comments.Body: Do you mean this error should be catched and showed in the terminal but without exit?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> Do you mean this error should be cached and showed in the terminal but without exit?

ohhh sorry i forgot to fill in the field; it should not be an user-facing error.

let me explain why the error happens

1. b.md/b.md is created
2. b.md/b.md is deleted, but b.md/ isn't deleted
3. b.md is created

proper behavior:
when Lume sees empty folder it should overwrite it

this may mess with `site.copy('empty_directory')`; not sure who would want to put an empty directory in the output though.
Comments.Author: iacore

Comments.Body:
This has been fixed and will be released in Lume 2.3.
From now on, if a file is removed and its folder is empty, it's removed too.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5XCdkD.Title: Use property alias for `metas` plugin
I_kwDOEYAvFc5XCdkD.Author: oscarotero
I_kwDOEYAvFc5XCdkD.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

The `metas` plugin has the `defaultPageData` option to configure default values for the `metas` properties. I think this is not convenient due two reasons:

- It cannot be configured per page because it's defined in the `_config.ts` file
- If some defaults values are defined in a `_data` file (for example, `metas.title`), then you need to do `metas.title = null` to use the default value configured.

I propose to change the plugin to implement a syntax to define aliases to existing variables. For example:

```yml
title: Hello world
description: This is my site

metas:
  title: $title
  description: $description
```

Any value starting with `$` sign would be interpreted as an alias to an existing value (in the example above, `title` and `description`. It could allow also values like `$header.title`.

This allows to have different defaults for different types of pages, using `_data` files in subfolders.

**Edit:**

Maybe the sign `~` is better, because it's what's used by the `url` plugin to refer to source files (like `~/about-me.md`):

```yml
title: Hello world
description: This is my site

metas:
  title: ~title
  description: ~description
```

Other alternative syntaxes:

```yml
title: Hello world
description: This is my site

metas:
  title: &title
  description: &description
```
(Cons: The `&` sign is a thing in Yaml).


```yml
title: Hello world
description: This is my site

metas:
  title: {{ title }}
  description: {{ description }}
```
(Const: It looks like nunjucks, people may think it's possible to use filters and other nunjucks features).

Any feedback is welcome!

MDU6SXNzdWU5NDEwNDEwODI=.Title: Improve metrics to be more analytics friendly
MDU6SXNzdWU5NDEwNDEwODI=.Author: shah
MDU6SXNzdWU5NDEwNDEwODI=.Body:
@oscarotero and team, I have these suggestions for improving metrics:

1. Increase granularity
2. Allow "baggage" (in open telemetry terms) to be contributed by pages

**Increasing granularity**. Right now we have the following in `metrics.json` where the "name" has event details that need to parsed in order to be consumable for analytics:

```json
{
    "name": "Load: /support/generate-static-build.sh",
    "entryType": "measure",
    "startTime": 1009.5581999999999,
    "duration": 0.1815000000000282,
    "detail": null
  }
```

Instead, I suggest something like this:

```
{
    "name": "Load: /home", // human-friendly, not useful for analytics
    "nature": { category: "load" }, // analytics-friendly, nature can be like a URL with depth /load/X, /preprocess, /render/Z, etc. and can be merged with metrics contributed by pages being rendered
    "target": { url: "/page/url", ...anything else we know about target... }, // analytics-friendly
    "entryType": "measure", // "measure" is the only entry I see here -- is there anything else?
    "startTime": 1007.8624, // analytics-friendly, 
    "duration": 2187.5929, // suggest removing "duration" and only including analytics-friendly "endTime"
    "detail": null // suggest renaming to "baggage" using OpenTelemetry terminology; and allow this to be contributed/merged from generated pages (through filter.metrics({ nature: {  }, baggage: {... anything ...}})
}
```

**Allow metrics "contribution" by pages**. Many of my pages run database calls or other expensive calls and I'd like to add my own metrics for those expensive items.

```ts
export default async function ( // for Lume
  page: lumeAide.RenderablePage,
  filters: lumeAide.Filters,
) {
  // do something expensive
  filter.metrics({ nature: {  }, baggage: {... anything ...}}); // I can call my own metrics?
}
```

Comments.Body:
These metrics are generated with the standard [`Performance` web interface](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Performance), that allows to [create marks](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Performance/mark) and measure the time between these marks (or one mark to now) with [performance.measure](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Performance/measure). I think it's better to use an already implemented and available interface instead of reiventing the wheel and create new system.

The performance object is already available in Deno as a global object, so you can use it at any point of your javascript/typescript code. `site.metrics` is just a wrapper of the `performance` object to be used internally by lume: See for example: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/metrics.ts#L33

So, if you want to add additional metrics from your typescript pages, just need to do something like this:

```ts
export default async function (data, filters) {
    performance.mark("SQL query"); //Create a timing mark named "SQL query"
    const result = await makeExpensiveQuery();
    performance.measure("SQL query"); //Measure the timing from the mark "SQL query" to now.
}
```

Now, the measure of "SQL query" will be included in the list of your metrics. Note that lume only export the entries of type "measure" (not "mark") because marks are used only as the starting point of a measure. [See this](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/metrics.ts#L57)

Maybe we could include additional info in the `details` property but, due marks cannot have duplicated names, we have to contain all info in the entry name to avoid duplications.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Perfect @oscarotero - I _completely_ agree we should stay with Deno's strategy of being as close to web interface as possible. With this new information you've armed me with about metrics I'll do some studying and implement what I need on top. I'm trying to get the metrics to be useful on the server side through Prometheus and OpenTelemetry taxonomy and I'll let you know if I get stuck anywhere. Thanks!
Comments.Author: shah
shah

Comments.Body: @oscarotero if you'd rather put the extra information in `detail` that's fine too -- my only goal is to have is parseable without doing any string manipulation. Thanks!
Comments.Author: shah

Comments.Body: I don't think the name is a good place to set info. This is the job of `detail` property.
Comments.Author: oscarotero
shah
oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Sure, if this is the best I can get I'll take it :-).

But, it's not as scalable for complex needs because the Lume core is providing the messages and subjects instead of a type-safe instrumentation supplier doing all messages and categorization. With the design I suggested, tossing in OpenTelemetry as the supplier would be trivial. With your (even much improved) design I am still forced to create an adapter in the middle instead of using a proper type-safe interface. In general I agree with you about reducing verbosity but complex sites like mine that have hundreds of thousands of pages with over a dozen developers need different flexibility in different page generators.

I appreciate you taking the input and improving it though, thanks! I'll use what you've created when the next release comes out and give you more feedback.
Comments.Author: shah
shah

Comments.Body:
I think the solution is very similar to your proposal. The only difference is that instead having one method for every thing to measure, we have just one method. I know that you're concerned about typings (so you can accept only some options for every measure, for example require a `Page` for every `renderPage` measurement), but in practice, is almost the same. In fact, there are other metrics that you have not taken into account, like processors and preprocessors. And if you want to measure database queries, for example, you should have to create new methods.

This is why I believe this way is more scalable. It works like events: there isn't an event class for every event type, there's just one event with the property `type`. This allows to have only two methods: `addEventListener` and `dispatchEvent`, instead of `addBeforeBuildEvent`, `dispatchBeforeBuildEvent`, `addAfterBuildEvent`, `dispatchBeforeBuildEvent`, and so on.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
@oscarotero I know it _looks_ similar to my proposal but it's missing the main purpose: flexibility for analytics through multiple implementations. In your version, you're making the decision _a priori_ about what to name a subject (which makes it type-unsafe and defeats typing) because you have _concrete classes_ instead of an _interface_. 

By putting in a minor abstraction (`Instrumentable`) you get unlimited flexibility with your primary goal of allowing everything complex to be done outside of the simplicity-focused core. Lume's initial TypeScript implementation is fantastic but there are many places there are concrete classes where interfaces should be (like Metrics). This may be the first place where you might take the TypeScript-centric approach to creating small interfaces and default _simple_ implementations in Lume core with non-core implementations can do all the complex work on their own. Similar to your plugins concept.

This is mainly an architecture choice difference -- your style is good, solid, JavaScript concrete classes approach ... my suggestion is to use TypeScript-focused style with interfaces and multiple implementations. If it would be helpful to have a quick voice call to discuss this topic I'm happy to jump on a call. The reason I'm spending time on this discussion is that the architecture of Lume is fantastic but not type-safe yet and it would be great to increase type-safety as you progress to 1.0.

Thanks for hearing me out!
Comments.Author: shah
oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Affirmative @oscarotero -- returning a function is not as extensible as returning an interface (like you said, I'm planning on expanding the `observe` object) :-). 

Also, you're right that I would like to create an interface without extending the base class. Requiring _inheritance_ (`class X extends Y`) is generally more brittle than using _composition_ (implementing an interface with property functions). With interface implementations the core never worries about any _base classes_ - only _default implementations_. 

As Lume grows and more developers join the core (like I would love to!) the more base classes you have, the more brittle the system will be because base functionality can break in inherited classes. Adding more interfaces with default implementations in favor of base classes with inheritance is ideal - it would allow us to use Inversion of Control (IoC) in `_config.ts` for all complexity and leave the core super-simple.
Comments.Author: shah

Comments.Body: I think this is already implemented, so I'm closing it.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5ZsntY.Title: Allow to define data with `site.data()` to a folder/file
I_kwDOEYAvFc5ZsntY.Author: oscarotero
I_kwDOEYAvFc5ZsntY.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

`site.data()` is a function to assign a shared data to the site. For example:

```js
site.data("site_name", "My Blog");
```

This assign the variable `site_name` to all pages with the value "My Blog". It is similar to include this variable in a `_data.yml` file in the project root.

The idea is to be able to assign data only to a directory or even a page. For example:

```js
site.data("site_name", "My Blog"); // Assign data to the whole site
site.data("site_name", "My Gallery", "/photos"); // Assign data to the "/photos" folder
site.data("site_name", "My Photo", "/photos/photo1.md"); // Assign data to the "/photos/photo1.md" file
```

I_kwDOEYAvFc6kpZGp.Title: esbuild plugin doesn't build when src is specified
I_kwDOEYAvFc6kpZGp.Author: ooker777

Comments.Body:
Are your scripts inside `docs` folder?
Note also that you don't need to specify `entryPoints`. Lume automatically bundle all `ts` and `js` files found in the `src` folder. You can simply run `site.use(esbuild())` (without options) and it should work.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5qwpwa.Title: RFC: Putting code, tests and docs of plugins next to each other
I_kwDOEYAvFc5qwpwa.Author: not-my-profile
I_kwDOEYAvFc5qwpwa.Body:
The files making up a plugin are currently scattered across three different locations:

* `plugins/` in the lume repository contains the actual code, e.g. `plugins/katex.ts`
* `tests/` in the lume repository contains the test code, e.g. `tests/katex.test.ts`
* [`plugins/` in the lume.land repository](https://github.com/lumeland/lume.land/tree/6b1fddaf01c19d3c9d58c20d38d8a4dd526054b3/plugins) contains the documentation, e.g. `plugins/katex.md`

I think it would make sense to instead put all these files next to each other in this repository, e.g. we could have:

* `plugins/katex/katex.ts`
* `plugins/katex/katex.test.ts`
* `plugins/katex/README.md`

The reasoning being that if you update the code, you likely also should update the tests and the documentation as well and that's easier to do when the files are all next to each other. Putting them next to each other also makes it easier to spot discrepancies. (Especially having the .md files in another repository makes contributing to plugins more difficult since you'd often need two PRs.)

Comments.Body:
Yeah, it makes sense but I can see some drawbacks:

- From users POV, it requires to repeat the plugin name in the imported url. It's clearer to import it from `lume/plugins/katext.ts` thank `lume/plugins/katext/katext.ts`.
- It's a breaking change: all sites built with Lume would break
- Tests are not only TypeScript files (like `katex.test.ts`) but they need assets (stored in `/tests/assets`) and they generate snapshots (stored in `/tests/__snapshots__`).
- Some plugins need other plugins to work. For example, Tailwind requires Postcss, or SourceMap is used by other plugins like Postcss, Esbuild, SASS, etc.
- We must to figure out a way to import the plugins documentation in the lume.land site if they are in different repositories.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Thanks for the proposal, but I think it's better as it is
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc650GDI.Title: gzip tests fail on deno 2.3.3
I_kwDOEYAvFc650GDI.Author: Garciat
I_kwDOEYAvFc650GDI.Body:
Commit: 8856dabc49951566e76926800b335f9d4bddd737 (`main` at the time of posting) Deno: 2.3.3 (latest on Homebrew) System: `macOS 15.5 24F74 arm64` Command: `deno task test tests/gzip.test.ts` Some findings: - The tests output indicates that there is a slight difference in the compressed payload size between the snapshot and my local run. - e.g. `Uint8Array(82)` (snapshot) vs `Uint8Array(78)` (mine) - Recent CI test runs for macos [are passing](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/actions/runs/15414818356/job/43375038370). - Although the OS version is 14.7.6. - It seems like `macos-latest` [refers](https://github.com/actions/runner-images/blob/d8c2fb464ab0965a7cc86255d8903f539e80f135/README.md?plain=1#L28) to `macos-14` and not `macos-15`. - ❇ **AI**: it would be good to include `macos-15` in the test matrix, as [14.x only has <25% market share as of 2025](https://telemetrydeck.com/survey/apple/macOS/versions/). - Deno's compression seems to be implemented [here](https://github.com/denoland/deno/tree/v2.3.3/ext/web). It [depends on](https://github.com/denoland/deno/blob/v2.3.3/ext/web/Cargo.toml#L24) the `flate2` library built with `default` features, which uses a Rust backend (`miniz_oxide`). - If that's true, the system's `zlib` should not be a factor here. (Something I considered based on https://github.com/mozilla/pdf.js/issues/17399) - And if that's true, the above macOS version discrepancy should also not be a factor. - The remaining discrepancy would be my Deno version. However, the version of `flate2` (1.0.30) hasn't changed between [2.3.3](https://github.com/denoland/deno/blame/v2.3.3/Cargo.toml#L157) and [2.3.5](https://github.com/denoland/deno/blame/v2.3.5/Cargo.toml#L160). - Still, I manually downloaded 2.3.5 and ran the tests with it. They passed. Conclusions: - There is nothing to do here. If other macOS users run into this issue, they should upgrade their Deno version. Unfortunately, Homebrew seems to be stuck on 2.3.3 per https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/pull/225214. (PS: I investigated this for fun 😄)
Tests output ``` running 2 tests from ./tests/gzip.test.ts gzip plugin ... FAILED (40ms) gzip plugin with options ... ignored (0ms) ERRORS gzip plugin => ./tests/gzip.test.ts:6:6 error: AssertionError: Snapshot does not match: [Diff] Actual / Expected [ { content: "

This page is exported to /404.html, not /404/index.html

", data: { basename: "404", children: "

This page is exported to /404.html, not /404/index.html

", content: "This page is exported to `/404.html`, not `/404/index.html` ", date: [], imagick: "Array(1)", mergedKeys: [ "tags", "metas", "imagick", ], metas: [ "title", "description", ], page: [ "src", "data", "isCopy", ], paginate: "paginate", search: [], site: "Default site name", tags: "Array(0)", url: "/404.html", }, src: { ext: ".md", path: "/404", remote: undefined, }, }, { - content: "Uint8Array(97)", + content: "Uint8Array(93)", data: { basename: "404", - content: "Uint8Array(97)", + content: "Uint8Array(93)", page: [ "src", "data", "isCopy", ], url: "/404.html.gz", }, src: { ext: "", path: "", remote: undefined, }, }, { content: "Content of Page 2", data: { basename: "overrided-page2", children: "Content of Page 2", colors: "Array(3)", content: "Content of Page 2", date: I_kwDOEYAvFc5t19Z3.Title: Support for Mermaid
I_kwDOEYAvFc5t19Z3.Author: alaminkouser
I_kwDOEYAvFc5t19Z3.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

# [Mermaid](https://mermaid.js.org/)

- A Graph Generator

Comments.Body:
This came up on Discord server some time ago (https://discord.com/channels/794537085641818124/1111980136691146773) but looks like it's not possible to run Mermaid on server side due https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/issues/3650.

There are a couple of alternative options that you can use:

- Serea plugin: https://github.com/idris-maps/serea
- OI Lume Viz plugin: https://open-innovations.github.io/oi-lume-viz/
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I found this article: https://css-tricks.com/making-mermaid-diagrams-in-markdown/

Mermaid has cdn. And It can  be used in browser. I tested in this page:
https://alaminkouser.github.io/mermaid/
Source: https://github.com/alaminkouser/alaminkouser.github.io/tree/b2ed2c811bfea580b20f89d2625306a00d0db27c

Although I can not find a way to generate SVG and link/embed the svg, but this currently works.
Comments.Author: alaminkouser

Comments.Body:
Yes, the only way to run mermaid is in the browser.
But it would be great to have a way to run it in build time, so there's no need to import any script in the html because the code is already generated. That's the problem with mermaid. I hope they implement server side support soon.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Any thoughts regarding this:
https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid-cli
Comments.Author: alaminkouser

Comments.Body:
Seems that it [uses puppeteer](https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid-cli/blob/master/src/index.js#L404) for rendering.
I'm not sure about performance (specially if there are many diagrams and charts) but I guess we can give a try.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: deno has puppeteer. So we may avoid npm and use puppeteer in deno. I will test it.
Comments.Author: alaminkouser

Comments.Body:
Here is a quick code for running puppeteer in deno and getting all svg and save the svgs in a dir:
https://github.com/alaminkouser/deno-mermaid/tree/61d526f78d71bd9ab3b1cf12f40a9b39c90e1bbc
Comments.Author: alaminkouser

Comments.Body:
Okay, thanks!
I'm getting this error:

```
➜  deno-mermaid git:(main) deno run -A main.ts
error: Uncaught PermissionDenied: Permission denied (os error 13)
    this.proc = Deno.run({
                     ^
    at opRun (ext:runtime/40_process.js:46:14)
    at Object.run (ext:runtime/40_process.js:132:15)
    at BrowserRunner.start (https://deno.land/x/puppeteer@16.2.0/src/deno/BrowserRunner.ts:64:22)
    at ChromeLauncher.launch (https://deno.land/x/puppeteer@16.2.0/src/deno/Launcher.ts:108:12)
    at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:183:11)
    at async file:///Users/oscarotero/www/deno-mermaid/main.ts:3:17
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> Okay, thanks! I'm getting this error:
> 
> ```
> ➜  deno-mermaid git:(main) deno run -A main.ts
> error: Uncaught PermissionDenied: Permission denied (os error 13)
>     this.proc = Deno.run({
>                      ^
>     at opRun (ext:runtime/40_process.js:46:14)
>     at Object.run (ext:runtime/40_process.js:132:15)

I am not sure, but you may need to run this command:
```PUPPETEER_PRODUCT=chrome deno run -A --unstable https://deno.land/x/puppeteer@16.2.0/install.ts```
Comments.Author: alaminkouser

Comments.Body:
@alaminkouser ok, the problem was the execution permissions for the downloaded binary.

I've been experimenting with puppeteer (Deno already supports the Node version which is more updated).
You can take a look to this experimental plugin:
https://github.com/lumeland/experimental-plugins/tree/main/puppeteer

There are some issues yet but it's a good start.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Interesting. I will also try this.
Comments.Author: alaminkouser

Comments.Body:
I was able to render mermaid on server side with the Astral experimental plugin:
https://github.com/lumeland/experimental-plugins/tree/main/astral

It runs the javascript code on server side and save the generated HTML code.

For example, this page https://github.com/lumeland/experimental-plugins/blob/main/astral/demo/page.vto#L24-L28
- Run the javascript code
- Remove the script element (all elements with `astral-delete` attribute are removed)
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Amazing! Next week I will try this. It can be powerful and faster. Now generated websites can be without/less JS codes!!!
Comments.Author: alaminkouser

Comments.Body:
- I cloned the [experimental repo](https://github.com/lumeland/experimental-plugins/tree/main)
- went to `astral/demo` directory
- ran `deno task build`
- It generated the website.
- It is the output:
![Screenshot from 2024-06-28 22-56-08](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/assets/36203422/8b8b8e9b-b9d0-41ba-9af2-8f959cb4a8b2)

- It is working.
- I found this output in command line while I ran `deno task build`:
![Screenshot from 2024-06-28 22-50-24](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/assets/36203422/895a3dbe-c172-4ac5-a86f-72dccb50aa0f)

It is downloading chrome. It may not use puppeteer, but uses chrome in server side.

> I tested it with a newly deno install in my computer.
Comments.Author: alaminkouser

Comments.Body:
Yes, it doesn't use puppeteer, but astral https://github.com/lino-levan/astral which is like Puppeteer but for Deno (Puppeteer doesn't work in Deno).

For now, the plugin is just a POC and there are some limitations (for example, it only can load assets from external urls). But for this specific use case it works pretty well.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Only downside being it uses chrome. So cicd will be slow.

But seems stable.
Comments.Author: alaminkouser

Comments.Body:
I didn't play much with the library, but [seems that Firefox is also supported](https://github.com/lino-levan/astral/blob/302a2aa858b3bc5dfc54758cfdc27f10acfd658b/src/browser.ts#L69).

If no browser is specified, it downloads chrome automatically.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Discussion for anyone interested: [What makes mermaid.js cannot work on server-side, while other visualization libs can?](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/discussions/641 "What makes mermaid.js cannot work on server-side, while other visualization libs can? · lumeland/lume · Discussion #641")
Comments.Author: ooker777

Comments.Body: FYI I made a plugin to inject Mermaid script at the front end: https://github.com/ooker777/lume-mermaid-plugin
Comments.Author: ooker777

Comments.Body:
To add to the list of options, I've created a [Kroki plugin for Lume](https://code.hangdaan.com/foster/lume-plugin-kroki) which converts codeblocks containing diagram descriptions into an `<img/>` during the site build. It currently has support for all of the [Kroki Diagram Types](https://kroki.io/#support); Mermaid being one of them.

The images are rendered by a [Kroki](https://kroki.io/) server. By default, the plugin uses the official instance at https://kroki.io, but can be configured to use a self-hosted instance.
Comments.Author: foster-hangdaan

I_kwDOEYAvFc5XIgl6.Title: Headless WordPress plugin
I_kwDOEYAvFc5XIgl6.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Done here https://github.com/lumeland/experimental-plugins/tree/main/wordpress
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Correct me if I'm wrong, but the plugin doesn't seem to support CPTs, Custom Taxonomies (CT) and Custom (role) Users right?

In my current WP project, I created a CPT with 2 CT. Not to mention I also have plenty Custom Fields. If you plan to release the plugin now, the user scope will be limited. It not that bad if it can support wordpress.com smoothly.
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
The plugin is very limited as is now.
I think maybe is not a good idea because for big sites it's very slow because it depends on the WP API, HTTP connection, etc.

I think a better aproach would be to simply create a script to download the content once, save it as markdown files and then you can build the site using the downloaded content. If you need to upgrade the content, just download it again.

Probably [an archetype](https://lume.land/docs/core/archetypes/) would fit better for this use case.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Well, indeed that it is the most flexible way to do so. But I wonder if the `type: "post",` could be set manually with fallback value is "post". If possible, it would make the plugin be much more flexible. 

I'm not worry much about big sites, however, with one more field can be add manually, ten more unaware problems may arrive =))

But still, I'm kinda curious of how far I can push this one :?
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body: It's you feel strong and want to work on improving this plugin, I'll appreciate it :)
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: About the demo config file, it didn't work right? Because my TypeScript complaint wrong type.
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
It should work fine now.
The blog was down and the demo was using a local copy of Lume
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5y2CA3.Title: Prism breaks JavaScript highligting using code_highligt
I_kwDOEYAvFc5y2CA3.Author: Hexagon
I_kwDOEYAvFc5y2CA3.Body:
### Version

v1.19.1

### Platform

deno 1.37.1 (release, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu)

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

This could very well be a wontfix, i got here by accident... But it could be a symptom of something else going wrong:

Setup a fresh lume project, include `code_highlight` and `prism`:

```bash
@Hexagon ➜ /workspaces/lumebug (master) $ deno run -Ar https://deno.land/x/lume/init.ts
Warning Implicitly using latest version (v1.19.1) for https://deno.land/x/lume/init.ts
 ? Choose the configuration file format › _config.ts (TypeScript)
 ? Do you want to install some plugins now? › Yes
 ? Select the plugins to install › code_highlight, prism
```

**Change `_config.yaml` so that prism is used before code_highlight**

```typescript
// ...
site.use(prism());
site.use(code_highlight());
// ...
```

**Create `index.md`, include a javascript code block**

```markdown
# Hello Bug!

\`\`\`javascript
const a = 1;
\`\`\`
```

*^ Had to escape the backticks, unescape them*

**Build site**

`deno task lume`

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

Every time

### What is the expected behavior?

Success!

### What do you see instead?

Pages and pages of:

```
          mode: ""
        },

      },

        },
        <ref *20> Element [EventTarget] {
          nodeName: "HTML",
          nodeType: 1,
          childNodes: [NodeList],
          parentNode: [HTMLDocument [EventTarget]],
          parentElement: null,

        <ref *15> Element [EventTarget] {

Comments.Body: Hmm, why are you using `prism` and `code_highlight`? Both plugins are mutually exclusive because they do the same things but with different libraries.

Maybe a warning if they both are activated at the same time, if someone else is careless enough to activate them both? :)

Ah, ok, got it.
Good idea, I'll think something.

I_kwDOEYAvFc6QZgmU.Title: Search misses pages that have a tag with spaces
I_kwDOEYAvFc6QZgmU.Author: basus
I_kwDOEYAvFc6QZgmU.Body:
### Version 2.2.3 ### Platform macOS Sonoma 14.5 with Deno 1.45.1 ### What steps will reproduce the bug? I'm trying to use the following snippet to get all the tags that are currently known and then count the number of pages associated with each tag: ```html
    {{ for tag of search.values("tags").sort() }}
  • {{ tag }} {{ search.pages(tag).length }}
    • {{ /for }}
``` ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? _No response_ ### What is the expected behavior? _No response_ ### What do you see instead? For most tags, I get the right count. But for tags with spaces like `"hello world"`, the tag shows up in the list given by the first `search`, but the second `search` always has a length of 0. ### Additional information I'm pretty new to using Lume, so maybe there are more idiomatic ways or working with tags that I'm not aware of?

Comments.Body:
You have to put the tags between quotes:

```js
search.pages("tag-without-spaces 'tag with spaces'")
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Ahh ok, I managed to get it working with the following: ```
    {{ for tag of search.values("tags").sort() }} {{> tag = tag.includes(' ') ? "'".concat(tag, "'") : tag }}
  • {{ tag }} {{ search.pages(tag).length }}
    • {{ /for }}
```
Comments.Author: basus

Comments.Body:
Or even simpler: ```html
    {{ for tag of search.values("tags").sort() }}
  • {{ tag }} {{ search.pages(`"${tag}"`).length }}
    • {{ /for }}
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Yes, that worked perfectly. Thank you. Still getting used to the templating system.
Comments.Author: basus

I_kwDOEYAvFc5ZRVfJ.Title: TypeError: Deno.spawn is not a function after upgrading Deno to 1.29 (was working in 1.28.3)

Windows and GitHub Page Deploy

1. Setup lume
2. add pagefind plugin
3. build OR run (deno task serve)

Everytime

Run and Build and Deploy

```
TypeError: Deno.spawn is not a function
    at (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.14.1/plugins/pagefind.ts:182:51)
    at async Events.dispatchEvent (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.14.1/core/events.ts:49:11)
    at async Site.build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.14.1/core/site.ts:521:5)
    at async build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.14.1/cli/build.ts:36:3)

```

Thanks for reporting this
It's fixed in v1.14.2
