I_kwDOEYAvFc6mNEXP.Title: Watcher ignore configuration is confusing
I_kwDOEYAvFc6mNEXP.Author: sagikazarmark
I_kwDOEYAvFc6mNEXP.Body:
### Version

2.5.0

### Platform

darwin

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

1. Create a directory you want to ignore (eg. `.devenv`)
2. Follow the [documentation](https://lume.land/docs/configuration/config-file/#watcher-options) to ignore it from the file watcher
3. The directory doesn't get ignored (in my case it leads to some issues, see below)

For reference, this is the initial configuration I tried:

```ts
import lume from "lume/mod.ts";

const site = lume({
    watcher: {
        ignore: [
            ".devenv",
            ".direnv",

            "./.devenv",
            "./.direnv",
        ],
    },
});

site.ignore(".devenv", ".direnv");

export default site;
```

To make things even more confusing, the watcher ignore config and the site ignore config does not seem to be in sync (which was my initial assumption).

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

_No response_

### What is the expected behavior?

The file watcher should ignore said directories.

### What do you see instead?

The file watcher entering the directories I want to exclude have two consequences in my case:

These directories contain a lot of files which means starting Lume takes a lot of time.

It also failed to start due to a broken link in those directories:

```
error: Uncaught (in worker "") (in promise) NotFound: No such file or directory (os error 2): stat '/Users/mark/Projects/sagikazarmark/sagikazarmark.com/.direnv/flake-inputs/8p8giar71b6aqddqkjckzhirwvjnf02x-source/tests/functional/lang/symlink-resolution/broken'
    const info = Deno.statSync(src);
                      ^
    at Object.statSync (ext:deno_fs/30_fs.js:415:3)
    at FS.#walkLink (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:167:23)
    at FS.#walkFs (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:141:23)
    at FS.#walkFs (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:160:21)
    at FS.#walkFs (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:160:21)
    at FS.#walkFs (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:160:21)
    at FS.#walkFs (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:160:21)
    at FS.#walkLink (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:181:19)
    at FS.#walkFs (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:141:23)
    at FS.#walkFs (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.0/core/fs.ts:160:21)
error: Uncaught (in promise) Error: Unhandled error in child worker.
    at Worker.#pollControl (ext:runtime/11_workers.js:204:19)
    at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:175:7)
```

### Additional information

After some debugging I managed to get the configuration work:

```ts
import lume from "lume/mod.ts";

const site = lume({
    // src: "src",
    watcher: {
        ignore: [
            "/.devenv",
            "/.direnv",
        ],
    },
});

site.ignore(".devenv", ".direnv");

export default site;
```

Adding a leading slash to the path seems to have done the trick. There is no mention of that in the configuration though.

---

I use Nix with direnv and devenv to manage Deno, hence those two directories.

I also figured out I could just move the source directory somewhere else.

Comments.Body:
The `site.ignore()` ignores the src files to create pages, but these files are scanned because they can be used by any page. For example the `_includes` page is ignored (because it starts with `_` but it's the same if it were ignored with `site.ignore()`), but pages can include templates or use layouts from this folder.

The watcher ignore only instruct to don't trigger a new rebuild if some files have been changed. For default, the `_site` folder is ignored by the watcher (to avoid infinite loop) but the `_includes` folder isn't (even ignored by `site`, it's not ignored by the watcher).

What you want is a way to ignore completely the folder for both watcher and site. This isn't implemented for now (it's a good idea, I will do).

And regarding the leading slash, that's fair. It must be added automatically by Lume if it's missing. I'll fix it.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5PdMYe.Title: Remove Deno arguments passed by Lume
I_kwDOEYAvFc5PdMYe.Author: oscarotero
I_kwDOEYAvFc5PdMYe.Body:
Lume allows to pass arguments to Deno using the `--` separator. For example to use the `--compact` flag for Node compatibilty:

```
lume -s -- --compact
```

This feature was introduced before Deno tasks and Deno config file. Now this can be done editing the deno.json config file. So this feature (that is not documented) must be removed.

I_kwDOEYAvFc6iZSoR.Title: Is the server port options taken in account from the configuration file?
I_kwDOEYAvFc6iZSoR.Author: e-roux
I_kwDOEYAvFc6iZSoR.Body:
### Version

2.4.2

### Platform

Darwin

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

After bootstrapping a site with `deno run -A https://lume.land/init.ts` with the configuration:

```ts
import lume from "lume/mod.ts";

const site = lume({
  server: {
    port: 8000,
  },
});

export default site;
```

the site is accessible on port 3000 but not 8000

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

always

### What is the expected behavior?

```shell
Task serve deno task lume -s
Task lume echo "import 'lume/cli.ts'" | deno run -A - "-s"
Loading config file file:///private/tmp/lumi/_config.ts
🍾 Site built into ./_site
  0 files generated in 0.00 seconds
  Server started at:
  http://localhost:8000/ (local)
  http://192.168.178.30:8000/ (network)
```

### What do you see instead?

```shell
Task serve deno task lume -s
Task lume echo "import 'lume/cli.ts'" | deno run -A - "-s"
Loading config file file:///private/tmp/lumi/_config.ts
🍾 Site built into ./_site
  0 files generated in 0.00 seconds
  Server started at:
  http://localhost:3000/ (local)
  http://192.168.178.30:3000/ (network)
```

### Additional information

The port is taken in account when passed to the command line

Comments.Body:
Sorry, this is a bug. Seems that the default url for server mode (`localhost:3000`) overrides the port. I'll fix it in the next version.
Meanwhile, you can use `--port` flag (`serve deno task lume -s --port=8000`)
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: thanks for the quick reponse. Works with the flag passed to `deno task serve`
Comments.Author: e-roux

I_kwDOEYAvFc5XLJ_e.Title: Can't enable code splitting in esbuild
I_kwDOEYAvFc5XLJ_e.Author: DrSensor
I_kwDOEYAvFc5XLJ_e.Body:
### Version

1.31.1

### Platform

Linux

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Here is my `_config.ts`
```ts
import esbuild from "lume/plugins/esbuild.ts";
import minify_html from "lume/plugins/minify_html.ts";

import lume from "lume/mod.ts";

export default lume()
  .ignore("lib")
  .use(esbuild({
    options: {
      splitting: true,
      mangleProps: /^_/,
    },
  }))
  .use(minify_html());
```

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

When `splitting` option enabled in esbuild

### What is the expected behavior?

For example, I have this project structure:
```
lib
└── utils.ts

elements
├── render-scope.ts
└── page-link.ts

index.html
```
where both `render-scope.ts` and `page-link.ts` depend on `lib/utils.ts`.

If I run it with esbuild CLI, the result would look like this:
```console
$ esbuild elements/*.ts --bundle --splitting --outdir=_site/elements --format=esm


  _site/elements/chunk-YLOKRXMB.js  5.0kb
  _site/elements/page-link.js       2.0kb
  _site/elements/render-scope.js    1.6kb

⚡ Done in 9ms
```

### What do you see instead?

```console
$ deno task build

Task build deno task lume
Task lume echo "import 'lume/task.ts'" | deno run --unstable --allow-read --allow-run --lock=lock.json -
Loading config file _config.ts


✘ [ERROR] Must use "outdir" when code splitting is enabled

✘ [ERROR] Must use "outdir" when code splitting is enabled

✘ [ERROR] Must use "outdir" when code splitting is enabled

✘ [ERROR] Must use "outdir" when code splitting is enabled


Error: Error processing page
- page: /demo/counter/module.js
- processor:
    at (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.13.1/core/processors.ts:44:19)
    at async concurrent (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.13.1/core/utils.ts:175:3)
    at async Processors.run (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.13.1/core/processors.ts:34:7)
    at async Site.#buildPages (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.13.1/core/site.ts:610:5)
    at async Site.build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.13.1/core/site.ts:494:9)
    at async build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.13.1/cli/build.ts:36:3)
    at async Command.execute (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.4/command/command.ts:1790:7)
    at async (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.13.1/cli.ts:147:3)

Caused by Error: Build failed with 1 error:
error: Must use "outdir" when code splitting is enabled
    at failureErrorWithLog (https://deno.land/x/esbuild@v0.15.14/mod.js:1546:15)
    at (https://deno.land/x/esbuild@v0.15.14/mod.js:1004:28)
    at runOnEndCallbacks (https://deno.land/x/esbuild@v0.15.14/mod.js:1418:61)
    at buildResponseToResult (https://deno.land/x/esbuild@v0.15.14/mod.js:1002:7)
    at (https://deno.land/x/esbuild@v0.15.14/mod.js:1114:14)
    at responseCallbacks.<computed> (https://deno.land/x/esbuild@v0.15.14/mod.js:651:9)
    at handleIncomingPacket (https://deno.land/x/esbuild@v0.15.14/mod.js:706:9)
    at readFromStdout (https://deno.land/x/esbuild@v0.15.14/mod.js:627:7)
    at (https://deno.land/x/esbuild@v0.15.14/mod.js:1828:11)
```
I have also try specifying `outdir` but it conflict with `outfile`.
I guess the plugin dynamically populate the `outfile` option 🤔

### Additional information

_No response_

Comments.Body:
Ok, it makes sense.
esbuild plugin process one file each time. `splitting` mode requires to process all files at the same time, in order to discover common dependencies. I'll take a look.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I have added support for this. Can you test it, please?
Update Lume to the latest development version with `deno task lume upgrade --dev`.
Thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero
DrSensor
oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I just notice the new release and when `splitting:true` it keep the file extensions instead of renaming it to `.js`
```js

    esbuild({
      extensions: [".ts"],
      options: {

Ups! Sorry, I'll fix it.

```js
site.process([".ts"], (page) => page.data.url.replace(/\.ts$/, ".js");
```

Comments.Body: lastmod is not a reliable value. Sometimes in some CI environments it's the present time (the moment where the file is created after cloning the repo, instead of when this file content was modified for the last time). Another option is using Git Last Updated value, but this also doesn't work for CI like GitHub Actions, because the repository is cloned without the history (only the last commit).

_config.js:
```
import lume from "https://deno.land/x/lume@v0.14.0/mod.js";

I_kwDOEYAvFc5NfCFu.Title: Support `deno.jsonc` for Deno config file
I_kwDOEYAvFc5NfCFu.Author: binyamin
I_kwDOEYAvFc5NfCFu.Body:
### Summary
Currently, Lume only supports `deno.json` files. Deno itself supports `deno.jsonc` files as well. Ideally, Lume would match Deno's behavior.

### Brainstorming
- Deno's standard library includes a JSONC parser in [`std/encoding`](https://deno.land/std@0.147.0/encoding/jsonc.ts). It does _not_ include a method for _serializing_ JSONC (see https://github.com/denoland/deno_std/discussions/1803).
- We only write to the deno config after the _vendor_ and _init_ commands. That's not too often. It might be worth parsing the JSONC using `std/encoding`, and doing some RegExp magic to overwrite a portion of the file. Just brainstorming here.

### Notes
- This feature is not in any way critical. I simply wanted to track JSONC support, and to explain why Lume does not in fact support JSONC.

Comments.Body:
As you said, Lume needs to read and write `deno.json` files to configure tasks and the import map file. In addition to `vendor` and `init`, this file is updated after `upgrade` and `import-map` commands.

Personally, I think it was a bad idea from Deno developers to allow to use jsonc here, it's not standard, introduce a unneeded complexity and inconsistency (what happens if `deno.json` and `deno.jsonc` exists in the same folder?, why it can be used for deno config but not for import_map.jsonc?), it's hard to parse and, specially to write while keeping the existing comments.

When Deno standard library include a option to parse and stringify JSONC, I'll consider support it, althought I think everybody should use json format.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> why it can be used for deno config but not for import_map.jsonc?

Because Deno follows the spec, which only uses JSON: https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/12993
Comments.Author: kidonng

Comments.Body:
Upgrading Lume still yields this error:
```
deno task lume upgrade
Task lume echo "import 'lume/cli.ts'" | deno run -A --v8-flags=--max-old-space-size=8192 - "upgrade"
Deno configuration file not found: deno.json
```
Btw, there is a std lib for jsonc now: [@std/jsonc - JSR](https://jsr.io/@std/jsonc "@std/jsonc - JSR")
Comments.Author: ooker777

Comments.Body: @ooker777 You're right. In recent Lume versions the upgrade script has changed and jsonc files are not loaded. I'll fix it.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

MDU6SXNzdWU5Mjg4MDEyMzE=.Title: Understanding most errors is hard because of printError()
MDU6SXNzdWU5Mjg4MDEyMzE=.Author: fserb
MDU6SXNzdWU5Mjg4MDEyMzE=.Body:
I think Lume is used by people who are somewhat familiar with Deno (at least you need to be familiar enough to create a config file and most documentation assumes one can code a bit of Javascript).

That said, I think hiding the exception stack by default is a bad idea.

When I have a problem in my config, most of the time there's no good info on `printDataError`, and I end up getting something like:

```
Error: Cannot read property 'toLowerCase' of undefined

Task completed in 0m1.283s
```

This is unexpected and not really useful since most of the time the error message (even if it had the accompanying src page file that caused it) will still not uniquely identify where the error is coming from. 

I think it's a fair effort to try to associate a particular error to a source page file (that `printDataError` does) and I think Lume should definitely keep doing this and I'll try to track why I'm not getting it on those cases: I'm not sure if it's data files or preprocessors, or a Markdown/Nunjuck plugin. This reinforces my point! I got an error and I have no clue where the error is even coming from. Did I mess up my config? Is the plugin I'm using broken? Did I forgot a field somewhere?

I know I'm able to set `{verbose: 2}`, but my point is a bit deeper than that. Deno errors are not made to be presented without the stack. They make almost as much sense as just writing "Error" and nothing else.

I think we should remove the `debug` option on `printError()` and always output the full exception. It's usually not that verbose (2 to 10 lines); even non "core" developers can understand what it is (and most people using Lume will be familiar with them); it makes the error messages meaningful; and it makes it easier for users to create issues when it comes to that.

The way it currently is, we are almost saying _"there's an error, I'll sometimes tell you what is the offending file, but in any case change something until it works"_.

Comments.Body:
Ok, get it.
Maybe for errors it doesn't make sense.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc54YJC4.Title: Picture/Imagick fails to display image starting with a date in the filename
I_kwDOEYAvFc54YJC4.Author: enjikaka
I_kwDOEYAvFc54YJC4.Body:
### Version

1.19.4

### Platform

macOS

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

- Set up imagick & picture
- Try to use and image that starts with a date and continues with a name, for example `2021-01-30-herpderp.jpg`

https://github.com/enjikaka/lume-img-error

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

100 % reproducible

### What is the expected behavior?

The image should load

### What do you see instead?

The image does not load

![localhost_3000_](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/assets/877455/206b0e85-39b8-4913-9a7f-e8ec38071893)


### Additional information

- If I change the format of the file name to `herpderp-2021-01-30.jpg` the image loads. 
- It only seems to happen when the file starts with a date? Tried `2-jordgubbe.jpg` and that works, so a number as start doesn't seem to be the trigger
- Images are referenced in HTML in `_site/` but the image files are not created

Comments.Body:
If the filename of a page starts with a date, Lume detects it and use it to the `date` page variable. More info: https://lume.land/docs/creating-pages/page-files/#page-date

So I guess what happens is Lume detects the date in `2021-01-30-herpderp.jpg` and save the file as `herpderp.jpg`. This is not a bug, but the expected behavior.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Aha. In my example repo linked there is a ”jordgubbar.jpg” so I guess the file is generated then. But the picture element doesnt reference that generated name, only the source file with date.

I_kwDOEYAvFc5lIfLT.Title: lume task --serve does not use server.js as doc says
I_kwDOEYAvFc5lIfLT.Author: max-l
I_kwDOEYAvFc5lIfLT.Body:
### Version

1.16.0

### Platform

ubuntu

### What steps will reproduce the bug?


The project's ./server.js  is never  run.

To reproduce, write junk code in this file, 

then run "lume --serve" as specified here:   https://lume.land/docs/core/server/

And the server will start normaly



### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

every time

### What is the expected behavior?


the customize server in ./server.js should run instead of the default one

### What do you see instead?

the default server

### Additional information

_No response_

Comments.Body:
Ok, I just noticed that the server must be assigned to lume config : 

```
import {server} from "./server.js"
const site = lume({server})
```

So it's not a bug, 
but a note in the doc would help: https://lume.land/docs/core/server/
Comments.Author: max-l

Comments.Body:
Fair enough, the documentation is not clear on this.

The `./server.js` file is not used by lume at all, this is just an example of how to create your own server to, for example, Deno deploy. And this file must be executed directly by deno `deno run -A ./server.js`. 

`lume --serve` uses this class internally but with some default middlewares (`reload`, `not_found`, `no_cache` and `logger`) in order to create a proper development experience. If you want to include additional middlewares or configure the server somehow, use the [server options in the _config file](https://lume.land/docs/configuration/config-file/#server-options).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc56WGOr.Author: MichaelHirn
I_kwDOEYAvFc56WGOr.Body:
### Version

2.0.1

### Platform

mac

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

1. Target Page: create a page with custom `url` data attribute.
2. Search Page: create another page that lists all pages via `search.pages()` (make sure `rankingOrder` is greater than the Target Page)

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

_No response_

### What is the expected behavior?

Search Page returns the Target Page

### What do you see instead?

Search Page does not find or return Target Page. No error or warning is raised either.

Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Hey @oscarotero - that explains it then. I had a `posts/2023-12-01-daily.md` page with date field like this

```yaml
---
url: ./custom-post-url
---
```

I have since moved away from the `-daily` slugs in the file name to `posts/2023-12-01-custom-post-url.md` and got rid of the `url` and it works. Judging by your answer I could probably keep it in the initial format and simple append `.html` to the `url` attribute value and it would also work?
Comments.Author: MichaelHirn

Comments.Body:
You can append `.html` or `/`, depending on whether you prefer pretty urls or not. For example:

```yml
---
url: ./custom-post-url/
---
```

But in Lume 2, you have the variable `basename` that is used to change the name of the file without worrying about extensions or pretty url configurations, so it's easier to do simply:

```yml
---
basename: custom-post-url
---
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5w8iWh.Title: Simplify Twitter Card Tags and Remove Unused Keywords Meta Tag
I_kwDOEYAvFc5w8iWh.Author: ghost
I_kwDOEYAvFc5w8iWh.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

## Issue Description

The current implementation of the `metas.ts` plugin in Lume includes unnecessary Twitter Card tags (`twitter:*`) even when OpenGraph tags (`og:*`) are present. Twitter provides fallback behavior for OpenGraph tags, which means that we don't need to duplicate our efforts by including both sets of tags.

Additionally, the keywords meta tag is no longer utilized by search engines for SEO purposes. Therefore, it can be safely removed to reduce output sizes and maintain consistent development practices.

## Proposed Changes

- Remove the `twitter:title` tag if `og:title` is present, optimizing the Twitter Card tags.
- Simplify Twitter Card tags to include only `twitter:site` and `twitter:card` when `og:*` tags are present, eliminating redundant tags.
- Consider removing the keywords meta tag as it is no longer effective for SEO, resulting in a cleaner codebase and reduced output size.

## Additional Information

- [Twitter Card Markup Documentation](https://developer.twitter.com/en/docs/twitter-for-websites/cards/overview/markup) provides details on Twitter's card markup requirements.
- [Getting Started with Twitter Cards](https://developer.twitter.com/en/docs/twitter-for-websites/cards/guides/getting-started) offers guidance on implementing Twitter Cards effectively.
- [Social Media Meta Tags](https://head-2ml.pages.dev/#social-media) provides insights into best practices for social media meta tags.
- [Google's Statement on Keywords Meta Tag](https://developers.google.com/search/blog/2009/09/google-does-not-use-keywords-meta-tag) clarifies that Google does not use the keywords meta tag for ranking websites.
- [Twitter Confirmation on Keywords Meta Tag](https://twitter.com/ChristiJOlson/status/1266415751172247552) highlights that Twitter also does not use the keywords meta tag.

These changes will streamline the meta tag implementation, reduce redundancy, and ensure alignment with current SEO practices and social media card standards. 😄

Comments.Body: I'm ok about removing redundant tags but not removing the keywords meta tag. This is not only about SEO, if users want to specify the keywords tag, they should be able to do.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: So, going to create a PR!
Comments.Author: ghost

MDU6SXNzdWU4MjM3MTYyMzE=.Title: Image processing plugin
MDU6SXNzdWU4MjM3MTYyMzE=.Author: kevinlang
MDU6SXNzdWU4MjM3MTYyMzE=.Body:
It would be nice to have a plugin that can take a source image and output various optimized formats, leveraging `srcset` and the like to enable responsive images.

As far as I can tell, this is blocked by https://github.com/lovell/sharp/issues/2583 which is blocked by upstream Deno support.

Comments.Body:
I've used sharp in other node projects and it's an incredible library, and a Deno version would allow us to create plugin similar to Eleventy (https://www.11ty.dev/docs/plugins/image/)

For now, the only way is a script on "afterBuild" event, for example using imagick:

```js
site.addEventListener("afterBuild", "convert -resize 800x800 _site/*.jpg");
```

But definitelly, if sharp is ported to Deno, we will have a image processing plugin.
Comments.Author: oscarotero
oscarotero

Comments.Body:
ImageScript has the `rewrite` branch that looks awesome: https://github.com/matmen/ImageScript/tree/rewrite
It includes a lot of improvements, like Typescript and more interpolation modes, so it's a good alternative to sharp.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I managed to hack something together with the afterBuild hook from @oscarotero and using the squoosh cli for now: https://github.com/enjikaka/barlingshult/blob/main/squoosh.js

As Squoosh is WASM; wouldn't that CLI, or Squoosh itself, be portable to Deno for this also? 🤔
Comments.Author: enjikaka

Comments.Body:
Squoosh is interesting. I never worked with WASM but looks like a Deno port should be feasible. If anyone want to work on this, that would be awesome.

BTW, @enjikaka I like you approach of using the HTML as the source to get and convert the images. It's similar to other plugins like [inline](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/plugins/inline.js). I'll change this plugin for something more similar to yours.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
If it's keeps raining outside I might give the Deno/WASM/Squoosh combo a try; otherwise garden word has higher priority here. 👨🏻‍🌾 

@oscarotero Glad you like it. I haven't thought it through completely either, so the HTML-approach might not be sustainable in the long run. But it worked for now at least. It would be a lot cleaner code with proper path handling and DOM parsing like in the inline plugin!
Comments.Author: enjikaka

Comments.Body: Garden has always priority over programing, agreed. And about the html-approach, yes, I'm refering to parse and use DOM API to search the images. I'm thinking on something like this: ```html ```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Hm, instead of `resize` maybe re-use srcset but without urls and add that in while transforming? In: ```html ``` Out: ```html ``` At first glance that feels like an easier API for an end-user, at least if familiar with srcet from before. That would need a check against exernal images though. The `resize` attribute option is better in that way; that it being defined or not would be checked for, instead of the src. `data-srcset` as input has the same pro, with the added benefit that the input doesn't have an invalid srcset or invalid attribute name.
Comments.Author: enjikaka

It allows to process images using ImageMagick and generate different versions.

It uses `_data` files to assign different processing functions. Example:

I_kwDOEYAvFc6PLYFS.Title: HMR doesn't detect new _data.yml when remote file is specified
I_kwDOEYAvFc6PLYFS.Author: wjharney
I_kwDOEYAvFc6PLYFS.Body:
### Version

v2.2.2

### Platform

macOS

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

The expected behavior of HMR is that creating, updating, or deleting any file will result in changes being reflected in
the browser without needing to refresh the page or restart the server. However, when creating or deleting a `_data.yml`
file that has an associated remote file, the expected changes are not seen unless the server is restarted.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Clone this gist: https://gist.github.com/wjharney/d73914e8e74b77bc3a99e938af33bce9
2. Run `deno task serve` to start the server.
3. Visit http://localhost:3000/posts/hello-world/ to see the post, with the heading "Base Template".
4. While the server is running, run `echo 'layout: layouts/replacement.vto' > posts/_data.yml` to change the layout used for the post.
5. Refresh the page.

**Expected Result:** The heading should be "Replacement Template".

**Actual Result:** The heading is "Base Template".

6. Stop and restart the server. The post now uses the correct template, with the heading "Replacement Template".
7. Delete `posts/_data.yml`.
8. Refresh the page.

**Expected Result:** The heading should be "Base Template".

**Actual Result:** Neither template is used; the page only contains the post content, "Hello World".



### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

_No response_

### What is the expected behavior?

The page should be updated without needing to reload the page or restart the server.

### What do you see instead?

The changes aren't reflected unless the server is restarted.

### Additional information

- Updating `posts/_data.yml` works as expected.
- Creating a third layout while the server is running and updating the `_data.yml` to point to that layout works as expected.
- Creating, updating, or deleting a `_data.yml` that does not have a remote file associated works as expected.

Comments.Body:
Indeed, that's a bug.
I'm investigating the best way to fix it. Will be fixed in the next version of Lume.
Thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I just uploaded a fix.
If you want to test it, you can upgrade Lume to the latest development version `deno task lume upgrade --dev`.
It will be available in the next stable version (v2.2.4)
Comments.Author: oscarotero

### Version 2.1.3 ### Platform ubuntu ### What steps will reproduce the bug? 1. Create project with following files: `deno.json`, `_config.ts`, `main.tsx` `deno.json` ```json { "imports": { "lume/": "https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.1.3/" }, "tasks": { "lume": "echo \"import 'lume/cli.ts'\" | deno run -A -", "build": "deno task lume", "serve": "deno task lume -s" }, "compilerOptions": { "exactOptionalPropertyTypes": true, "types": [ "lume/types.ts" ], "jsx": "precompile", "jsxImportSource": "https://esm.sh/preact@10.19.3" } } ``` `_config.ts` ```ts import lume from "lume/mod.ts" import esbuild from "lume/plugins/esbuild.ts" const site = lume() site.use(esbuild({ extensions: [".tsx"], options: { minify: false, bundle: false, splitting: true }, })) export default site ``` `main.tsx` ``` import { render } from "https://esm.sh/preact@10.19.3" const App = () =>

Example Page

render(, document.getElementById("root")!) ``` 2. run `deno task build`. ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? it always happens. ### What is the expected behavior? after building the project must stop immediately. ### What do you see instead? ``` $ deno task build Task build deno task lume Task lume echo "import 'lume/cli.ts'" | deno run -A - Loading config file file:///home/scarf/repo/etc/esbuild-issue/_config.ts 🔥 /main.js <- /main.tsx 🍾 Site built into ./_site 1 files generated in 0.01 seconds WARN After waiting 10 seconds, there are some timers that avoid ending the process. WARN They have been forcibly closed. ``` The plugin hangs and gets killed after 10 seconds. ### Additional information it might be that `esbuild.stop()` isn't called?

I_kwDOEYAvFc5fxiKE.Title: The date plugin should support specifying a timezone
I_kwDOEYAvFc5fxiKE.Author: andreubotella
I_kwDOEYAvFc5fxiKE.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Currently, the date plugin uses the system's default timezone. This can result in a difference between local testing and deployment, if the build for deployment uses CI (Github Actions uses the UTC timezone, for example, [although this is configurable](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/set-timezone)).

The timezone to use should be able to be set in the plugin's options, but it would also be nice to have a format that will display a date as with `Date.prototype.toISOString`. This is equivalent to `date("ATOM")` in a UTC timezone, but since `toISOString` is a standard, it would be useful to also make it available regardless of the timezone set in the plugin options.

Comments.Body:
The only way I know to set a timezone that works consistently everywhere (not only with `date` plugin but also anywhere you do a `new Date()`, is by setting the `TZ` environment variable.

This is what I use in the task for tests: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/deno.json#L4

So I recommend to edit your lume task to include this environment variable with your timezone of choice.

It might be configurable in the `date` plugin options (there's a package to convert dates to different timezones https://date-fns.org/v2.29.3/docs/Time-Zones) but not sure if it's the best way, because it would work only at plugin level, to format a date. The dates defined in frontmatters or filenames (like `2022-01-01_hello.md`) would be created with the system's timezone, and this could affect to order the pages by date, or to select pages from a specific date.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
In my (WIP) blog I'm using `"git created"` by default, and overriding that when needed with ISO timestamps, so I don't run into this issue when parsing dates. But yeah, it is an issue in general. Whenever the temporal proposal ships, it should allow parsing dates with a timezone option, but I'm not sure if it would allow the variety of date formats that `new Date()` supports.

I framed this issue specifically about date formatting because of that, but if you think any such options should also apply to date parsing, that's fine. I can define a custom `date` filter, so this is not a blocker for me.
Comments.Author: andreubotella

Comments.Body:
Yeah, I think temporal should make things more easy.

If you find a simple way to have this in the `date` plugin, please let me know. There are some issues in date-fns related with this https://github.com/date-fns/date-fns/issues/489 but looks like there's not way to pass a timezone for formatting.
Comments.Author: oscarotero
binyamin

Comments.Body:
@binyamin dayjs looks nice but the available formats are different from date-fns: [dayjs](https://day.js.org/docs/en/display/format) vs [date-fns](https://date-fns.org/v2.30.0/docs/format), so it would break things.

I'd rather to wait until I can switch to something more standard like the Temporal API or even the Intl API that doesn't require any external dependency and it's already supported by Deno (but it's buggy in my native language, Galician, so I cannot use it for now).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Temporal API is stable in Deno 1.40 now: https://deno.com/blog/v1.40 :)
Comments.Author: brookback

Comments.Body:
It's not stable yet, it requires the `--unstable-temporal` flag.
But Lume 2 comes with the temporal polyfill as a dependency, so maybe it can be used to modify the timezone of a specific date.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Ah, I misread. Sorry.
Comments.Author: brookback

I_kwDOEYAvFc6tEmZw.Title: LightningCSS plugin not working as expected
I_kwDOEYAvFc6tEmZw.Author: yeskunall

Comments.Body:
The problem is in `site.copy("/styles.css")`.
Copied files are not processed. If you remove this line, it should work.

This is the expected behavior but many people reported this as a bug, and it willl be fixed in Lume 3. You can see more details in the release post that I'm working on: https://github.com/lumeland/blog/blob/main/posts/lume-3.md
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> Copied files are not processed. If you remove this line, it should work.

Totally forgot about this. A massive oversight on my part. Thank you as always, Óscar!

FWIW, I also had to drop the relative path and simply use `reset.css` (and not `./reset.css`) or I got this error:

```sh
error: Uncaught (in worker "") (in promise) Error: No result
    at napi_create_error (file:///Users/kimchi/.cache/deno/npm/registry.npmjs.org/napi-wasm/1.1.3/index.mjs:959:15)
    at <anonymous> (wasm://wasm/0362caf2:1:270635)
    at <anonymous> (wasm://wasm/0362caf2:1:124704)
    at Object.bundle (file:///Users/kimchi/.cache/deno/npm/registry.npmjs.org/napi-wasm/1.1.3/index.mjs:265:19)
    at bundleAsync (file:///Users/kimchi/.cache/deno/npm/registry.npmjs.org/lightningcss-wasm/1.29.1/async.mjs:68:21)
    at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:177:7)
    at async lightningCSSBundler (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.2/plugins/lightningcss.ts:144:24)
    at async Processors.run (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.2/core/processors.ts:32:7)
    at async Site.#buildPages (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.2/core/site.ts:702:5)
    at async Site.build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.2/core/site.ts:550:9)
error: Uncaught (in promise) Error: Unhandled error in child worker.
    at Worker.#pollControl (ext:runtime/11_workers.js:204:19)
    at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:177:7)
```
Comments.Author: yeskunall

Comments.Body:
`./reset.css` path is relative to `styles.css` so Lume loads the file `/reset.css` instead of `/_includes/reset.css`.
Removing the relative path (simply `reset.css`) makes Lume to find the file in the `_includes` folder.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5y3Jnb.Title: tailwindcss arbitrary variants are broken
I_kwDOEYAvFc5y3Jnb.Author: hesxenon
I_kwDOEYAvFc5y3Jnb.Body:
### Version

1.19.1

### Platform

linux

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Setup a new lume project with jsx + tailwind, then create jsx layout that makes use of arbitrary variants like `[&:not(:focus)]`. 

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

every time, it's not a heisenbug :)

### What is the expected behavior?

Arbitrary variants should work

### What do you see instead?

Arbitrary variants don't work

### Additional information

I've traced this bug a bit and the reason is that when the tailwind plugin processes the `page.content` the string is already html escaped (`[&:not(:focus)]`) and thus the needed classes won't be generated.

While I can work around this by registering a processor before the tailwind plugin and just "un-escape" the content string it feels very brittle and hacky.

I'm not sure what the appropriate solution would be here but I _think_ it would make more sense to only escape the generated HTML before saving instead of adapting the tailwind plugin for this use case as this would prevent further bugs like this for other future plugins.

Comments.Body:
Hi. I'm not a Tailwind expert. Do you mean something like this? ```jsx export default function () { return
Hello world
} ``` As I can see, the output html code is this: ```html
Hello world
``` Lume doesn't escape HTML code, maybe it's JSX? I just tried this HTML code in nunjucks and the exported code is the same: ```html
Hello world
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: damn, I wasn't sure who's responsible for escaping the generated html - so it seems the jsx plugin should be investigated? If that's not possible I guess the only fix is for the tailwind plugin to "un-escape" the content before passing it to tailwind, no?
Comments.Author: hesxenon

Comments.Body: I think it is an issue from Tailwind side. JSX does the correct thing, since in HTML the `&` must be always escaped.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
well yes and no - on one side it's tailwinds fault for building on a syntax that contains dangerous characters, on the other side this syntax is inside a string where it needn't be escaped.

So no, I don't think that we can just say tailwind has to change their syntax.
Comments.Author: hesxenon

Comments.Body: okay, I tried getting around this by passing `extensions: [".html", ".tsx"]` to the tailwindcss plugin options, to no avail :/ the respective classes sadly still aren't generated.

oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc6Ss2xl.Title: JS file changes require server restart
I_kwDOEYAvFc6Ss2xl.Author: josh-collinsworth
I_kwDOEYAvFc6Ss2xl.Body:
### Version

2.2.4

### Platform

MacOS Sonoma 14.5 (M2)

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

- Load a basic client-side `.js` file. In my case, I have a `/_includes/layout.tsx` file that calls to `.js` file in the root folder via a  `<script>` tag.)
- After starting the server, make a change to the JS file
- Although the CLI reports that the change has been detected and then sent to the broser, the change is not registered until and unless the server is restarted. The JavaScript that was in the file initially will continue to run, unchanged, even after a hard refresh and with caching disabled in dev tools.

(Note: it appears this may be the case with images as well; perhaps any static assets)

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

As far as I can tell, there's nothing special other than the conditions above.

### What is the expected behavior?

After detecting the change and sending it to the browser, the new JS should load instead of the old JS.

### What do you see instead?

The old, initial JS continues to load and run until and unless the server is restarted.

### Additional information

We're seeing this issue on https://github.com/denoland/docs, if you'd like to reproduce from there. (In my case, editing `sidebar.client.ts`, which should run on any individual doc page.)

Comments.Body: Thanks @josh-collinsworth It's indeed a bug that [I just fixed it](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/commit/d15ada379be418dad90bed2c228533cbd52ed47c). I'm in the middle of releasing Lume v2.3 with some new features, and one of the changes is to run the build in a Worker. This will allows to restart the build completely after some changes like modify the `_config.ts` file (i.e. to add or remove plugins) without need to stop and restart the build manually. Unfortunately, now Prism is failing: Inspecting the NPM code, seems that the `self` variable is not defined: Replacing this variable with `globalThis` works fine. Maybe this is a Deno bug? `self` property [should exist in Workers contexts](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/self) If Workers are not stable on Deno, I can revert the changes and run the build in the main thread instead of a Worker, but it's a pitty because we lose the ability to rebuild after installing a new plugin.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: FYI I have filed an issue with the `self` bug: https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/25008
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Seems that the Deno bug with Workers is fixed!
You can use the latest development version of Lume with `deno task lume upgrade --dev`.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Hi @oscarotero - it looks like we may have a recent regression on this bug somewhere. (Or, maybe it was fixed for one file type, but not another.)

I'm noticing that I need to restart the server before I can see changes to `.tsx` files, even when on the latest Lume, in the [Deno Docs repo](https://github.com/denoland/docs).

I _believe_ this was working before, but if so, it hasn't been for a bit, I'm told. Any insights as to where this issue with `.tsx` files might be coming from?
Comments.Author: josh-collinsworth

Comments.Body:
Hi. I just tried to modify `_components/Footer.tsx` and `_includes/layout.tsx` and it was working in my end (the site is rebuilt and the browser reloaded, showing the changes).

Do you know any particular file with which doesn't work?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Sorry, @oscarotero, I didn't realize the problem is much more narrowly scoped than I thought.

The issue can be seen [in this PR](https://github.com/denoland/docs/pull/1245) (which I think should be merged into `main` within the next day or so).

Specifically, the issue seems to occur in the `examples/index.tsx` file; there, we're importing and using a `<LandingPage />` component.

Changes made to the base `examples/index.tsx` file seem to update just fine. However, changes made to that imported `LandingPage.tsx` component are _not_ reflected without a server restart.

So maybe the issue is with one `.tsx` file importing another and using it in this way, somehow? Or maybe we're just doing something wrong here. Looks like this is a little more off-topic of the original issue than I thought, but If you have any guidance it would be much appreciated.
Comments.Author: josh-collinsworth
oscarotero

Comments.Body: @oscarotero Thank you so much for your help! Problem solved. ✅
Comments.Author: josh-collinsworth

MDU6SXNzdWU5MzkyODcyMzA=.Title: Using `site.dest()` to set metrics destination throws exception
MDU6SXNzdWU5MzkyODcyMzA=.Author: shah
MDU6SXNzdWU5MzkyODcyMzA=.Body:
@oscarotero per our conversation on Discord, I'm trying to set the destination of the metrics file to the site's output directory in my `_config.ts` using your suggestion:

`site.options.metrics = site.dest(".observability-metrics.json");` 

Doing a build with that line in `_config.ts` results in:

```javascript
in _config.ts shows this error:
error: Uncaught (in promise) NotFound: No such file or directory (os error 2)
  async save(file: string) {
                         ^
    at deno:core/core.js:86:46
    at unwrapOpResult (deno:core/core.js:106:13)
    at async open (deno:runtime/js/40_files.js:46:17)
    at async writeFile (deno:runtime/js/40_write_file.js:58:18)
    at async Metrics.save (https://deno.land/x/lume@v0.25.1/metrics.ts:80:26)
    at async printMetrics (https://deno.land/x/lume@v0.25.1/cli/build.ts:50:33)
```

When I used the following, it works fine:

`site = lume({metrics: "_site/.observability-metrics.json"})`

It looks like a timing issue when using `site.dest(...)` (perhaps because dest isn't created?). I like my metrics to go to the destination folder because it's useful for CI/CD and my site has a `/health` directory.

Comments.Body:
The problem is `site.dest()` returns the full path to this file, but `site.options.metrics` expects a path relative to cwd.
The solution is use the `site.options.dest` value:

```js
site.options.metrics = join(site.options.dest, ".observability-metrics.json");
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
That worked, thanks!

Another quick request: if `--quiet` is requested, `⏲ Metrics data saved in ...` message should probably be suppressed.
Comments.Author: shah

Comments.Body: Ok, done in `master`. I'll release `v0.25.2` in a couple with this changes and other possible bugfixes.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5NkDB9.Title: SASS plugin includes
I_kwDOEYAvFc5NkDB9.Author: jrson83
I_kwDOEYAvFc5NkDB9.Body:
# Version Info
Windows 11 (64 bit)
deno 1.23.2 (release, x86_64-pc-windows-msvc)
v8 10.4.132.8
typescript 4.7.2
lume v1.10.0

# Description

The SASS docs [Configuration](https://lume.land/plugins/sass/#configuration) chapter states:

> includes: An array of directories to search for the `@imported` files. By default is _includes.

When I move `.scss` files outside the `_includes` directory, they get transformed to `_site/whatever_dir/[file].css`, even if I do not specify any `sass.includes` path.

I created the following project structure:

```
lume-root
 ┣ _includes
 ┃ ┗ sass
 ┃ ┃ ┣ index.scss
 ┃ ┃ ┗ _test.scss
 ┣ deno.json
 ┣ import_map.json
 ┗ _config.ts
```

```scss
// index.scss
@import "./test";

body {
  background-color: #000;
}
```

```js
// _config.ts
import lume from "lume/mod.ts";
import sass from "lume/plugins/sass.ts";

const site = lume();

site.use(sass());

export default site;
```

When I run `deno task serve`, the `.scss` files are not getting transformed to `.css`.

In serve mode, if I make changes to the files they getting registered:
```shell
Changes detected:
- /_includes/sass/index.scss
```

I tried setting `sass.includes` with `./` and without `./` combinations as `string` and also as `Array<string>`, but nothing worked.

- ["./_includes/sass/"]
- ["./_includes/sass"]
- ["./_includes/"]
- ["./_includes"]

I created a different directory with an underscore `_styles` and moved the files in the directory, they not getting transformed. I also tried to specify the `sass.includes`.

So it seems, the plugins default is to search in every subdir of the rootdir, but not if the dir contains an underscore.
If you need I'll create an example for reproduction. Thanks for the great work!

Comments.Body:
The `_includes` folder contains files that can be included by other files but they aren't loaded and rendered if no other file include them. In your example, you have all scss files in the `_includes` folder but there isn't any other scss file outside this folder that import these files. Let's say you have this structure:

```
lume-root
 ┣ _includes
 ┃ ┗ sass
 ┃   ┗ component1.scss
 ┃   ┗ component2.scss
 ┣ deno.json
 ┣ import_map.json
 ┣ styles.scss
 ┗ _config.ts
```
And the `styles.scss` file (that it's ouside `_includes` so Lume will load it) import the other two files:

```scss
// styles.scss

@import "sass/component1.scss";
@import "sass/component2.scss";
```

This will output the `_site/styles.css` file with the content of the two imported files.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> In your example, you have all scss files in the _includes folder but there isn't any other scss file outside this folder that import these files. Let's say you have this structure:

Okay, now I got it, If I set it up like this, it is working. Thanks for the explanation.

Closing.
Comments.Author: jrson83

I_kwDOEYAvFc6VnC0E.Title: Detect changes in _config.ts file if the src folder is not cwd
I_kwDOEYAvFc6VnC0E.Author: oscarotero
I_kwDOEYAvFc6VnC0E.Body:
If the src folder is a subdirectory (say `./src`) Lume watcher doesn't detect changes in the _config file because it's out of src.
Watcher should add automatically the `_config.ts` and `_cms.ts` files.

I_kwDOEYAvFc6SVagj.Title: Error: The pages (generated) and (generated) have the same output path "/favicon.ico". Use distinct 'url' values to resolve the conflict
I_kwDOEYAvFc6SVagj.Author: ooker777
I_kwDOEYAvFc6SVagj.Body:
Without the favicon plugin, an empty project with only an `index.md` and `favicon.svg` at the root works fine. However, with this config:
```js
import lume from "lume/mod.ts";
import wiki from "wiki/mod.ts";
import favicon from "lume/plugins/favicon.ts";

const site = lume();

site.use(favicon({
  // input: "/favicon.svg",
}));

site.use(wiki());

export default site;
```
The error:
```
deno task build
Task build deno task lume
Task lume echo "import 'lume/cli.ts'" | deno run -A -
Loading config file file:///D:/QC%20supplements/Code/Websites/GW-vat-ly-an-du-trong-triet-hoc/_config.ts
🔥 / <- /index.md
🔥 /styles.css <- /styles.css
🔥 /favicon.ico <- (generated)
🔥 /apple-touch-icon.png <- (generated)       
🔥 /pagefind/pagefind.en_2c8b113be1.pf_meta <-
 (generated)
🔥 /pagefind/wasm.en.pagefind <- (generated)  
🔥 /pagefind/fragment/en_b0352ea.pf_fragment <- (generated)
🔥 /pagefind/index/en_c8c9509.pf_index <- (generated)
🔥 /pagefind/pagefind.js <- (generated)       
🔥 /pagefind/pagefind-highlight.js <- (generated)
🔥 /pagefind/pagefind-ui.js <- (generated)    
🔥 /pagefind/pagefind-ui.css <- (generated)   
🔥 /pagefind/pagefind-modular-ui.js <- (generated)
🔥 /pagefind/pagefind-modular-ui.css <- (generated)
🔥 /pagefind/pagefind-entry.json <- (generated)
Error: The pages (generated) and (generated) have the same output path "/favicon.ico". Use distinct 'url' values to resolve the conflict. 
    at FSWriter.savePage (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.2.4/core/writer.ts:83:15)
    at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:168:7)
    at async https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.2.4/core/writer.ts:50:13
    at async Promise.all (index 4)
    at async concurrent (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.2.4/core/utils/concurrent.ts:21:3)     
    at async FSWriter.savePages (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.2.4/core/writer.ts:47:5)       
    at async Site.build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.2.4/core/site.ts:537:19)
    at async build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.2.4/cli/build.ts:22:3)
    at async Command.fn (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.2.4/cli.ts:147:5)
    at async Command.execute (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.7/command/command.ts:1794:7)
```

v2.2.4 on Windows

Comments.Body: Favicon plugin is already used by `wiki` theme. If you use it again, it's run twice, generating two favicon.ico files and that's why you get the error, because you're generating the same output file twice.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: that makes sense. In that case, how can I use a custom path to the favicon? It seems to me that I have to download the theme locally, not importing from the repo

Comments.Body:
1. It seems there is a problem with how Deno deal with modules with unspecified version. (I intentionally do so to get the latest versions.) At first I thought that it's the [caching](https://docs.deno.com/runtime/fundamentals/modules/#reloading-modules "Modules and dependencies") that causes the problem, but using the `--reload` flag doesn't solve. I must specify the version to have it worked. Can you confirm?
2. I just want to add some buttons to other sites, e.g. Discord, YouTube, Facebook, and some more info on the sidebar. It seems that the layout structure has been changed with the new version of the theme
Comments.Author: ooker777

Comments.Body:
> I intentionally do so to get the latest versions.

Module versions should be always specified. This avoid a lot of problems like different behaviors depending on Deno cache.

The performance issue you have reported on Discord is caused by the menu. In old versions, the menu was generated in the HTML. So a site with 1000 pages had a tree menu of 1000 elements and due the menu is build for all 1000 pages, this means the creation of 1000x1000 = 1 million of new html which make deno-dom library slow.
In recent versions, this have been changed and now, the tree menu is exported to a json, and the menu is generated by the browser with a custom element. This is what have changed in the default layout.

Regarding extra buttons like social media, the new version allows to place links with icons. Take a look to https://vento.js.org/ as an example.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5cffTl.Title: Passing `Data` to components breaks their behavior
I_kwDOEYAvFc5cffTl.Author: naiyerasif
I_kwDOEYAvFc5cffTl.Body:
### Version 1.15.1 ### Platform Windows ### What steps will reproduce the bug? Currently, Lume injects the components in the templates so that they are easily accessible by the layouts and templates. For example, I have an `_components/icon.ts` as follows. ```ts import { attributes } from "lume/plugins/attributes.ts" const defaults = { class: ["icon"], role: "img" } export default function (name: string, { title, ...attribs }: { title?: string; [key: string]: unknown }) { const href = `#${name}` const props = attributes({ ...attribs, ...defaults }) if (title) { return ` ` } else { return ` ` } } ``` Lume fails to render the component when I try to use it in a template through `comp` as described by the [docs](https://lume.land/docs/core/components/#create-your-own-components), for example, like this. ```ts export const title = "Home" export const renderOrder = 1 export default function ({ comp }) { const icon = comp.icon("moon", { slot: "dark" }) console.log(icon) return `

${title}

${icon}` } ``` The issue originates because of the ` ...attribs` specified in the component as I sometimes expect dynamic HTML attributes to be passed to the component (for example, `slot` in this case). Unfortunately, Lume also passes the entire `Data` object to the component and the `attributes` plugin fails to handle it. This also leads to unexpected issues. For example, sometimes, I may pass a `title` attribute to the component. But when I export a `title` in the template, that's also being implicitly passed to the component which is definitely not correct. ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? Reproducing this does not require anything specific. Here's the repository with the reproduction: https://github.com/naiyerasif/lume-components-issue Launch the application through `deno task serve` and you should encounter the issue described above. ### What is the expected behavior? The components should receive only those arguments which are explicitly defined in their contract. The `Data` object should not be passed to them at all. ### What do you see instead? I get the following error on console. ```sh Error: Error rendering this page - page: /index.tmpl.ts at (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/renderer.ts:138:19) at async concurrent (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/utils.ts:193:3) at async Renderer.renderPages (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/renderer.ts:126:7) at async Site.#buildPages (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/site.ts:590:5) at async Site.build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/site.ts:518:9) at async build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/cli/build.ts:36:3) at async Command.execute (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.7/command/command.ts:1794:7) at async Command.parseCommand (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.7/command/command.ts:1639:14) at async (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/cli.ts:158:3) Caused by TypeError: value.replace is not a function at escape (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/plugins/attributes.ts:124:16) at joinAttributes (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/plugins/attributes.ts:117:29) at attributes (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/plugins/attributes.ts:27:10) at default (file:///E:/web/lume-components-issue/src/_components/icon.ts:7:16) | 6 | const href = `#${name}` | 7 | const props = attributes({ ...attribs, ...defaults }) | ~ | ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^ | 8 | at ModuleEngine.renderSync (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/plugins/modules.ts:38:9) at (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/component_loader.ts:100:20) at Object.render (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/component_loader.ts:98:32) at Proxy. (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/source.ts:542:60) at default (file:///E:/web/lume-components-issue/src/index.tmpl.ts:5:20) | 4 | export default function ({ comp }) { | 5 | const icon = comp.icon("moon", { slot: "dark" }) | ~ | ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^ | 6 | console.log(icon) ``` ### Additional information _No response_

Comments.Body:
As a workaround, I am explicitly importing the components as follows. ```ts import icon from "./_components/icon.ts" export const title = "Home" export const renderOrder = 1 export default function () { return `

${title}

${icon("moon", { slot: "dark" })}` } ```
Comments.Author: naiyerasif

Comments.Body:
Mmm, ok. Interesting use case.

Components are designed to work with any template engine, so they need to receive all properties in the first  argument in an object (this is how JSX works).
And they also needs access to the `comp` object in order to create nested components, and other global data like `search`, etc.

Both requirements are not compatible with the use of spread `{...attribs}` to collect the remaining attributes. I have to think in a way to fix this. Maybe defining these properties as not enumerable. 🤔  

Another workaround is using an `attr` property to store all attributes:

You component:

```js
export default function ({ name, title, attrs }) {
}
```

In your template:

```js
const icon = comp.icon({ name: "moon", attrs: {slot: "dark"} })
```

I'm going to do some tests to see if I can fix it.
Thanks.
Comments.Author: oscarotero
oscarotero

Comments.Body: @oscarotero Thanks for taking a stab at this. I tried the dev build and I am still getting the same error. Here's what I changed: https://github.com/naiyerasif/lume-components-issue/commit/bdbb735693d001618d1c1607f2f7fde8fa5e2c98#diff-ca7f5073a339af20e76bf73a56628c71ae873d2fe29dce97e2677a03ad9dc8bc
Comments.Author: naiyerasif
oscarotero

Comments.Body: Yup, that worked. Thanks @oscarotero.
Comments.Author: naiyerasif

Comments.Body: Lume 1.15.2 was released, including this change.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5vV0Wn.Title: Layout doesn't applied to `.js` urls
I_kwDOEYAvFc5vV0Wn.Author: cawa-93
I_kwDOEYAvFc5vV0Wn.Body:
### Version

v1.18.4

### Platform

Windows 11

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Create a generator for `.js` file using layout

```js
// app.tmpl.js

const url = './app.js'
const layout = 'layout.njk'
```

```njk
<!-- _includes/layout.njk -->
console.log('app.js')
```
And run `serve`. 

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?
The only problem is when you try to generate the `.js` file. If you generate the usual html (`export const url = './app/'`), the layout is applied as expected.


### What is the expected behavior?

single `app.js` file with `console.log('app.js')` should be generated

### What do you see instead?

```node
⚠️ Warning Skipped page /app.js (file content is empty)
```
Although obviously the file should not be empty,

### Additional information

_No response_

Comments.Body:
Layouts are used only for `.html` pages.
Any page that generates a non-html page (js, css, jpg, etc) doesn't not use the layout. 
See [regular pages vs asset pages](https://lume.land/docs/core/concepts/#page-files).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc57R5j5.Title: JSX onclick attributes are stripped during build
I_kwDOEYAvFc57R5j5.Author: brimworks
I_kwDOEYAvFc57R5j5.Body:
### Version

2.0.1

### Platform

Mac

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Write a simple JSX component with an `onClick` attribute:

(`_components/Button.jsx`)
```jsx
export default ({ }, helpers) => (
<button onClick="alert('hi')">Click Me!</button>
)
```

When this component is rendered, it strips out the `onclick` HTML attribute.

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

Always

### What is the expected behavior?

When rendered, the `onClick` HTML attribute should remain unmolested.

### What do you see instead?

The `onClick` HTML attribute is stripped off.

### Additional information

_No response_

Comments.Body:
Note that if I use `onclick` attribute name I get:

```
Warning: Invalid event handler property `onclick`. React events use the camelCase naming convention, for example `onClick`.
    at button
```

...as this was the suggestion here: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/issues/396#issuecomment-1474031811
Comments.Author: brimworks

Comments.Body:
Seems that React cannot [render statically attributes for events](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/24440991/render-onclick-attribute-using-react-js-when-calling-rendercomponenttostaticmark).

You can use the `jsx_preact` plugin, that keeps the `onclick` attribute.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Thanks for the response! Yes, switching to `jsx_preact` plugin fixes the problem. Perhaps a note about this should be added to the doc site... and maybe even make preact the default? Just some thoughts. I very much appreciate the help!
Comments.Author: brimworks

I_kwDOEYAvFc51_7ju.Title: [UX] Inline plugin should support rewriting path in `site.process()`
I_kwDOEYAvFc51_7ju.Author: ngdangtu-vn
I_kwDOEYAvFc51_7ju.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

This is how I setup `_config.js`

1. Install inline plugin
2. Install sass plugin
3. Add rewrite path to `_config.js`   
  ```js
site.process(['.css'], (p) => {
   if (!p.data.url) return void 0
   p.data.url = p.data.url.replace('style/', '')
})
  ```
4. Add `<link rel="stylesheet" href="/my-styles.css" inline>` to layout file

The `my-style.scss` is actually inside `/style/my-style.scss` and being rewritten to `/my-style.css`. At first I thought because the plugin ran early so I switch step 1 and step 3 position. But the result is still the same. The inline plugin requires us to input correct path before rewritten with the post-compiled file (instead scss, we go css).

This can be a bit confuse for later comeback. Is it possible to set path after rewritten only?

Comments.Body:
This can be solved by reordering like this: 2 → 3 → 1

Not great if you want to keep all installing plugins code in 1 block but it's clear that what is happening inside the lume :?
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
The plugins/processors are executed in the same order they are registered:

1. SASS plugin: will compile the scss files and convert `/style/my-style.scss` to `/style/my-style.css`
2. Rewrite processor: converts the file to `/my-style.css`
3. Inline plugin: It can detect the`/my-style.css` from the `<link>` element and inline it.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: One day, I will write a document about this one. I keep forgetting it and make a mess :p
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

I_kwDOEYAvFc5UCpW8.Title: Use `deno run` instead of `deno eval` in deno.json
I_kwDOEYAvFc5UCpW8.Author: DrSensor
I_kwDOEYAvFc5UCpW8.Body:
I just notice `deno run` support evaluating from stdin. So I think it's better to use it because you can write the lock file and reload the dependencies. For example:
```sh
echo "import 'lume/task.ts'" | deno run --reload --lock=lock.json --lock-write -
```
> **Note** there is a `-` in the end of line

This is better since `deno eval` doesn't support `--lock`/`--lock-write` and `--reload` flag. Some people (including me) might want to keep the lock file to comply with [reproducible build](https://reproducible-builds.org/) philosophy without vendoring the whole dependencies.

Here is my deno.json
```json
{
  "importMap": "build.importmap",
  "tasks": {
    "lume": "echo \"import 'lume/task.ts'\" | deno run --unstable --allow-read --allow-run --lock=lock.json -",
    "update": "deno cache --unstable --lock=lock.json --lock-write --reload _config.ts && echo \"import 'lume/task.ts'\" | deno run --unstable --allow-read --allow-run --lock=lock.json --lock-write --reload - --version"
  }
}
```

Comments.Body:
Oh, that's fantastic! I didn't know this was possible. It also allows to configure the permissions (`deno eval` has all permissions implicitly).
I'll change it in the next version. Thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Every deployment after the furst

### What is the expected behavior?

_No response_

### What do you see instead?

the error as in the log

### Additional information

_No response_

Comments.Body:
Deno deploy doesn't support `npm` imports.
Why do you want to run Lume in Deno Deploy? The recomended way to deploy static sites to Deno Deploy is by using the GitHub Actions. More info here: https://lume.land/docs/advanced/deployment/#deno-deploy
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: That is exacly how i started, and the github action throws the exact same error.. That was my reason to roll with deno deploy, so i could capture the complete log output. The strange thing is, is that my initial deployment worked fine.... My reason for deploying is to better understand the on_demand plugin... My working deployment: https://willem-haring-lume.deno.dev/
Comments.Author: willem-haring

Comments.Body:
Ok, understood. The `on_demand` plugin doesn't work in Deno Deploy because Lume now uses `npm:` imports which aren't supported yet in Deno Deploy. I guess it's matter of time they implement this (issue: https://github.com/denoland/deploy_feedback/issues/314).

Meanwhile, you can use the version `1.11.x` (the last version without npm imports) that should work fine). You have a test here: https://github.com/lumeland/test-lume-ondemand
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Thanks for the feedback, will close this issue then, but might be use-full for others. 

Keep up the good work!
Comments.Author: willem-haring
oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5Q6Oid.Title: Add the `site.component()` function to add global components dynamically
I_kwDOEYAvFc5Q6Oid.Author: oscarotero
I_kwDOEYAvFc5Q6Oid.Body: It would work similar to `site.data()` but for components.

Join our Discord https://discord.gg/deno 18:26:47.250 | Download https://deno.land/x/lume/ci.ts 18:26:47.746 | Warning Implicitly using latest version (v1.17.3) for https://deno.land/x/lume/ci.ts 18:26:47.747 | Download https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.17.3/ci.ts 18:26:48.156 | error: Module not found "https://deno.land/x/lume/ci.ts". 18:26:48.164 | Failed: build command exited with code: 1 18:26:48.920 | Failed: error occurred while running build command ### Additional information I changed the build line to refer to the prior version explicitly and it works. ```sh curl -fsSL https://deno.land/x/install/install.sh | sh && /opt/buildhome/.deno/bin/deno run -A https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.12.0/ci.ts --location=https://main.xxxxx.pages.dev ``` Also, I upgraded lume on my local machine and ran `lume --serve` and that fails. ```sh TypeError: Could not find npm package 'date-fns' matching 2.30.0. Comments.Author: oscarotero
mlanza

Comments.Body:
`lume/task.ts` file was removed too in previous versions. The correct `lume` task is
```
echo "import 'lume/cli.ts'" | deno run --unstable -A -
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
This makes no reference to the cloudflare destination site...

```sh
curl -fsSL https://deno.land/x/install/install.sh | sh && /opt/buildhome/.deno/bin/deno task build
```

It doesn't work.  This does:

```sh
curl -fsSL https://deno.land/x/install/install.sh | sh && /opt/buildhome/.deno/bin/deno run -A https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.12.0/ci.ts --location=https://main.xxxxx.pages.dev
```

Obviously, xxxx out name.
Comments.Author: mlanza

Comments.Body:
It should work if you have the import_map.json file updated with the latest version of Lume.

For example, [this project](https://github.com/lumeland/base-blog) works fine in Cloudflare (https://base-blog.pages.dev/)

Obviously, you can use your script to continue using Lume 1.12.0.
