I_kwDOEYAvFc6XmuVL.Title: Error loading layout files
I_kwDOEYAvFc6XmuVL.Author: smcjustin
I_kwDOEYAvFc6XmuVL.Body:
### Version

v2.3.2

### Platform

MacOS Sequoia 15.0

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

1. Install Deno v1.46.3 and Lume v2.3.2, Lume CMS v0.6.3
2. In the `/src` folder create a `index.md` file. In the front matter add `layout: layouts/base.vto`
3. Create the folder `_includes/layouts` and add the file `base.vto` with boilerplate html
4. Run `lume serve`

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

Every time.

### What is the expected behavior?

Lume should create the file `index.html` in the `_src` directory using the `base.vto` template.

### What do you see instead?

Lume throws an error: **The layout file "/_includes/layouts/base.vto" doesn't exist**

Here is the full output:
```
error: Uncaught (in worker "") (in promise) Error: Error rendering the layout of the page /index.md
            throw new Error(
                  ^
    at https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.3.2/core/renderer.ts:176:19
    at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:175:7)
Caused by: Error: The layout file "/_includes/layouts/base.vto" doesn't exist
    at Renderer.#renderLayout (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.3.2/core/renderer.ts:279:15)
    at https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.3.2/core/renderer.ts:159:52
    at concurrent (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.3.2/core/utils/concurrent.ts:10:33)
    at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:175:7)
    at async Renderer.renderPages (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.3.2/core/renderer.ts:155:7)
    at async Site.#buildPages (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.3.2/core/site.ts:641:5)
    at async Site.build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.3.2/core/site.ts:541:9)
    at async buildSite (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.3.2/cli/utils.ts:48:3)
    at async onmessage (https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.3.2/cli/build_worker.ts:23:16)
error: Uncaught (in promise) Error: Unhandled error in child worker.
    at Worker.#pollControl (ext:runtime/11_workers.js:204:19)
    at eventLoopTick (ext:core/01_core.js:175:7)
```

### Additional information

_No response_

Comments.Body: Is the `_includes` folder inside the `src` folder?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Sorry, that was the issue.

It would be helpful to update this line in **Getting Started > Create a Layout** to be more clear:

> Create a new directory `_includes` and the file `layout.vto` inside it.

To:

> Create a new directory `_includes` _inside the `src` directory_. Create the file `layout.vto` inside it.
Comments.Author: smcjustin

I_kwDOEYAvFc6CZBfT.Title: Search plugin seems to be missing pages generated by the multilanguage plugin
I_kwDOEYAvFc6CZBfT.Author: mindreframer
I_kwDOEYAvFc6CZBfT.Body:
### Version https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.1.1 ### Platform OSX (12.7.3) ### What steps will reproduce the bug? - configure multilanguage plugin - create some pages with same `id` for different languages - render a list of all pages like ```
    {{ for page of search.pages("") }}
  • {{ page.title }} - {{page.url}} - {{page.layout}}
    • {{ /for }}
``` -> pages with multilang features are missing in the list ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? consistently ### What is the expected behavior? multilang pages should be included in search with empty string ### What do you see instead? - nothing ### Additional information Feel free to ask for a bug reproduction in code, if for some reason this bug is not reproducible

Comments.Body: correction: this only manifests with JSX files. Multilang Markdown files are visible in search.pages result
Comments.Author: mindreframer

Comments.Body: I have made a reproduction repo: https://github.com/mindreframer/lume-bug-page-search
Comments.Author: mindreframer

Comments.Body: And it seems it is not related to the extension. I have markdown files, and some of them are in the search results, some not.
Comments.Author: mindreframer

Comments.Body:
The fix/workaround is to include `/` at the end of the URL: 

- https://github.com/mindreframer/lume-bug-page-search/commit/c5be812bf9dd5826bd85d1c62cffbd7622564afe

Then the pages are included in the search result.
Comments.Author: mindreframer

Comments.Body:
That's not a bug.

`search.pages()` only returns HTML pages. This means the url must end with `.html` or `/` (which is equivalent to `/index.html`).

For example: `/pathname` is a non-HTML page, it's just a file without extension. But `/pathname/` is a HTML page because is exported to `/pathname/index.html`.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Thank you for the response! Sorry, been absent last 2 days. OK, good to know, that a missing slash at the end is considered a non-HTML page. Maybe it makes sense to document somewhere, I went through the complete documentation and could not figure out the reason for this behaviour. 

And thanks for Lume, I enjoy using it so far!
Comments.Author: mindreframer

Comments.Body: But then I lose the actual slugify logic?

Comments.Body:
https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/22717d57cc709407270a3654452da86e9a552eaa/utils.js#L97-L102

Can a config key to disable just the L101 step be made?

oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Yeah, that’s one possibility.

And/or maybe the `slugifyUrls` option could take an object of options to configure the slugifier: `slugifyUrls: { nonAscii: true }` (maybe there’s a better name?) would preserve non-ASCII letters (and maybe numbers too) in slugs as it.

Or maybe it could take a string: `"ascii"` (the current default), `"unicode"` (to preserve non-ASCII letters, and maybe numbers, too) or `"off"` (to disable the slugifier).
Comments.Author: valtlai

Comments.Body:
I like the idea of an object with options. For example:
- separator (By default `-`)
- lowercase (By default `true`)
- replacements (The object/map with the characters to replace)
- onlyAlfanumeric (By default `true`)
- onlyFilenames (slugify only the last part of the url, not the directories)

I_kwDOEYAvFc5N5qNn.Title: Module does not provide an export named 'loadWASM'
I_kwDOEYAvFc5N5qNn.Author: jrson83
I_kwDOEYAvFc5N5qNn.Body:
I tried using [rehype-pretty-code](https://github.com/atomiks/rehype-pretty-code) from [esm.sh](https://esm.sh/rehype-pretty-code@0.3.2) with the experimental remark/rehype plugin:

```js
import rehypePrettyCode from "https://esm.sh/rehype-pretty-code@0.3.2";
```

I'm getting the following error on build/serve:

```shell
SyntaxError: The requested module '/v87/vscode-oniguruma@1.6.2/deno/vscode-oniguruma.js' does not provide an export named 'loadWASM'
    at async createSite (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.10.0/cli/utils.ts:15:17)
    at async build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.10.0/cli/build.ts:29:16)
    at async Command.execute (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.24.2/command/command.ts:1275:7)
    at async (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.10.0/cli.ts:173:3)
```

I tried adding the [shiki](https://esm.sh/shiki@0.10.1) esm.sh module to my `import_map.json`. When I run `deno cache --reload _config.ts` I see it is downloading the required module from [vscode-oniguruma](https://esm.sh/v87/vscode-oniguruma@1.6.2/deno/vscode-oniguruma.js), but I still get the same error.

My `deno.json`:

```json
"compilerOptions": {
  "lib": [
    "dom",
    "dom.iterable",
    "dom.asynciterable",
    "deno.ns",
    "deno.unstable"
  ],
}
```

I also tried to use [shikigami](https://github.com/norskeld/shikigami) from [esm.sh](https://esm.sh/@nrsk/shikigami@1.1.2) with the markdown-it plugin and get the same error. I checked the repo from vscode-oniguruma. The `index.ts` has [no default export](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-oniguruma/blob/main/src/index.ts#L394).

```js
import { loadTheme, shikigami } from "https://esm.sh/@nrsk/shikigami@1.1.2";
```

I'm not sure if this is related to `cliffy` or `lume`.

Comments.Body:
Running `deno info https://esm.sh/rehype-pretty-code@0.3.2` you can see the dependencies of this package, that contains `vscode-oniguruma`. Probably the error is on importing the library from `esm.sh`, that doesn't work for all packages.
In Lume I had to import some dependencies from other CDNs (markdown-it, for example, is imported from jspm.dev because esm.sh fails). The automatic conversion of CJS to ESM by esm.sh doesn't work always.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Unfortunately, I also faced this issue which led me to write [remark-starry-night](https://github.com/Microflash/remark-starry-night) to get my codeblocks highlighted with `vscode-textmate` grammars.

Try using jspm.dev or unpkg.com URL imports and see if that works.

Comments.Body:
Thanks for the info. After 3 hours trying to import from different sources, I give up now. There must be seriously something wrong. I'm at least stuck at:

```shell
SyntaxError: The requested module 'vscode-oniguruma' does not provide an export named 'createOnigScanner'
```

Well, it does inside the [main.d.ts](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-oniguruma/blob/main/main.d.ts#L22), but however it seems not to get referenced when importing `release/main.js`, since there exist no types inside the `release` folder.

![2022-07-18_23h30_19](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45284565/179620964-a65aa3b2-5f2e-43c1-b71f-b280f0ae9acb.png)

I_kwDOEYAvFc5x8nsg.Title: Picture plugin enhancements
I_kwDOEYAvFc5x8nsg.Author: cgwrench
I_kwDOEYAvFc5x8nsg.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

I've been testing the picture plugin, as I'm looking to migrate from Eleventy (and it's image plugin) to Lume. There are a few additional features the Eleventy's image plugin offers that I'd love to see in Lume's picture plugin[^1]:

1. `width` and `height` attributes are added to the `img` element.
2. Images aren't scaled up beyond their original dimensions.
3. It's easy to add `deocding="async"` and `loading="lazy"` attributes to the `img` element.
4. It enforces that there is an `alt` attribute set on the `img` element.
5. A hash of the image's contents is included in the filename of generated images (to support setting long cache headers).

I thought I'd raise them here to see if any of these could be incorporated into the picture plugin. I'd be happy to try and implement some of these myself, if there is an appetite to add these.

I think item 5 is covered by https://github.com/lumeland/lume/issues/352, and I think it makes sense to be a separate plugin rather than something purely image related. I'd love to see some progress made on this (and would be happy to try and contribute this, but would need some guidance on the right approach to take).

I think items 3 & 4 are simple enough to do with a plugin I could write myself, so no worries if these don't become part of the picture plugin.

I don't think that item 2 is possible with the picture plugin. Is this currently possible? If not, is this a feature you'd like to see with the picture plugin?

Now for item 1, again this could be done as part of plugin I could write. However, this one gave me some pause for thought. Doing this myself would mean that I've have to read the contents of the generated images to work out their dimensions. As the imagick plugin has already read and processed the images, it seems a bit wasteful to re-read them. However, this is totally doable with another plugin that runs after the picture/imagick plugins, so it's also not a deal breaker if I have to write this myself.

Is adding `width` and `height` attributes to `img` elements something that could be done with the picture plugin?

[^1]: This has partially been covered by [this discussion](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/discussions/350). Apologies for re-raising some of these points.

Comments.Author: oscarotero
cgwrench

Comments.Body:
For point 1: Yeah, your code looks reasonable. There's a repo for experimental / beta plugins: https://github.com/lumeland/experimental-plugins
If you want to work on this plugin, we can include it here, in order to test it.

For point 2: Ok, sorry for the missunderstanding. I'll check that.

For point 5: Great! let me know when you want to start!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: I think point 2 is the only outstanding issue from this thread, and that seems to have been addressed by #530, so I think this issue can be closed.
Comments.Author: cgwrench

I_kwDOEYAvFc6MsdKZ.Title: webp asset files corrupted on build
I_kwDOEYAvFc6MsdKZ.Author: quadratz
I_kwDOEYAvFc6MsdKZ.Body:
### Version

v2.2.1

### Platform

Linux

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Steps:
1. Create a folder called `assets` at the root of the project. Move all webp files there.
2. Add `site.loadAssets([".webp"])` to _config.ts.
3. Run `deno task build`.
4. All of the webp files built are corrupted; their file sizes have changed, and they cannot be opened by the image viewer.

The reproduction repository is available at https://github.com/quadratz/lume-issue.

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

> How often does it reproduce?

Always.

>Is there a required condition?

Not sure.

### What is the expected behavior?

The build result of webp should not changed because there is no process happens.

### What do you see instead?

The webp files produced after building have a larger size than the originals, and they are also corrupted.

### Additional information

The reproduction repository is available at https://github.com/quadratz/lume-issue.

Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Thank you. I can confirm that adding the binary loader solves the issue. Perhaps we should add this information to the documentation.
Comments.Author: quadratz

Comments.Body: Good idea. Thanks for the PR on lume.land!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

MDU6SXNzdWU4MDkzNjA2NDY=.Title: Changes in Pug layout files not updating (with `lume --serve`)
MDU6SXNzdWU4MDkzNjA2NDY=.Author: jaymakes11
MDU6SXNzdWU4MDkzNjA2NDY=.Body:
Example:

```pug
//- _layout.pug

html.no-js(lang="en")
   head
      meta(charset='utf-8')
   body
      div A message from `_layout.pug`
      block pageContent


//- home.pug

extends _layout.pug
block pageContent
   h1 Home
```

Making a change in `home.pug` triggers an update. Making a change in `_layout.pug` does _not_ trigger an update.

When making a change in `_layout.pug`, the cli does say "Changes detected. Building... Done", but the browser does not reload and Lume doesn't seem to actually rebuild (manually reloading the browser does not show the update).

Comments.Body:
Mmm, ok. Looks like pug caches the layouts loaded with `extends`. I'll try to fix it.
Meanwhile, you can use a frontmatter to configure your layout. Look this example: https://github.com/lumeland/test/blob/master/pug/pug-example.pug
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Ok, this bug is fixed in `0.15.1`.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

deno task lume upgrade --dev --version=v3-dev

I_kwDOEYAvFc5N3XDd.Title: Detect and load `.env` files automatically?
I_kwDOEYAvFc5N3XDd.Author: oscarotero
I_kwDOEYAvFc5N3XDd.Body:
Should Lume load automatically .env files in the root directory?

[bun.sh](https://bun.sh/) does it automatically. Deno's std library has dotenv implemented: https://deno.land/std@0.148.0/dotenv so it's easy to use it in Lume.

Comments.Body:
I'm not sure if you're seeking feedback on this, so please feel free to ignore this if not!

For what it's worth, I would be inclined against adding this behaviour, unless there's a significant reason for adding it. While it would definitely be convenient for most cases, I think there is an advantage to keeping `env` management more explicit. Especially given that, as you noted, it's not too inconvenient to add it to a project with Deno's standard lib.

Having said that, I could imagine that some people using Lume might not know Deno in great depth yet, so it would be nice to make it easy for them to use `.env` config. What would you think of the idea of adding a section to the docs which gives an example of how to add `env` management using Deno's standard libs?

Again, this is just my opinion. And I'd definitely be interested to hear the argument for the alternative view!
Comments.Author: bryophyta

Comments.Body: I don't have opinions about the feature itself, but I'm very curious about use cases in a static site generator. Environmental variables are mostly for server side programs and for a SSG it would require at most `NODE_ENV`.
Comments.Author: kidonng

Comments.Body:
Interesting question! A couple of use-cases for environmental variables which I can think of, for a library like Lume:

- Set a target directory for the build process to build to (e.g. if you have an experimental build and a stable build).
- You might also use an abstraction like `dev`/`prod` to control other similar things about the build process (e.g. in dev you might want to skip lengthy processes like image processing).
- If you're running the build process in a continuous integration setup like Github Actions, you may want to be able to pass sensitive values like an API key or salt to the build process, without exposing them in your logs etc.

You could probably find good alternatives to using env variables for all of these use-cases, but it does seem to me like they'd be quite helpful. I'd be interested to hear your thoughts though -- do you think that using env variables would be unsuitable in these cases?
Comments.Author: bryophyta

Comments.Body:
> I'm not sure if you're seeking feedback on this

@bryophyta Feedback is always welcome! It's interesting your thoughts about making the `env` variables more explicit and reduce the  "magic" from Lume.

> I'm very curious about use cases in a static site generator.

A real use case is a static site using the data from the Eventbrite API to show the number of tickets sold. The site is regenerated every hour to get up to date data. It requires a token and an event Id to connect to the API, that are stored in env variables.


> - Set a target directory for the build process to build to (e.g. if you have an experimental build and a stable build).
> - You might also use an abstraction like dev/prod to control other similar things about the build process (e.g. in dev you might want to skip lengthy processes like image processing).

I think these use cases can be addressed easily passing the env variable directly in the build script. For example: `ENV=prod deno task build`. Or creating two deno tasks like `deno task build` and `deno task build-prod`.
To me, the most interesing use case of env files is to store secret values that you don't want to include in the git repo. But I'm agree that maybe it's not a so common use case.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> * Set a target directory for the build process to build to (e.g. if you have an experimental build and a stable build).

This should be using different config, not environment variables.

> * You might also use an abstraction like `dev`/`prod` to control other similar things about the build process (e.g. in dev you might want to skip lengthy processes like image processing).

This is what `NODE_ENV` does (pardon me for mentioning again, I just need a metaphor).

> * If you're running the build process in a continuous integration setup like Github Actions, you may want to be able to pass sensitive values like an API key or salt to the build process, without exposing them in your logs etc.

I think @oscarotero's example covers this well, and also quoting them:

> But I'm agree that maybe it's not a so common use case.

If lume starts to load `.env` automatically, then it probably needs to provide a config to opt out. Or lume can provide a option to load `.env` that is disabled by default.

But it's just too much hassle. Anyone who really wants `.env` need to do nothing more than putting the following line in `_config.ts`:

```js
import "https://deno.land/std/dotenv/load.ts"
```

This is not much harder in Node either, just an extra step to install `dotenv`. The whole point of `.env` is easy to configure.

To sum it all, I love tools picking up related files, but only if those are indeed related.

I would also like to quote one of the goals (or selling points) of Deno:

> Secure by default. No file, network, or environment access, unless explicitly enabled.

---

> [bun.sh](https://bun.sh/) does it automatically

It's a marketing strategy expected from an all-in-one Node alternative that aims to eliminate as much dependencies as possible. But it also contradicts to Deno's philosophy.
Comments.Author: kidonng

Comments.Body:
Vento filters use the pipe operator `|>`. This should work:

```
{{ feature |> vto(it) }}
```

The example in the documentation is from Nunjucks (maybe it's confusing, I'll fix it).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: `{{ feature |> vto(it) }}` does not fix it. I get `[object Promise]` displayed, regardless of whether the piped text contains markup to replace or not:
Comments.Author: doga

Comments.Body: I see. `vto` filter is async. You have to do `{{ feature |> await vto(it) }}`.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
That fixed it, thanks.

_Sidenote:_ Vento's `vto` filter is extremely useful for i18n'ed websites, and since Vento is now officially part of Lume, I am officially retiring my [lume_cross_language_content Lume plugin](https://deno.land/x/lume_cross_language_content@v2.0.1), which was serving the same purpose. `lume_cross_language_content` wasn't really compatible with `deno task serve`, although it was working perfectly with `deno task build`.
Comments.Author: doga

I_kwDOEYAvFc5f_DhR.Title: Finer control over URLs
I_kwDOEYAvFc5f_DhR.Author: thegedge

Comments.Body:
Hi, @thegedge 

Nice comments! To clarify:

> Dates are not stripped from the URL.

As I can see, your files are named like `yyyy-mm-dd-slug` so that's not detected as a date, because Lume uses a underscore to separate dates and slugs (`yyyy-mm-dd_slug`). [See code here](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/core/filesystem.ts#L41).

In theory, the output files should keep the dates because they are not interpreted as dates so Lume doesn't remove it, and I assume that's what you want, right?



> I think it would be helpful if Lume would use the default URL if the url function returned undefined / null / some placeholder value.

This is how it works already. [See the code ](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/core/page_preparer.ts#L63). But it only detect `undefined`, not `null`. 



The use of `modifyUrls` plugin to remove the `.html` extensions is the right way to do that. Previously there was the `no-html-extension` option but it was very hacky [and was removed in Lume 1.13.0](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md#removed-3). The Lume local server should support this kind of URLs ([Here's the code](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/core/server.ts#L143-L145)) out of the box, so you don't need `netlify dev` for that. Let me know if this is not true.

---

PR are very welcome, but I want to understand better the changes that you want to implement.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> This is how it works already. [See the code ](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/core/page_preparer.ts?rgh-link-date=2023-03-06T11%3A00%3A20Z#L63). But it only detect undefined, not null.

Oh, nice! It didn't look to be working for me, but that's likely my own mistake.

> The Lume local server should support this kind of URLs ([Here's the code](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/core/server.ts?rgh-link-date=2023-03-06T11%3A00%3A20Z#L143-L145)) out of the box, so you don't need netlify dev for that. Let me know if this is not true.

I think I may have added the `modifyUrl` plugins _after_ switching to `netlify dev`, and neglected to try out `lume serve` again, but you're totally right that this is working.

> In theory, the output files should keep the dates because they are not interpreted as dates so Lume doesn't remove it, and I assume that's what you want, right?

It is, but let me clarify: My files are named `yyyy-mm-dd_slug` but I want my URL to be `yyyy-mm-dd-slug`. Ideally, I could name my files `yyyy-mm-dd-slug` and have the date parsed properly + remain in the URL. Here's what I'd propose:

1. Support parsing the date from files named something like `yyyy-mm-dd-slug.md`. I suspect this won't be easy to generalize, so a lume option to provide a date parsing regex.
2. Add a lume option to keep the date in the slug.

What do you think?

Either way, once I get my site completely migrated, I'd be happy to put together a "Next.js to lume" migration document on https://github.com/lumeland/lume.land :)
Comments.Author: thegedge
oscarotero

Comments.Body:
That's significantly simpler than what I had. Much appreciated!

I'm happy to consider that the way forward in this case, so feel free to close unless you think providing an option is useful, just not high priority.
Comments.Author: thegedge

Comments.Body:
Nice!
I close this, because the url function in `_data.ts` is the most flexible solution.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5W-rsy.Title: Allowing filters to use dynamic variables in Pug
I_kwDOEYAvFc5W-rsy.Author: pluiedev
I_kwDOEYAvFc5W-rsy.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Pug has [a filter system](https://pugjs.org/language/filters.html) that is currently hooked up to Lume's filters, that other template engines like Nunjucks also use. However, Pug limits filter inputs to plain text only, which is incredibly inconvenient for Lume users.

For example, the `date` filter added by [the Date plugin](https://lume.land/plugins/date/) can be used in Pug like this:
```pug
:date(pattern="HUMAN_DATETIME") 2022-11-22 7:00:30Z
```
However, this does not work with Pug variables (so something like `:date()= variable` does not work), and that is by design: 
> **Warning**
> **Filters are rendered at compile time.** This makes them fast, but it also means that they cannot support dynamic content or options.
>
> By default, compilation in the browser does not have access to JSTransformer-based filters, unless the JSTransformer modules are explicitly packed and made available through a CommonJS platform (such as Browserify or Webpack). In fact, the page you are reading right now uses Browserify to make the filters available in the browser.
>
> Templates pre-compiled on the server do not have this limitation.

As Lume precompiles templates as an SSG, I think we should lift this limitation and bring Pug in line with other template engines like Nunjucks.

Comments.Body:
Okay, I have to investigate the Pug API. I'm already precompiling the templates [see the code here](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/bb2232e9b645a2342d1dd8ea4f7a1e566e9f1864/plugins/pug.ts#L67-L69), so it should work for dynamic variables.

Any help here is very appreciated :)
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
@pluiedev I've tried to implement this but without success.
Do you know how to run Pug in server side to allow to use dynamic variables?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> @pluiedev I've tried to implement this but without success. Do you know how to run Pug in server side to allow to use dynamic variables?

I don't either, sadly — it looks like Pug is kinda poorly documented in that regard. We would have to ask the Pug maintainers I guess? No clue.
Comments.Author: pluiedev

Comments.Body: If you want to try, that's fine. But the last commit to the repo was almost 2 years ago and the maintainers don't reply the issues, so it's seems the project is abandoned.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> If you want to try, that's fine. But the last commit to the repo was almost 2 years ago and the maintainers don't reply the issues, so it's seems the project is abandoned.

Mhm yeah, which is a shame — I really like the terseness that Pug provides and I don't want to give it up just yet. I'll try to look into it myself then :eyes:
Comments.Author: pluiedev
pluiedev

Comments.Body: Sound good!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Okay so I did some more digging, and I think this is impossible without substantially modifying Pug, or using filters in another manner.

Basically, Pug evaluates filters before linking and code gen — there's no way to externally bypass filter evaluation, and there's no code in code gen (as far as I can tell) that supports filters, since `pug-code-gen` actually throws an error if it manages to find an unprocessed filter in the AST. Tl;dr if we were to support dynamic filters, we have to substantially alter the Pug compiler, which is admittedly too complicated for me.

However, I think we can just avoid using the builtin filter feature, and directly use interpolated, (un-)buffered code.
Instead of:
```pug
:date(pattern="HUMAN_DATETIME")= date
```
We can write
```pug
= filters.date(date, "HUMAN_DATETIME")
```

Which IMO actually works better, but we'd have to think about what to do with filters like `md`, which works great with just plain text.
Comments.Author: pluiedev

Comments.Body:
Nice!
Is it possible to have both? For example  `filters.md(value)` and `:md`?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> Nice! Is it possible to have both? For example `filters.md(value)` and `:md`?

It's possible yeah, you just have to inject filters into the page data before rendering, which makes them available to the template as a global variable. Gonna open a PR that does exactly that very soon 👍
Comments.Author: pluiedev

Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
alternatively, you can create [Lume components](https://lume.land/docs/core/components/):

- Store your component in `_components/index.tsx` and export it as default.
- In your pages, you have the `comp` variable with all components found:
```tsx
export default function ({ comp }) {
  return <comp.Index />
}
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> It looks like some React internals doesn't work in this way. This works fine:
> 
> change `index.tsx` to this:
> 
> ```tsx
> import { Index } from "./src/components/index.tsx";
> 
> export default function () {
>   return <Index />
> }
> ```
> 
> The `index` component doesn't return anything. You need to add a `return`.

Ah great. That fixed the issue. Thank you.
The return somehow got lost when I tried to make a small example.

`this.ctx` comes from Nunjucks filters.
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body: `url` use the `location` configuration as base. If the location is, for example, `https://mysite.com/subdirectory`, the prefix `/subdirectory` is prepended to the returned path. For example: `` returns ``. Does it work for you?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Not really, I'd still have to do one build for each location. Maybe a `relative_path` or `relative_url` filter?

But mostly, how do you feel about a `preprocess` plugin entry that happens before rendering?
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body:
Ok, got it.
Processors are executed after rendering. I think it's good because doing so, they are independent of the template engine used.

There is a `inline` processor [here](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/plugins/inline.js) to inline automatically all css, javascript, images, etc, by searching in the html code all elements with the `inline` attribute.

I think we can do something similar for your use case, a processor to search all elements using an absolute path in the href/src attributes and convert them to relative. In fact, I have done something like that in [another ssg I've created some years ago](https://github.com/oscarotero/zume/blob/master/src/plugins/urls.js)
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Ahnnn. That could totally work. Good point.

I made a [quick implementation of preprocess](https://github.com/fserb/lume/tree/preprocess). But I'd defer to you if you think it's a good idea for this to exist at all.
Comments.Author: fserb
fserb

Comments.Body:
I've created a plugin to convert all urls to relative: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/plugins/relative_urls.js

You only have to import to your `_config.js` file:

```js
import relative from "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lumeland/lume/master/plugins/relative_urls.js";

site.use(relative())
```

I have tested it and seems to work fine. Could you check it?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: It works perfectly. Thanks! :)
Comments.Author: fserb

MDU6SXNzdWU5MzA3MTUyMjc=.Title: Is _data.* read multiple times or is it a singleton?
MDU6SXNzdWU5MzA3MTUyMjc=.Author: shah
MDU6SXNzdWU5MzA3MTUyMjc=.Body:
I am using `_data.ts` to setup my common data across pages. One of my variables is _sourced_ from a PostgreSQL database so in `_data.ts` I connect to the database, read the content, and store in a variable that I then use across multiple pages. Everything is working well with respect to generating HTML.

What I noticed was that `_data.ts` seems to be sourced for each page (and is running my database connection for each page). Since the data is common to all pages, I assumed `_data.ts` would only be loaded once and then the variables are made available to all page templates but that does not seem to be the case.

So I'm wondering is `_data.*` _meant_ to sourced once for _each page_ or once _per build_? For now I've solved my problem by creating a singleton in `_data.ts` but wanted to report this issue to see what you thought.

Comments.Body:
`_data` files/folders are loaded only once.

You can see here a real example: https://github.com/tarugoconf/tarugo21/blob/master/_data/eventbrite.js#L12
This file get data from the eventbrite API and export it with the name `metrics`.

Maybe you are exporting a function that is executed for each page instead the result?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Ok great to know that, _by design_, `_data.ts` should only be loaded once. I'll try to figure out what I might have done to load it multiple times because for me (to get typesafety) I have to manually import `_data.ts` in my pages so it's possible I caused my own grief :-).
Comments.Author: shah

I_kwDOEYAvFc49SDAl.Title: Make quiet even more quiet
I_kwDOEYAvFc49SDAl.Author: x80486
I_kwDOEYAvFc49SDAl.Body:
I'm running Arch Linux, Deno `1.15.1`, and Lume `v1.1.1`:

```shell
[x80486@uplink:~/lume-template]$ uname -a 
Linux uplink 5.10.72-1-lts #1 SMP Sat, 09 Oct 2021 17:34:39 +0000 x86_64 GNU/Linux
[x80486@uplink:~/lume-template]$ deno --version 
deno 1.15.1 (release, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu)
v8 9.5.172.19
typescript 4.4.2
[x80486@uplink:~/lume-template]$ lume --version 
v1.1.1
```

I'm building a sample project with `lume --quiet`, but still, there is a lot of output in the console:

```shell
[x80486@uplink:~/lume-template]$ lume --quiet 
Download https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.1.1/cli.ts
Download https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.1.1/cli/build.ts
Download https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.1.1/cli/init.ts
Download https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.1.1/cli/run.ts
Download https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.1.1/cli/upgrade.ts
Download https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.1.1/cli/utils.ts
Download https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.1.1/deps/cliffy.ts
Download https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.1.1/core/utils.ts
Download https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.1.1/deps/colors.ts
Download https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.1.1/cli/serve.ts
... many many more!
```

I'm assuming that with `--quiet` Lume will only show warnings and/or errors, which is what I'm expecting with this. So is it possible to make `--quiet` to remove those `Download ...` statements as well?

I_kwDOEYAvFc5PezKh.Title: Remove CLI interface
I_kwDOEYAvFc5PezKh.Author: oscarotero
I_kwDOEYAvFc5PezKh.Body:
For v2.0.0 I'm thinking of removing the possibility of installing Lume with `deno run -A https://deno.land/x/lume/install.ts`.

There are some reasons for this:
- Deno install doesn't work in all cases and requires to update the `PATH`.
- It's more clear and portable to update Lume by editing the `import_map.json` file. It allows to have different projects using different versions of Lume.
- Deno tasks is a better way to run Lume.
- This removes completely the bug of having two different versions of Lume (one installed globally and other different in the import_map.json).

The new workflow could be the following:

- Init a new project with `deno run -A https://deno.land/x/lume/init.ts`. This script is like `lume init`, creating the _config.ts, import_map.json and deno.json files.
- The task `deno task lume` is the main entry for all Lume comands. For example:
  - `deno task lume upgrade` To upgrade Lume (the import_map.json file)
  - `deno task lume vendor` To vendor the project.
  -  `deno task lume` To build the project
  -  `deno task lume -s` To build the project and start a server
  - etc
- The tasks `deno task build` and `deno task serve` can be keept, as an alias of `deno task lume` and `deno task lume -s` (as they are already).

The only drawback is that all projects must be initiated. It's not allowed to run Lume without the `_config.ts` file as already. But I don't think there are many cases in which the default config is enough and having a explicit `_config` file is always better.

Comments.Body: To me, this creates another step for getting set-up. I vote that the CLI stays. It could be less dominant, less powerful even, but it can provide a better experience for developers.
Comments.Author: binyamin

Comments.Body:
@binyamin Ok, I think we can combine the best of the two worlds. I've created Lume CLI as a separated module.
Here's more info: https://lume.land/blog/posts/lume-cli/
Comments.Author: oscarotero

MDU6SXNzdWU3NTM0MzIyODA=.Title: Replace denopkg?

I'd suggest using https://www.jsdelivr.com/ instead, as it caches and serves via cdn, which will be more efficient than simply downloading the raw source files from Github as denopkg does. (I tend to use statically.io, as it also handles GitLab as well as Github, but jsdelivr is well-established.)

Comments.Body: Ok, I didn't know jsdelivr. Thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Yes, you're right. I'll fix it.
Thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Fixed in v0.10.0
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5WlDjp.Title: Replace Page.isHtml with Page.contentType
I_kwDOEYAvFc5WlDjp.Author: Protonull
I_kwDOEYAvFc5WlDjp.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Just saw the [changelog](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/v1.13.0/CHANGELOG.md#1130---2022-11-16) which included the addition of `Page.isHtml`, which is great, but I wonder if a broader is-function might be more useful. Lume's docs mention the possibility of dynamically generated assets [here](https://lume.land/docs/core/processors/#create-or-remove-pages-dynamically), and those assets could be blobs like PDFs of other files, or text files, indeed like the example'd Javascript-map file. But what if it's an XML file like an RSS feed? Would that appear to Lume as a HTML file? Would that therefore impact how that file is rendered? I'm not sure tbh, but I do think either way there's enough of a reason to allow for more exhaustive check. You could even keep the `Page.isHtml` by having its value set via `Page.isHtml = Page.contentType === "text/html";` You could check the prefix of the content-type to know how to save the file: if the type is prefixed with `text/` then you know that you can safely use `Deno.writeTextFile()` instead of another method.

Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: @oscarotero What about markdown and nunjucks files, which _become_ HTML? I found this property very useful.
Comments.Author: binyamin

Comments.Body:
@binyamin Pages have two properties:
- `page.src`: Info about the source file (`page.src.ext` returns the extension, like `.md`, `.njk`, etc).
- `page.outputPath`: The output path of the page. For example `/about-me/index.html` (with prettyUrls enabled) or `/about-me.html` with prettyUrls disabled.

So, if you want to detect if a page will become to HTML, just do `page.outputPath.endsWith(".html")`.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Extensions {

I_kwDOEYAvFc4-dMY1.Title: Triggering update from beforeUpdate
I_kwDOEYAvFc4-dMY1.Author: fserb

Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: BTW, is there any specific reason to use a custom bundler? Have you try the bundler plugin?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Yeah. The Deno blunder is not even close to good enough for this (to be fair, it's not designed for this). I need inline source map, minification (and keeping top names, because of WebComponents), tree-shaking, backward compatibility js version support, etc. 

I'll try Asset/process version, I think I tried it before and failed, but I'll report back.
Comments.Author: fserb
fserb

Comments.Body: Well, technically now the bundles are re-running every time anything changes, it seems.
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body:
Yes, this is the expected behavior. 
The only difference between `build` and `update` is that update only reload the changed files and save the output files if they changed the content (using a hash [as you can see here](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/core/emitter.ts#L31)) but the process in the middle (preprocessor, render and processor) are executed to all pages.
This is because you never know how a change in a file can affect to other files.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Hi again, @fserb 
I just found your repo (https://github.com/fserb/bkc) and created a `esbuild` plugin for Lume using your code. You can take a look here. https://github.com/lumeland/experimental-plugins
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Oh cool. :)

Yeah, there are a few issues with the current plugin that we may want to solve:

1. as mentioned before, it would probably be better if we rebuild only if some set of extensions changed, as opposed to any file.
2. it may be useful to consider two use cases:
   a. when we want to esbuild every js/ts files in the tree and we have a clear way to define dependencies (like `node_modules` or something).
   b. when we have a well defined set of js/ts files as entry points and everything else is a dependency. 

What I currently do for b is: I have a `jslib` directory that I `site.ignore()` and leave all dependencies there.
It's kinda okey, but I wonder if there could be a way inside the plugin to handle those cases (like passing lists of matching globs either for entry points or dependencies).

I'm not sure if the current way Lume handles assets processing supports that. Do you think this is a reasonable request? If so, how could we do it?

I could try to do it over the weekend, but I'm not sure even if there's a way to solve either of those things at this point.

nit: we also probably want to remove `incremental: true` from the user options. But I think the overall way to deal with the args (and overwritting the build specific ones) was a great way to deal with it.
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body:
Ok, I understand.

The reason to rebuild the entire site after any change, instead of only some pages, is because it's very complex to know in advance the pages that will be affected by any file change. For example:

- A change in a css file can affects to all files that imports this file.
- The affected files can be `.css` files, but also other extensions that generate css files (for example, a njk file that outputs a css file).
- And if you use the `inline` plugin, that inlines assets like css, js, images, etc in the html documents, the change in the css file can affect to other html pages.

The only way I can think to have this is configuring directly by the user. For example, with an option like `site.updateContext([".js", ".ts"])` (or a better name), indicating that the ".js" and ".ts" extensions are independent of the other pages, meaning that:
- If a ".js" and/or ".ts" file changes, all ".js" and ".ts" files are regenerated but the other pages aren't.
- If any other extensions changes (like "njk" files), all pages of the site will be regenerated except ".js" and ".ts" files.

But this option should be configured manually by the user because, as said, it depends on many things like relations between pages, plugins installed, etc.

---

And about the second issue, there's a similar request here https://github.com/lumeland/lume/issues/53. But I'd like to avoid globs because it can affects to performance.
Perhaps, this comment https://github.com/lumeland/lume/issues/53#issuecomment-864572282 is a good solution: allow not only strings, but also functions that returns true/false, so you can do something like this:

```js
site.ignore((filename) => isIgnored(filename));
```

This could be a good solution for complex ignored conditions. Anyway, in your case I think the best solution is just ignore the folder (or rename the folder prefixing it with `_`).

Btw, can I add your site to the pages of examples? (https://lumeland.github.io/getting-started/examples/)
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
@fserb I just pushed some changes in this direction. The idea is the following:

You can configure scoped extensions that will be treated independently of the rest of the pages. For example:

```ts
site.scopedExtensions([".css"], [".js", ".ts"]);
```

Here we are defining two scopes (one for `.css` files, and other for `.js` and `.ts` files).
This means that if a `.css` file changes, only the `.css` files will be rebuild, but not the other pages. And if a `.js` or `.ts` file changes, only `.js` and `.ts` will be updated.
If any other page changes (ex: a `.njk` file), all the "unscoped" pages will be rebuild, but scoped extensions (`.css`, `.js` and `.ts`) won't.

To manage this, there's a new `Changes` class that you can see here: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/core/changes.ts. This class generates a function that will be used to filter the pages that will be updated.

I not sure if this explanation is clear enough, so let me know your thoughts. I'm also open to change the function name if there's a better name.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
This makes sense to me. And it does address the problem.

I wonder if it would make more sense to make those scopes more generic.  The reason I say this is that the two obvious dependency connection for any file is "my directory or any subdirectory" and "same extension". 

I know you've been trying to avoid globs for perf reasons on `copy`. But maybe in the case of changes is a more contained problem, as we only have to iterate over the changes, not all files?

Overall, I keep wondering if extensions is the right abstraction across the board on Lume, or if things would be cleaner and more powerful if everything was a regexp (and extensions and globs are just subset of Regexps).

Re: adding the file as an example, sure. I haven't made the site officially public yet, but go ahead. :)
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body:
Ok, fair enough.
I've renamed the function to `scopedUpdates` and now it accepts functions instead of extensions. This allows to use glob or any other method to validate a file path:

```js
site.scopedUpdates(
    (path) => path.endsWith(".css"), // A scope with all css files
    (path) => path.startsWith("/js/"), // A scope with all files inside "js" folder
)
```

And about the use of extensions instead of regexp, I have thought several times in a way to allow to define functions, but it's more complicated that it seems. The truth is that extensions are a very flexible and understandable format because it define which files must be loaded and how they will be processed:

- Lume needs to know the extension to build the url of a page. For example:
  - `/about-me.md` is converted to `/about-me/` url, removing the `.md` suffix
  - `/styles.css.njk` is converted to `/styles.css` url, removing the `.njk` suffix
  - `/styles.css.tmpl.js` is converted to `/styles.css` url, removing the `.tmpl.js` suffix
- It also needs to know the extension to know how to handle every file (md, njk, jsx, etc)

A regexp only returns `true` or `false` (well, it can return captured values too), but it's more tricky to work with it to define loaders because we need to know the suffix of the path that we have to remove and sometimes it's not too easy. For example `.tmpl.js` suffix is used to generate pages using javascript templates and uses the module loader, which is different to `.js` that is the format of client side javascript files that are loaded as text files.

In short, we need the extensions, even if we use regexp, glob or any other advanced way to load pages. And having the two things (extensions and glob/functions/etc) would make Lume more complicated and confusing (in addition to possible performance issues). I'm not entirely closed to implement that, but I have to think in the best way to do it so they can coexist harmoniously.
Comments.Author: oscarotero
fserb

Comments.Body:
I love to hear good ideas for Lume, so don't worry :)

Your idea of the `Format` type sounds great, but I'm not sure if it covers all use cases. For example:

- A format has a `loader` assigned, a function responsive to read the file content. You can see [the different loaders here](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/tree/master/core/loaders).
- There are two types of files: pages and data. The same format can have different loaders depending on the type. For example:
  - `.js` files can be loaded as text files, because they aren't executed, but bundled, minified, etc.
  - But javascript data files (like `_data.js` or `_data/site.js`) are loaded as javascript modules, because they need to be executed at the build time.
- Formats are used also to detect the template engine used to render the pages. For example:
  - The page `about-me.njk` uses the Nunjucks template engine.
  - A page with the variable `layout: main.pug` uses the Pug template engine to render the layout file in `_includes`.
  - A page with the variable `templateEngine: njk,md` will be rendered twice, first using Nunjucks and then markdown [see this](https://lumeland.github.io/core/multiple-template-engines/)
- Processors and preprocessors use also the extensions to determine if a file must be processed before/after rendering. Both extensions (src and dest) are used. Let's say we register a processor to minify `.css` files:
  - A sass file (`.scss`) that ouputs `.css` is processed, because the extension is present in the dest file.
  - A `.css` file is processed too because the extension is present in the src file.

File extensions can cover all these casuistry with a single api (just prefix the format at the end of the file name) and it works in both directions (you can register anything (loader, processor, etc) for all files of the same extension; and you can assign a extension to a individual file). In addition to that, you don't need to see the code in the `_config.js` file to find out how a file will be loaded and processed, because it's clear in the file extension that, for example, a `.md` file is a markdown file that will be loaded and render as markdown.

Your proposal could work as a low level API to handle this but I see some limitations, for example how to use `my format` (in your example) as a format for layouts stored in `_includes`? or for processors/preprocessors? The format priority to resolve conflicts sounds a bit hacky...

Anyway, I'll keep thinking about this and open for suggestions.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

MDU6SXNzdWU5MjY1NjkzNDI=.Title: lume server doesn't reset computed extraData
MDU6SXNzdWU5MjY1NjkzNDI=.Author: fserb
MDU6SXNzdWU5MjY1NjkzNDI=.Body:
I have a plugin that does a Table of Contents based on Headings (h1, h2, h3... instead of tags). For this, I process() each page and extract the headings. All is good and it works fine.

The problem comes when I run lume in server/watch mode.
In that case, there doesn't seem to be way to "reset" the TOC and not double it at every reload, since I need to keep it global to all pages.

If I try as a global list, it obviously doesn't get cleared.
But if I try as part of site.extraData, it also doesn't get cleared.

I think it might be overkill to have a `site.rebuild(callback)`, but it feels like this should have a proper solution? I tried using events, but `beforeBuild` is not called all the time and `beforeUpdate` only reload some files. My current solution is to find a way to update the TOC instead of adding to it.


(related, but not really, it would be really nice to have a `--watch` option that does only watch/rebuild and no HTTP server)

Comments.Body:
Can I see the plugin? I think the event you need it `beforeUpdate`, that is triggered just before every new rebuild, but maybe if I take a look to the plugin I can help you to find the best solution.

And about the `--watch` option, why do you need this? Do you want to use a different HTTP server or you don't need a server at all?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
For the --watch, yes. I have a nginx running that has HTTPS on my local machine.

It's also a bit annoying if I do my own file watch and rerun Lume, because i's a bit slow to redo everything (and copy files, etc) (like 1s as opposed to a 100-200ms).

(nevermind the original bug here)
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body:
> (nevermind the original bug here)

Ok, I just looked your plugin and have some thoughs, but if you already solved it, ok :)

And about the `--watch`, it's easy to implement so I'll add it in the next version.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
@oscarotero I've just started using `lume`, and I think I've hit a similar problem with a `node:process` import going through the esbuild plugin.

In my case, it seems to be coming from https://esm.sh/v128/async-validator@4.2.5/denonext/async-validator.mjs which is being pulled through some transitive dependencies.

The output says:
```
Caused by Error: Build failed with 1 error:
deno:https://esm.sh/v128/async-validator@4.2.5/denonext/async-validator.mjs:2:23:
ERROR: [plugin: lumeLoader] No such file or directory (os error 2): readfile 'node:process'
```
Comments.Author: lucab
oscarotero

Comments.Body:
@oscarotero thanks for the hint, indeed I completely overlooked that detail.

Starting from your observation , it seems that the esbuild plugin is requesting the Deno version of the module to esm.sh (see the `denonext` in the URL above).
In turn, that causes esm.sh to add a `deno:process` polyfill to the code. Requesting the `es2015` version instead does inject https://esm.sh/v128/node_process.js as the polyfill for browsers. 
See the difference between the following:
 * `denonext` -> https://esm.sh/v128/async-validator@4.2.5/denonext/async-validator.mjs
 * `es2015` -> https://esm.sh/v128/async-validator@4.2.5/es2015/async-validator.mjs (the import URL is buggy though)
Comments.Author: lucab
oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc6BbpLV.Title: Use local version of Decap CMS script instead of unpkg.com
I_kwDOEYAvFc6BbpLV.Author: ghost
I_kwDOEYAvFc6BbpLV.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Hi,
The wait time for unpkg.com, for decap_cms.js, can be long (a minute or more). This is annoying especially in local/dev mode, but it also sometimes affects my production deployment.

Would it be possible to make this configurable, so that the user can specify a local copy of the script if they want?

I'm having a hard time deciding how to do this, though. First I tried doing it just for my site by writing a processor that modifies the rendered `/admin/` page, and rewrites the `src` of the script to a local url. This could be encaspulated in a plugin wrapping (or coming after) the DecapCMS plugin, and it could have the desired url as an option. But this seems like a non-starter because it seems like `deno-dom` doesn't know about `document.scripts`?

So the next thing I thought of was to make the DecapCMS plugin configurable: add an option `localscript` or some such which the page builder would check here: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/d5ac50063d60979124ef732121a7426fc9fb436e/plugins/decap_cms.ts#L88  falling back to unpkg if undefined.

It feels a bit weird to me to make this an option, but I don't see how else to make something like this configurable since the url is hard-coded.

Would you:

a. be open to a pull request adding this option
b. have a suggestion for how else I could modify this?

Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
@oscarotero thanks! I was trying pretty much this, except I was doing `document.scripts`. Should have looked for more documentation first.

This works great. No changes to DecapCMS plugin needed :)

Yeah, I was confused with the slow load times from unpkg.com too. Maybe it was just wifi issue ...

Anyways a good illustration of what you can do with processors, I hope this issue helps someone else in the future.
Comments.Author: ghost

MDU6SXNzdWU4NTQxMjI2NTI=.Title: Date format does not behave as expected (off by one day)
MDU6SXNzdWU4NTQxMjI2NTI=.Author: kevinlang
MDU6SXNzdWU4NTQxMjI2NTI=.Body:
I have a template with the date specified as e.g., `2021-04-08`.

I would expect this formatting:
```
post.data.date | date('PPP')
```

To return:
```
April 8th, 2021
```

However, it returns:
```
April 7th, 2021
```

If I format with the `ATOM` format, I can see that the issue seems to be related to stripping off the timezone, or something.
```
post.data.date | date(`ATOM')
=> "2021-04-07T20:00:00-04:00"
```

(I'm in GMT-4)

Comments.Body: Mmm, interesting. Maybe we have to add an option to define a default timezone, or something like that.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
https://github.com/date-fns/date-fns/issues/489#issuecomment-570635228

Yeah, looks like this is a known issue. There are some workarounds in that thread, I linked to one above.
Comments.Author: kevinlang

Comments.Body:
Intead in the plugin, I've fixed when the date is detected: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/commit/be274c7ecc7877a18dcc2a8158009168a9f99968

It's not published yet, but could you test it and confirm if it works fine to you?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Unfortunately it does not seem to have fixed the issue. It is still off by a day.

Here is how I ran the latest changes, in case that is somehow the issue (I'm not too familiar with deno):
1. Cloned this repo to an adjacent folder
2. Ensured that the commit above was present in the clone
3. From the adjacent folder that had my site, I ran `deno run --unstable -A ../lume/cli.js --serv`
Comments.Author: kevinlang
oscarotero

Comments.Body: Got it. I did that just now and the issue looks fixed. Thanks again for your assistance! :smile:
Comments.Author: kevinlang

Comments.Body: Great. You can undo this change in your _config.js and use the latest stable version after releasing this.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> Mmm, interesting. Maybe we have to add an option to define a default timezone, or something like that.

The following seems to work:

```js
Deno.env.set("TZ", "Europe/Helsinki");
```

We could add an option to abstract that:

```js
const site = lume({
  timeZone: "Europe/Helsinki",
});
```

*****

There should also be a note in the docs to not use a time zone offset in date values. The following won’t work correctly after the commit be274c7ecc7877a18dcc2a8158009168a9f99968:

```njk
---
date: 2021-04-04 03:02:01 +03:00
templateEngine: njk,md
---

{{ date }}
```

On my machine (where the time zone is `Europe/Helsinki` and offset UTC+03:00 in DST), it outputs:

```
Sun Apr 04 2021 00:02:01 GMT+0300 (Eastern European Summer Time)
```

But without `+03:00` in the `date` field, it works correctly. Before the commit, it behaved on the contrary: correctly with a time zone offset and incorrectly without it.

That’s because the time zone offset defaults to UTC (`Z` = `+00:00`).
Comments.Author: valtlai

Comments.Body:
Thanks @valtlai You're absolutelly right, my commit was only a hack for this specific case, but it doesn't solve the problem.

I didn't know about `TZ` environment variable, and it looks a better solution because it will be applied to all `Date` instances created, not only to the `date` value.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Looks like `Deno.env.set("TZ", "Z");` does the job.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
@kevinlang Finally I've reverted this change because it's not the best way to solve this problem.

For now, you can add `Deno.env.set("TZ", "Z")` to your `_config.js` file.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:

I_kwDOEYAvFc58qj-k.Title: Parsing Exception: CSS selector query doesn't get attribute value
I_kwDOEYAvFc58qj-k.Author: ngdangtu-vn
I_kwDOEYAvFc58qj-k.Body: ### Version 2.0.3 ### Platform any ### What steps will reproduce the bug? The issue was submitted in discord: https://discord.com/channels/794537085641818124/1197989006789595156/1197989006789595156 1. Add `_data.yml` ```yml metas: title: "=title" description: "=summary" image: "$.feed-image" ``` 2. Add content that has this HTML element: `` ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? Always. ### What is the expected behavior? The reporter expects to have the `src` set in place of meta `og:image`. ### What do you see instead? Nothing. the behaviou is correct. But it doesn't make sense for users. ### Additional information The source of problem: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/main/core/utils/data_values.ts#L26 This is an UX bug. This happens due to the convention only return the `innerHTML`. If it is a void element, nothing will returns. The design needs improve or to be removed as it makes users confused. Need further discussion about this feature.

Comments.Body:
So far we have 3 cases:

1. Only need children text node, for instance `h1`, `p`
2. Only need specific attribute, for instance `img` with `[src]`
3. The element provides rich data to use: `a` with `[href]` and child node

I also think about the nested element. But I don't think we need to support it. Users should point it directly to the element contains the info.


My solution could break the meaning of selector query. For example we have:

1. `$.feed-image` will always get text node
2. `$.feed-image[src]` will always get value of `src` attribute

It is pretty close to selector query syntax but act different as all query return `HTMLElement`. Another problem is some user may go very creative by adding `[src^="https:"]`... But looks on bright side, this syntax ensures a stricter query.
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
I see the problem. The solution of allowing the attribute name is a good idea, but `[src]` is confusing because it's a valid selector. I'd use a different syntax. An idea: the [`attr()` function ](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/attr). For eaxmple: 

```
$.feed-image attr(src)
```

Other solution is to make it more automatic:

- If the selector is to get an URL, get the value from `href` or `src` attribute.
- If not, get the value from the element content.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> If the selector is to get an URL, get the value from href or src attribute.

I have the 3rd case where we wouldn't know what user may need:

> 3. The element provides rich data to use: a with [href] and child node

It could be child text node from the element or it could be href.


I don't mind the `attr()` css function. Mind if I do this PR?
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body: Sure! PR is very appreciated 👍
Comments.Author: oscarotero

I_kwDOEYAvFc5NdnBO.Title: Can't copy files with prefix `_`
I_kwDOEYAvFc5NdnBO.Author: fabon-f
I_kwDOEYAvFc5NdnBO.Body:
# Problem

I want to copy `_headers` and `_redirects` to dest directory and tried like this in `_config.js`.

```js
site.copy("_headers")
site.copy("_redirects")
```

But these files are just silently ignored.

To my understanding, files with prefix `_` are ignored by default. But in my opinion it is better that this default behavior can be overridden by explicit config.

# Workaround for now

Renaming files and use second argument of `site.copy` works properly.

```
site.copy("headers", "_headers")
```

Comments.Body: ok, than this can be closed. I see they fixed the bug you reported already - though it'll be a while before it's effective, as the next release is in March.
Comments.Author: probins

Comments.Body:
> Looks like `Deno.emit()` (like the old `Deno.bundle()`) cannot load a js/ts file passing the content, and resolve the imported modules automatically. If `sources` option is used, all imported scripts must be there. This makes this plugin a bit useless for lume.

Bundlers usually work from an entry point, or 'rootSpecifier', as Deno calls it. They then fetch all the imported modules, and all the modules they import ... Not sure how that fits with Lume. I think it would only be useful if all the imports were external.
Comments.Author: probins

Comments.Body: Yeah, the problem is that the main entry point may be a nunjuks file that generate a js file, or something like that. This is why I want to provide the content of the entry point and Deno.emit() resolve the other imports (something similar to what the postcss plugin does).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: ok, I see what you mean. With rollup, you can pipe the entry point contents from stdin. Don't know whether that works with `deno bundle`.
Comments.Author: probins

I_kwDOEYAvFc56qwwO.Title: Image transform does not work with all caps JPG extension
I_kwDOEYAvFc56qwwO.Author: RickCogley
I_kwDOEYAvFc56qwwO.Body: ### Version 2 ### Platform macOS, Netlify ### What steps will reproduce the bug? If you have something like: ``` ``` .... but upload an image with an extension of JPG in upper case, the transformation does not work. ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? It's consistent, and I found out in the nwtn repo. The plugin won't render the transforms if the original is whatever.JPG. It must be whatever.jpg. ### What is the expected behavior? Users will upload whatever image format they get, if they are uploading to media folder via decapCMS for instance. They won't follow a rule like "the extension must be lowercase". ### What do you see instead? I'd like the plugin to be forgiving, and simply work, even if someone uploads whateverimage.JPG with an all caps extension. ### Additional information I didn't try but, it may be possible to set in plugin settings? `[ ".jpg", ".jpeg", ".png" ]` I think the default should just allow the variants, because cameras still export files with JPG as capitals, and also there might be some dumb situation where it's something.Jpg though I've never seen it.

Comments.Body:
Okay, thank you!
The extension detection must be case insensitive. I'll fix it for Lume 2.0.2
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Fixed. You can test it upgrading lume with `deno task lume upgrade --dev`.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: thank you for the quick work!
Comments.Author: RickCogley

I_kwDOEYAvFc5sw-g0.Title: Add JSON-LD plugin
I_kwDOEYAvFc5sw-g0.Author: oscarotero
I_kwDOEYAvFc5sw-g0.Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

More info: https://jsonld.com/
It could use: https://www.npmjs.com/package/schema-dts
Inspo: https://github.com/codiume/orbit/tree/main/packages/astro-seo-schema

Comments.Body: Hi, can I do this? I think the best way is to modify the metas plugin, what do you think?
Comments.Author: shuaixr

Comments.Body:
Hey @shuaixr 
Sure, thanks for your help!!

I don't think we should include this in the metas plugin, because JSON-LD is too big and complex.
Take a look to [astro-seo-schema](https://www.npmjs.com/package/astro-seo-schema) and [astro-seo-meta](https://www.npmjs.com/package/astro-seo-meta), they are different packages and I think Lume plugins should separate them too.

JSON-LD is hard, so I'd like a plugin that help to create entities easily. Maybe using a library like https://www.npmjs.com/package/schema-dts could be nice because it provides types to autocomplete the values. But would like to investigate if we can do it even easier. Let's see this example:

```js
{
  '@type': 'Person',
  '@id': 'https://my.site/#alyssa',
  name: 'Alyssa P. Hacker',
  hasOccupation: {
    '@type': 'Occupation',
    name: 'LISP Hacker',
    qualifications: 'Knows LISP',
  },
  mainEntityOfPage: {'@id': 'https://my.site/about/#page'},
  subjectOf: {'@id': 'https://my.site/about/#page'},
},
```

It should handle the URLs of the pages. The URLs `https://my.site/#alyssa` and `https://my.site/about/#page` should be managed by Lume, so if the site is built with a different url (`deno task build --location https://other.site`) all these URLs should change. I imagine a script that search recursively by all `@id` and `url` keys that doesn't contain the domain and complete them. For example:

```js
{
  '@type': 'Person',
  '@id': '#alyssa',
  name: 'Alyssa P. Hacker',
  hasOccupation: {
    '@type': 'Occupation',
    name: 'LISP Hacker',
    qualifications: 'Knows LISP',
  },
  mainEntityOfPage: {'@id': '/about/#page'},
  subjectOf: {'@id': '/about/#page'},
},
```

It must allow aliases, in the same way as metas. For example:

```js
{
  '@type': 'Person',
  '@id': '#alyssa',
  name: '=user.name',
  hasOccupation: {
    '@type': 'Occupation',
    name: '=user.occupation.name',
    qualifications: '=user.occupation.qualifications',
  },
  mainEntityOfPage: {'@id': '/about/#page'},
  subjectOf: {'@id': '/about/#page'},
},
```

It should be able to show or hide entities if some values are empty. For example, if the user doesn't have occupation, instead of output the properties with empty values:

```js
{
  '@type': 'Person',
  '@id': 'https://my.site/#alyssa',
  name: 'Alyssa P. Hacker',
  hasOccupation: {
    '@type': 'Occupation',
    name: '',
    qualifications: '',
  },
  mainEntityOfPage: {'@id': 'https://my.site/about/#page'},
  subjectOf: {'@id': 'https://my.site/about/#page'},
},
```

It should omit this object:

```js
{
  '@type': 'Person',
  '@id': 'https://my.site/#alyssa',
  name: 'Alyssa P. Hacker',
  mainEntityOfPage: {'@id': 'https://my.site/about/#page'},
  subjectOf: {'@id': 'https://my.site/about/#page'},
},
```

It would be nice if the plugin checked the data structure, in order to detect missing properties, or not found relations between entities.

What do you think? I know it's complex, so we can start with the basics and improve it little by little.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: After closer look at the metas plugin, I think you're right. I'll open a draft pr soon
Comments.Author: shuaixr

I_kwDOEYAvFc5H2Um8.Title: Convert string dates to `Date` automatically
I_kwDOEYAvFc5H2Um8.Author: oscarotero
I_kwDOEYAvFc5H2Um8.Body:
Lume expects the `date` property to be a `Date` instance. In `yaml` this is converted automatically but in JavaScript this code fails:

```js
export const date = "2022-04-15";
```

because it needs to be:

```js
export const date = new Date("2022-04-15");
```

Lume should convert valid strings to `Date` here: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/core/page_loader.ts#L86

I_kwDOEYAvFc5U-lUy.Author: oscarotero
I_kwDOEYAvFc5U-lUy.Body: The special 404 page (configured at `server.page404`) should be ignored from the page list.

I_kwDOEYAvFc6DriPV.Title: page does not refresh when _components/XXX.tsx is modified
I_kwDOEYAvFc6DriPV.Author: iacore
I_kwDOEYAvFc6DriPV.Body:
### Version

v2.0.1

### Platform

Linux

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

- Enable TSX
- have a .tsx page reference a component in _components/
- load the .tsx page in browser
- edit the component

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

100%

### What is the expected behavior?

the page should refresh to new content

### What do you see instead?

The page is stuck. Manual refresh doesn't work. I have to restart lume to see the new page.

### Additional information

_No response_

Comments.Body:
Hi. Can I see the code of how you're using the component? In Lume, components are loaded automatically in the `comp` variable. Example: ```tsx export default function ({ comp}: Lume.Data ) { return

} ```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
this can be workaround using watch or hmr.

1. run `echo 'import "lume/cli.ts"' > _main.ts` on terminal.
2. change tasks in `deno.json` to following:

```diff
 	"tasks": {
-		"lume": "echo \"import 'lume/cli.ts'\" | deno run -A -",
-		"build": "deno task lume",
-		"serve": "deno task lume -s"
+		"build": "deno run -A _main.ts",
+		"serve": "deno run -A --unstable-hmr _main.ts -s"
 	},
```

it should work like following:

```
$ deno task serve
Task serve deno run -A --unstable-hmr _main.ts -s
HMR Process started.
Loading config file file:///home/scarf/repo/etc/esbuild-issue/_config.ts
🔥 /main.js <- /main.tsx
🍾 Site built into ./_site
  1 files generated in 0.01 seconds
  Server started at:
  http://localhost:3000/ (local)
  http://192.168.1.90:3000/ (network)
Changes detected:
- /_config.ts
HMR Replaced changed module file:///home/scarf/repo/etc/esbuild-issue/_config.ts

```
Comments.Author: scarf005

Comments.Body:
> In Lume, components are loaded automatically in the `comp` variable. Example:

I use `import Component from '../Component.tsx'`.

I use a lot of functions between files too.

```
import { generate } from "./all.atom.xml.tsx"
```
Comments.Author: iacore

Comments.Body:
The problem is Deno caches the imported modules and there's no way to invalidate the cache to refresh the modules.

In Lume, this limitation is solved loading the file with a hash (for example, `import foo from "./foo.ts#hash123"`). Components are loaded in this way so if a file changes, Lume reload the file with a different hash.

Deno added recently a way to do hot module replacement (https://deno.com/blog/v1.38#hot-module-replacement) but it's still unstable and doesn't work well with Lume.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Related: https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/8327
Comments.Author: iacore

Comments.Body:
> this can be workaround using watch or hmr.
> 
>     1. run `echo 'import "lume/cli.ts"' > _main.ts` on terminal.
> 
>     2. change tasks in `deno.json` to following:

I totally missed this... now my site is mostly stable I don't have a easy way to test this. Hopefully someone else will find this useful.

The terminal output this: ```sh Changes detected: - /scripts/feature-flags.page.tsx Changes sent to the browser ``` But the brother never reloads that page. Even though I can see: Command run: ```sh deno task lume -sw ```

Comments.Body:
https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/972d55cae716a8acd847afc6db949ae4a979ac0d/middlewares/reload_client.js#L112-L115

looks like it's because live reload client doesn't handle extension other than js.

It's been well over a year since this issue was opened, so I'd it's time to shed some light on it once more. Notably, Deno's HMR flag(s?) aren't considered unstable anymore, so perhaps the `--hmr` or `--watch-hmr ` flag would be able to solve this? 

- [deno run --watch-hmr](https://docs.deno.com/runtime/getting_started/command_line_interface/#hot-module-replacement-mode )

[deno serve --hmr](https://docs.deno.com/runtime/reference/cli/run/#file-watching-options-hmr)

oscarotero

How about `Deno.watchFs`? 

https://docs.deno.com/examples/watching_files/
https://docs.deno.com/examples/file_system_events_tutorial/
