PR_kwDOEYAvFc5SsO_-.Author: kwaa

Comments.Body:
Thanks, but I'm not sure this is a good idea.

TOML format is often used for configuration stuff by many deployment services, like `netlify.toml`, `fly.toml`, etc. If this format is enabled by default, all sites using these configuration files will start to have new pages with these files.

I think it's better to have it disabled and only enable it when really need it, (using `site.ignore` if you have files that need to be ignored).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> TOML format is often used for configuration stuff by many deployment services, like `netlify.toml`, `fly.toml`, etc. If this format is enabled by default, all sites using these configuration files will start to have new pages with these files.

JSON and YAML are also used for configuration of many deployment services (e.g. `vercel.json`, `.github/workflows/*.yml`)

Since there is some difficulty in using deno with these services (netlify, vercel), I think it should be assumed that anyone using them knows how to set `site.ignore` or `site.options.src`.

Considering that they are both powered by Deno std, I thought it would be best to make them work out of the box.
Comments.Author: kwaa

Comments.Body:
`vercel.json` is not deteted by lume (because the extension to create pages from json on Lume is `.tmpl.json` and github actions are on `.github` folder (ignored by Lume because it starts with a dot.

Anyway, the main problem, right now is that json and yaml are formats enabled by default from the very first version of Lume. Supporting for TOML by default is a good idea, but I would wait to Lume 2.0, that will include some breaking changes.

I'm going to put this PR on hold, and will merge it for Lume 2.0.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: OK, pls ping me when Lume 2.0 development starts.
Comments.Author: kwaa

Comments.Body:
Sure. Thanks for understanding!

My idea is try to match the release of Deno 2.0
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Merged to v2 branch.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

PR_kwDOEYAvFc5isjJH.Title: Allow to use this in the filters
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5isjJH.Body:
## Description Some templates engines (Vento, nunjucks, liquid) expose the current context in the `this` variable of filters. This allows to get page data without passing explicity throught arguments. This change creates the `HelperThis` type to access to this data. Example: ```js site.filter("translate", function (key) { const lang = this?.data.lang; return myTranslateFunction(key, lang); }); ``` The language is not passed explicity to the function. Instead of that, it's got from the `lang` variable of the page. It allows to use the filter in any nunjucks/liquid page: ```html --- lang: gl ---

{{ "title" |> translate }}

``` ## Related Issues This allows to use the `date` filter (or other filters that depends on the page content, like the language) in a more ergonomic way. ### Check List - [x] Have you read the [CODE OF CONDUCT](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) - [x] Have you read the document [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) - [x] One pull request per feature. If you want to do more than one thing, send multiple pull request. - [x] Write tests. - [x] Run deno `fmt` to fix the code format before commit. - [ ] Document any change in the `CHANGELOG.md`.

PR_kwDOEYAvFc4xccIQ.Title: Fix: Assets cannot be rendered with chained extension
PR_kwDOEYAvFc4xccIQ.Body:
## Describe the bug
You cannot get "hydration.client.tsx" rendered with a `_config.ts` below.
```ts
site.loadPages([".tsx"], moduleLoader, tsxEngine)
  .loadAssets([".client.tsx"], clientSideScriptLoader)
  .process([".client.tsx"], (page) => page.dest.ext = ".js")
```

## Background
The problem on code which directory causes the bug is that pages are always preferred over assets.
https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/f02e6832db31cc55f6da96fcab39213e50c17411/core/source.ts#L212-L213

1d67763c198972c202aed359f1f21e9f0f076aca fixes this, however, another problem occurrs.
In the current implementation, `Site.renderer.renderPages()` renders everything with `Site.renderer.engines.render()`. So when you have a matching extension in the engines, the file will be rendered by the corresponding engine even if the file is actually an asset, not a page.
https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/f02e6832db31cc55f6da96fcab39213e50c17411/core/renderer.ts#L88-L97 

To prevent this, we have to use `if` clause not to execute `engines.render()`for assets. However, we have no knowledge about the file in problem is an asset or a page on the context of `Site.renderer`.
So I implemented `Site.assets` array to store assets separately from `Site.pages`. To make it more natural, I also implemented a new class `Asset`, which is an analogue of `Page`.
PR_kwDOEYAvFc4xccIQ.Author: aiotter

Comments.Body:
Sorry I forgot to run the test in my local environment.
I'll fix it tomorrow (maybe), but can you take a glance at my code beforehand?
If you don't want this kind of implementation, I'll close the PR and follow your way.
Comments.Author: aiotter

Comments.Body:
Hi, @aiotter 
Thanks for this. I understand the problem and clearly there's something that needs to be fixed.
I have to think more deeply about this solution, because I'm not convinced about separating assets and pages as different things.
There are cases in which a page is used to generate an asset (example: `styles.css.njk` is a nunjucks file that output a css file). The only difference of pages and assets is that pages use to output html code, and assets don't, so the pages output include the html extension (`page.njk -> page/index.html`) and assets maintain the extension of the input file (`styles.css -> styles.css`).

To handle simple and chained extensions, there's the [`Extension` class](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/core/extensions.ts). The problem here is that multiple Extensions instances are used (one for pages, other for assets etc), so they don't know each other. Maybe if we have a single `Extensions` instance that contain everything, the problem could be fixed (I'm thinking out loud). I imagine something like this:

```ts
const info = extensions.get(".tsx");
info.pageLoader; // Return the page loader for this extension or undefined
info.assetLoader; // Return the asset loader for this extension or undefined
info.dataLoader; // Return the data loader for this extension or undefined

const info2 = extensions.get(".client.tsx");
// info2 would have different values as info.
```

FYI, I have this with the Lume architecture, that includes all properties of the `Site` instance and their relationship: https://miro.com/app/board/uXjVOb60Gx8=/
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> I'm not convinced about separating assets and pages as different things

I know this can be your general idea, because there is only one class `Page` for representing generated resource.
However, the API of Lume is different. You have two different API for registering pages and assets. `Site.loadPages` with `PageLoader` and `Site.loadAssets` with `AssetLoader`. This confused me.

Don't you think it's better to make `Site.loadPages` take another argument representing to which extension the file will be rendered, like `site.loadPages([".client.tsx"], clientSideScriptLoader, , ".js")`?
You can leave `Site.loatAssets` to be aliased to `Site.loadPages` with the last argument set to `null`.
This makes clear for everyone that the asset is a special type of page, and extend flexibility of Lume by allowing users to generate page assets other than HTML using `Engine`s. For example, you can even create PHP with liquid engine. And this change of UI doesn't break the backward compatibility at all.

Edit: Or even better to rename current "page"-related functions and variables into "resource", and make `site.loadPages` to be aliased to `site.loadResources`? I feel it weird to generate `.js` files using `site.loadPages`. It's not like page.
Comments.Author: aiotter

Comments.Body:
Yes, I like it.
The API is different because I think it's more clear and easy for users to have a `loadPages` / `loadAssets` than a unique `loadResources` with a lot of options. But I like the idea of making aliases. In fact the `filter` function is an alias of `helper`. Let me try some things and I'll let you know.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Hi, again. I just commit the first version of the new `ResourceLoader` class that merges `PageLoader` and `AssetLoader`.
Take a look at #166 and let me know if it works for you.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
This PR is no needed, because this issue was fixed in #166, so I close this.
Thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

PR_kwDOEYAvFc5_K-FP.Title: Fix esbuild tests
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5_K-FP.Body:
<!--Please read the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)-->

## Description

The Test Snapshot `esbuild plugin with JSX 3` contains:
- The actual output JS file/page but with a `data.url` of `/_.._/esbuild_jsx/main.js` and no `src` connection
- A source file `/main.jsx` without any transformations and with a `src` connection

This is not the desired output. It should look something like this:
- Only one `/main.js` bundle with a `src` connection

This happens because the esbuild plugin (accidentally ?) modifies their plugin defaults:
```js
const options = merge(defaults, userOptions);
// ...
options.options.absWorkingDir ||= site.src();
const basePath = options.options.absWorkingDir;
```

This way the `absWorkingDir` option leaks from the first esbuild test to the other tests. All the esbuild tests use `test/assets/esbuild` as src -- except the `esbuild plugin with JSX` test which uses the `test/assets/esbuild_jsx` dir. However, because of the option leak we configure esbuild with a `absWorkingDir` of `test/assets/esbuild` instead of `test/assets/esbuild_jsx`. Mapping of esbuild output files fails and the output JS is added as a chunk. The `/_.._/` part seems to be an "escaped" `/../`.

You can verify this by running all the esbuild tests (without the PR patch):
`deno task test ./tests/esbuild.test.ts` - they should all pass
And then run only the `esbuild plugin with JSX` test (without the PR patch):
`deno task test ./tests/esbuild.test.ts --filter '/^esbuild plugin with JSX$/'` - this should fail and the diff should display the changes mentioned above (actual / desired output)

This PR changes the way the `absWorkingDir` option is handled to not modify the plugin defaults. I have also updated the `esbuild plugin with JSX 3` snapshot to reflect the desired data.

This should not have any impact on actual users and does not change the output files of actual users. This bug only occurs when using the esbuild plugin multiple times but with a different `site.src`.

## Related Issues

None

### Check List

- [x] Have you read the
      [CODE OF CONDUCT](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [x] Have you read the document
      [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)
  - [x] One pull request per feature. If you want to do more than one thing,
        send multiple pull request.
  - [x] Write tests.
  - [x] Run deno `fmt` to fix the code format before commit.
  - [x] Document any change in the `CHANGELOG.md`.
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5_K-FP.Author: into-the-v0id

Comments.Body:
Good catch!
I was expecting the `merge()` function to return a completely new object, but the property `options.options` is a reference to the defaults property, so hence the bug.

Changing `merge()` function to ensure the object is completely cloned (without references) is not easy (and not always possible for some potential values, like functions), so your fix is fine.
Thanks!!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

PR_kwDOEYAvFc5LzrA_.Title: Allow filename dates to be followed by either an underscore or hyphen
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5LzrA_.Body:
## Description

Many (most?) static site generators use a filename format where any date time is separated from the post slug using a hyphen.

[Jekyll](https://jekyllrb.com/docs/posts/#creating-posts):

> To create a post, add a file to your `_posts` directory with the following format:
>
> ```
> YEAR-MONTH-DAY-title.MARKUP
> ```

[Hexo](https://hexo.io/docs/writing#Filename):

> By default, Hexo uses the post title as its filename. You can edit the `new_post_name` setting in `_config.yml` to change the default filename. For example, `:year-:month-:day-:title.md` will prefix filenames with the post creation date. 

[Eleventy](https://www.11ty.dev/docs/dates/):

> If a date key is omitted from the file, we then look for a `YYYY-MM-DD` format anywhere in the file path (even folders).

(I can’t find a specific documented example of the full format but in my own testing, I note that using an underscore between the date and slug doesn’t work, and Eleventy will assume that the date is part of the slug.)

This means that, if you want to port an existing site over to Lume, you will need to update post filenames, which is one more thing to think about.

By parsing filenames using an explicit regex that matches the documented formats (dates `yyyy-mm-dd` or datetimes: `yyyy-mm-dd-hh-ii-ss`), dates can be followed by either an underscore (`_`) or a hyphen (`-`).

### Check List

- [x] Have you read the
      [CODE OF CONDUCT](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [x] Have you read the document
      [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)
  - [x] One pull request per feature. If you want to do more than one thing,
        send multiple pull request.
  - [x] Write tests.
  - [x] Run deno `fmt` to fix the code format before commit.
  - [x] Document any change in the `CHANGELOG.md`.
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5LzrA_.Author: paulrobertlloyd

Comments.Body: Hmmm, actually looking at the snapshot diff, the time is no included in a post’s date (I misread my local diff, I thought it was improving this and adding a time). Will tweak this some more…
Comments.Author: paulrobertlloyd

Comments.Body: Okay, so have fixed up the regex to not break the tests. Though, thinking about this some more, I’m guessing the original regex is inexact for speed? Will a stricter regex here impact overall build times?
Comments.Author: paulrobertlloyd
oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Ah, interesting, wasn’t aware of the ordering background.

I agree, 2 very similar regexes seems wasteful and expensive – that there were 2 is what had me concerned about performance. Consolidating into 1 and providing values directly to the `createDate` function makes a lot of sense.

Will update this PR with such a change. 👍
Comments.Author: paulrobertlloyd

Comments.Body:
Have updated the implementation so that:

* only one regex is needed
* uses named capture groups to help parse the regex
* uses types for values sent to `createDate` function

Also:

* updates one of the file fixtures so that it includes the date separated by a hyphen
* updates resulting snapshots
Comments.Author: paulrobertlloyd

Comments.Body: Awesome. Thanks!

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTUyMTQzMDEy.Title: Add plugin for Terser
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTUyMTQzMDEy.Body: As discussed. If you're happy with this, I can write some docs, mainly about the default options and source maps. Obviously, options that use the filesystem won't work.
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTUyMTQzMDEy.Author: probins

Comments.Body: hold on! There seems to be an error in the source map links. Will have to investigate.
Comments.Author: probins

Comments.Body: ok. The problem was that I was using `src.path` and not filename in the sourcemap url. The path assumes that cwd is the base address. This will work if the files are deployed at the server base url, but if not (for example, `server/user/...`), then the urls will not be found. Better to use the actual filename, i.e. the sourcemap is always in the same directory as the file.
Comments.Author: probins

Comments.Body:
It looks good to me.
FYI, 
- All pages and files have the `url` data value, that is generated automatically by lume. So if you want to get the url of a page/file, just need `page.data.url`.
- If the real url is under a subdirectory, you can use the function `site.url(page.data.url)`, that returns the url including the basePath if exists.
The problem of defining the filename with the `file.src` data, is that `file.src` may not be the same as `file.dest`. For example, you could create a js file with a frontmatter to define a different output file:

```js
---
permalink: js/my-script.js
---

// Here's the js code
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
ok. I've changed `file.src` to `file.dest` as suggested. And have also changed to use std `path.basename` to get the name for the sourcemap link - I think that's easier to read, and as Lume is importing `path` anyway, there's no extra overhead.

Terser has various options for sourcemaps, but I think this plugin should keep to something simple. If people want to use the different options, they can run Terser directly.
Comments.Author: probins

Comments.Body: fixes #29

PR_kwDOEYAvFc48W1_0.Title: Reject invalid url slugs
PR_kwDOEYAvFc48W1_0.Body:
## What

Adds a check in `renderer` to make sure that the `url` provided is of a valid type. Throws an error if it is not.

## Why

Previously, the checks were structured so that if an invalid type was provided (e.g. a `number` or `array`) then the rendered would 'fail silently', falling back onto the default url scheme without warning the user. (Closes #222.)

## Testing

I've checked it locally against a few different values. Is this something you'd like unit tests for? I haven't written any here because I'm not familiar with the testing library etc., but I'm happy to spend some time trying to work it out if you'd like a test for it.

PR_kwDOEYAvFc4-9jIo.Title: Metas plugin: allow for relative "./" path in og:image and og:icon URL
PR_kwDOEYAvFc4-9jIo.Body:
Fixes #255

Now you can set og:image or og:icon with a relative path, in case your assets are in the page directory.
Example:
This directory structure
```
posts/
  my-first-post/
    index.md
    hello-world.png
  my-second-post/
    index.md
    hola-mundo.png
  ...
```
will have the following meta in the `index.md` front matter
```yaml
metas
  title: "Hello world"
  description: "My first post"
  image: "./hello-world.png"
```

PR_kwDOEYAvFc5_mKz9.Title: Improve code_highlight configuration options type to be Partial
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5_mKz9.Body:
<!--Please read the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)-->

## Description
Make it so users of the Highlight Code Plugin don't have to pass all options to the configure wrapper for highlightjs.
The object is only merged with the `defaults` and then passed to [PublicApi::configure](https://highlightjs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#configure) which has a type signature itself of:
`configure: (options: Partial<HLJSOptions>) => void`
https://github.com/highlightjs/highlight.js/blob/4e485f222e8c604b92937870414cf401e9dff31f/types/index.d.ts#L38

## Related Issues
Example: I wanted to have the `pre code.mermaid-language` processed by another plugin so i wanted to add:
```
site.use(codeHighlight({
    options: {
        noHighlightRe: /^mermaid$/i
    }
}));
```
Deno-ts then sais:

> Type '{ noHighlightRe: RegExp; }' is missing the following properties from type 'Omit<HLJSOptions, "__emitter">': languageDetectRe, classPrefix, cssSelector`

PR_kwDOEYAvFc4yw8WS.Title: Prevent overwriting the output files
PR_kwDOEYAvFc4yw8WS.Body:
Imagine you accidentally create two tags `blog` and `Blog`, and let Lume generate `_site/tags/blog/index.html` and `_site/tags/Blog/index.html` respectively (this is exactly what I did).
Case-insensitive filesystems regard those two files as the same, allowing Lume to overwrite one with the other.

This PR warns user that there already exist a generated file.
PR_kwDOEYAvFc4yw8WS.Author: aiotter

Comments.Body: Note that I used the experimental api `Deno.flock` in order to make it sure that no other asynchronous functions are writing to the file while one function reads the file stat and then writes to the file.
Comments.Author: aiotter

Comments.Body:
OMG I didn't realise that Lume does overwrite the current file in `_site` when in `serve` mode.
How can we allow overwriting under that situation?
Comments.Author: aiotter
oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Note that `Writer` class has already a `#hashes` private map to prevent write unchanged pages in the filesystem https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/core/writer.ts#L23.
Maybe we could reuse this and add the duplicated page check.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Pathname of URL is case sensitive. If your file system allows `_site/tags/Blog` and `_site/tags/blog` coexist, therefore `https://example.com/tags/blog` and `https://example.com/tags/Blog` can be served at the same time.
In my understanding there is no API in Deno to get file system information, so I don't think it's possible to detect this type of duplication before actually saving them.

How about defining a property in Page object which represents the page is already built or not yet?
Comments.Author: aiotter

Comments.Body: Hmm? I wrote something strange. It does not work. Please forget about the suggestion  at the end of my last message🤔
Comments.Author: aiotter

Comments.Body: We can pool the file inode number we actually wrote to. This easily enables us to detect the duplication. But this is only possible on Unix-like system.
Comments.Author: aiotter

Comments.Body:
In order to make the site working consistently on any platform, I'd consider duplication detection case insensitive, meaning that `/tags/blog/` and `/tags/Blogs/` are the same page (even in unix-like system). If you see the code of the example above, the page id is always lowercase.

I also think that this detection should only show a warning, but the output file should be overwritten, so it's up to the user to fix it or not.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: FYI, eleventy has the same feature: https://github.com/11ty/eleventy/issues/562
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I meant it's difficult to support case sensitive output while prohibiting it in case sensitive file systems. It's not a problem if you warn duplication detected in case insensitively for all file systems.

I'll modify the commit. Part of this PR is better to be applied to fix the problem of the output file being mixture of duplicated two input content. This happens because writing to `_site` is done in race condition without locking the file.
Comments.Author: aiotter

Comments.Body:
Here it is!
You will see `_site/tags/Blog/index.html` as a mixture of the html for `blog` and `Blog`. This is because `Deno.writeTextFile` or `Deno.writeFile` can process the same file at the same time.
This commit will lock the file before truncate to make sure that writing out won't happen concurrently.
Comments.Author: aiotter

Comments.Body:
Nice, thank you!
I'll implement also a warning to show in these cases.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

PR_kwDOEYAvFc4xdMv5.Title: Merge pageLoader and assetsLoader into resourceLoader
PR_kwDOEYAvFc4xdMv5.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Hi, I'm surprised to your quick rewrite.
I have a suggestion for this code. Can I open another PR against `resource-loader` branch so that you'll see the diff easier?
Comments.Author: aiotter

Comments.Body:
Yes, sure!
Thanks
Comments.Author: oscarotero
aiotter

Comments.Body:
@aiotter Ok, this is because Lume render all pages (.tsx and .client.tsx) as .tsx pages (due the same issue as we had with pages and assets).

I've fixed it including the engine in the `Resource` interface (now renamed to `Extension`) https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/5eeb2d72199fa653f511d3885f4117d4dbb1c903/core.ts#L68-L72

This means that now the .tsx extension will have a loader and a engine different to .client.tsx.

The more I think about it, the more I think to extend this interface, to contain everything related with a extension (not only the page loader and engine, but also data loader, component loader, processors/preprocessors, etc). And maybe rename it to `Format`.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Now it works!

> The more I think about it, the more I think to extend this interface, to contain everything related with a extension (not only the page loader and engine, but also data loader, component loader, processors/preprocessors, etc). And maybe rename it to Format.

Nice! I feel it very natural and clear. And that's near to what I wanted to suggest. (So no need of another PR anymore)

I don't like that `ResourceType`. Assets and pages can be treated as the same. The only difference is extension conversion (pages can lack the Engine, so the absence of Engine is not specific to assets).
So how about this one?

```ts
interface Format {
  extension: {from: string[], to: string},
  loader: ResourceLoader,
  engine?: Engine,
}
```


Or even better, you can include extension conversion in `Engine`. In this case `Engine` may be better to be renamed to `Modifier`.
`Modifier` is responsible for modifying the `Resource` object, like converting the content from markdown to HTML (currently `ResourceLoader` is responsible for this), applying template engine like nunjucks, and changing the extension from `.md` to `.html`.

```ts
interface Format {
  extension: string[],
  loader: ResourceLoader,
  modifiers?: Modifier[],  // array is for multiple modifier
}

interface Modifier {
  modify(resource: Resource): Promise<void>;
  modifySync(resource: Resource): void;  // If this is not needed, Modifier can be implemented as a simple function
  // addHelper is not needed anymore; you can do it inside Modifier.modify()
}
```

You can implement `Engine` interface which extends `Modifier`, and in this interface you may have helpers, components, or other utilities.

This API fits for your basic concept of 'everything is a plugin'. I like this simple and clear concept, and that is why I choose to use Lume. With this api, now everything is processed by a modifier. `Engine`s are general modifiers. Processors are modifiers `push`ed to `Format.modifiers`, and preprocessors are modifiers `unshift`ed to it.
You can make `Site.loadXXXs`, `Site.process`, and `Site.preprocess` to be aliased to new API for managing site modifiers, if backward compatibility matters. The new APIs may be like a pair of `Site.modify(extensions).append(...modifiers)` and `Site.modify(extensions).prepend(...modifiers)`. Or make it simpler like `Site.modifier[extension].push(modifier)`. I'm not sure of the good UI. There must be a better way...
Comments.Author: aiotter

Comments.Body:
@aiotter thanks for your suggestions. They looks great, and clearly this is the direction I want to take: simple and easy code providing high flexibility and freedom.

The behavior of processors and preprocessors is a bit different to engines because they must be executed in the same order they where declared. For example, if you want to use a processor to transform css (like postcss) and other to minify the css code, the first processor must run **all pages** before start running the second processor. This is why they are saved in a different way because order matters here. In the render engines, the order doesn't no matter (unless you want to specify a specific order with `renderOrder` variable).

The idea of moving the different treatment of html pages and asset pages looks fine, but the difference is more than just replace the extension with `.html`:
- Depending of the configuration (`prettyUrls`), the HTML pages can simply replace the extension to `.html`, or add the `/index.html` suffix.
- The HTML pages also detects the `date` variable from the filename (like `2020-01-03_filename.md`) and remove this part to generate the final name. This behavior is not applied to assets (although it could).

I want to think about this before make more changes because I don't want to break backward compatibility. I've created a new class `Formats` to replace the old `Extensions` and extended the `Format` interface with all values needed by loaders and engines, so now the extensions are centralized in one place:

https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/5228c8a5786b18a08f4442fb9a5f5ffd00927513/core/formats.ts#L7-L16

I think this is enough for now but will continue working to make Lume more simple and flexible, like you are suggesting in next versions. Thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTYzMzQzNDI4.Title: Make CLI Commands more independent
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTYzMzQzNDI4.Body:
**Note:** this PR is to the `cli-refactor` branch, not master.

# What Changed

* The commands in `cli/` are more independent and self-actualizing
  * They export their own usage string
  * They parse their own command line options
* cli.js is cleaned up
  * it only parses the options that it cares about
  * handles printing usage info for every command
  * exports the current version

# I Need Help

I'm not sure how to implement the `run` command. This PR adds it as an `unimplemented()` piece of code. 
We could merge this into the `cli-refactor` branch then fix the run command in there, or we can update this PR if someone can assist in implementing it.
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTYzMzQzNDI4.Author: JordanShurmer

Comments.Body:
Test it out here:

```
deno run --unstable -A https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JordanShurmer/lume/1872c101bd57a943908a5666daa6339da410cdb1/cli.js --help


deno run --unstable -A https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JordanShurmer/lume/1872c101bd57a943908a5666daa6339da410cdb1/cli.js build --help

# etc.
```
Comments.Author: JordanShurmer

Comments.Body:
Hi, thanks for your work here.
Both `build` and `run` need an instance of `site`. This is why in my implementation I've created the site before executing these two commands ([See here](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/feature/cli-refactor/cli.js#L83)).

Maybe cli.js can export a `createSite(options)` function that can be imported and used by those two commands (like you did with `version`).

Then, `run` only need to execute [`site.run()`](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/feature/cli-refactor/cli.js#L116) with the name of the script. For example: `lume run deploy` should execute `site.run("deploy")`.

Another thing: can you run `deno fmt` on finish this pull request? I want to use the same code format of Deno (the only exception is CHANGELOG.md) that should be ignored.

Thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Thanks. all, for reviewing.

Made various changes to address the feedback:

* Implemented the run command
  * pulled the site building logic into a shared function in cliUtils.js
* Refactored the way cli.js pulls in the commands to avoid doing dynamic import
* Tried to clean up cli.js even more, let me know what you think

PR_kwDOEYAvFc5SFOlm.Title: fix: Site.copy doing nothing for trailing slashes
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5SFOlm.Body:
Previously

    site.copy("static/");

had no effect whatsoever because of the trailing slash.

This commit fixes this bug to recognize the trailing slash and remove it. The trailing slash is still significant since:

    site.copy("foo/");

will now only copy a directory "foo" but not a file named "foo" (as you would expect).

Fixes #425.
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5SFOlm.Author: not-my-profile

Comments.Body:
Hi! There's indeed a bug here!

Your fix seems to be okay, but I think the real issue is in the `normalizePath` function that should ensure that a path doesn't end with a slash (unless the path is exactly "/").

This is the way Lume handles internally all paths: always use posix separator (even on Windows), always starts with `/` and, here is the bug, never should end with `/` (except the path "/").

Would you like to make these changes?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Hi!

I did consider adding the trailing slash trimming to `normalizePath`, however decided against it since I think it would be counter-intuitive if `site.copy("foo/")` also copied a file named `foo` and not just a directory named `foo`. That is modelling the behavior after the well-known Linux behavior (ENOTDIR), e.g:

* `cp foo/ bar` will fail if `foo` isn't a directory with `cp: cannot stat 'foo/': Not a directory`.
* `fs.copyFileSync('foo/', 'bar')` will fail if `foo` isn't a directory with `Error: ENOTDIR: not a directory, copyfile 'foo/' -> 'bar'`.

Hence I introduced the `dirOnly` to enable the distinction between `foo/` and `foo`. Only that the `Site.copy` function currently doesn't report any error/warning if the specified file doesn't exist, which I'd however consider to be part of a larger question about error detection, for which I just opened the issue #427.

If we had a mechanism to report non-fatal errors as suggested in #427, we could make `site.copy("foo/")` report such an error if `foo` isn't a directory, and thus be consistent with the vast majority of other IO tools / functions. What do you think about this idea?
Comments.Author: not-my-profile

Comments.Body: I see but I think this distinction is no needed because AFAIK, is not possible to have a file and a folder with the same name in the same directory. I think Lume should be able to handle these small details and resolve the correct path without forcing to users to specify it in a specific way. For example, without the normalizePath function, the user would be forced to start all paths with slash, `site.copy("foo")` wouldn't work, but `site.copy("/foo")` would.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: You're right the distinction isn't strictly needed. I just think it would be weird for `site.copy("foo/")` to also copy a file named `foo` if it happens to exist since it looks like you have explicitly indicated the source to be a directory and the common practice for that mismatch scenario is to fail. Note that specifying a trailing slash for directories would still be optional, this PR doesn't force you to use trailing slashes for directories (`"foo"` would still apply to both files and directories).
Comments.Author: not-my-profile

Comments.Body:
Okay, good point. Had not thought on that.
Thank!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

PR_kwDOEYAvFc5q1yFl.Title: add date format HUMAN_SINCE
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5q1yFl.Body:
add date format HUMAN_SINCE
add date format HUMAN_SINCE_STRICT

## Description

I'd like to be able to have lume display an article as having been written "three days ago" rather than "March 23, 2024", but the date package doesn't currently support relative dates. I've exposed functionality in the already-relied-upon [date-fns](https://date-fns.org/) package to add two new date formats: 
 - `"HUMAN_SINCE"`, exposing [`formatDistanceToNow`](https://date-fns.org/v3.6.0/docs/formatDistanceToNow), and
 - `"HUMAN_SINCE_STRICT"`, exposing [`formatDistanceToNowStrict`](https://date-fns.org/v3.6.0/docs/formatDistanceToNowStrict).

Note: I have done everything on the check list below, but that check list does not tell me how I would update the plugin's documentation.

## Related Issues

PR_kwDOEYAvFc5SPhWa.Title: fix: error on comment-only YAML front matter
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5SPhWa.Body:
YAML front matters such as the following:

    ---
    # Nothing here.
    ---

previously resulted in:

    error: TypeError: Cannot set properties of null (setting 'content')

which as it turns out was caused by this Deno standard library peculiarity:

    > import {parse} from "https://deno.land/std@0.189.0/yaml/parse.ts";
    > parse('')
    undefined
    > parse('# test')
    null
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5SPhWa.Author: not-my-profile

Comments.Body: Okay, good catch! Probably this should be fixed in the std/yaml implementation, but for now it's okay to fix it here.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: The std/yaml impl is a copy of js-yaml, which [has a relevant issue from 2020](https://github.com/nodeca/js-yaml/issues/567#issuecomment-685070822).
Comments.Author: not-my-profile

Comments.Body: Thanks for the info. I just filed the issue at https://github.com/denoland/deno_std/issues/3426
Comments.Author: oscarotero

PR_kwDOEYAvFc5J24n6.Title: refactor: type `reload_client.ts` (WIP)
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5J24n6.Body:
## Description

`reload_client.js` -> `reload_client.ts`

inferred function parameter with their input values, however these two functions may have incorrect (placeholder) types:
```ts
function styleIsImported(url: URL, style: CSSStyleSheet)
function reloadStylesheet(element: Element | ProcessingInstruction)
```

https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/73200d261f3bda81c04fb820d71e118aaca8060a/middlewares/reload_client.js#L125-L127
- [CSSStyleSheet](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/CSSStyleSheet) [does not have origin attribute](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/blob/098e18ebc1d31bb0523c735355e815817091116a/lib/lib.dom.d.ts#L3418-L3432)

https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/73200d261f3bda81c04fb820d71e118aaca8060a/middlewares/reload_client.js#L155-L160
- [Element](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/blob/098e18ebc1d31bb0523c735355e815817091116a/lib/lib.dom.d.ts#L5010-L5119) | [ProcessingInstruction](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/blob/098e18ebc1d31bb0523c735355e815817091116a/lib/lib.dom.d.ts#L11042-L11046) does not have `href` attribute

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjEwOTM1ODA2.Title: Make slugifier better
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjEwOTM1ODA2.Body:
* Added Unicode normalization, which handles most of the slug characters. This way we don’t need to list lots of characters.
* Moved the hyphen at the end of the character classes, so it’s clearer that it represents a literal hyphen and not a character range.
* Added ß to the character list.
* Moved the character list inside the function, because it doesn’t need to be exported. *(Or does it?)*
* Replaced the unnecessary function with a string.
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjEwOTM1ODA2.Author: valtlai

Comments.Body: The reason to export the characters list is to allow to configure the characters substitution in `_config.js` file for other casuistry not covered. But I guess with the Unicode normalization (I didn't know this trick) it's not needed.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

PR_kwDOEYAvFc5iLYnL.Title: Support x-default in Multilanguage & Sitemap plugins
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5iLYnL.Body:
## Description

Right now it is just a draft for discussion

## Related Issues

Fix #528

### Check List

- [x] Have you read the
      [CODE OF CONDUCT](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [x] Have you read the document
      [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)
  - [x] One pull request per feature. If you want to do more than one thing,
        send multiple pull request.
  - [x] Write tests.
  - [x] Run deno `fmt` to fix the code format before commit.
  - [x] Document any change in the `CHANGELOG.md`.
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5iLYnL.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
I wouldn't treat this option like another language code. I guess the use case of `x-default` is to provide a language selector to some pages (typically the homepage). This code should be in the head and in the sitemap (it affects also to the `sitemap` plugin).

I imagine a variable at page level to indicate that a page has a default lang. For example:

```yml
lang: en
langSelector: /country-selector/
```

Other option is mark one page as `x-default`. When the plugin detects this page, it's treated as a special page for selecting the language, and it's included as `<link>` in the other pages. My only question is if the link to the `x-default` page must be in all pages or only in the homepage.

```yml
lang: x-default
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> My only question is if the link to the x-default page must be in all pages or only in the homepage.

Once users wish to support multiple languages with fallback system, `x-default` should be placed in all pages. Here is real life example:

![homepage](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/assets/68631458/2ba2daa1-4ec9-48e2-8805-90b54f6c710f)

![content page](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/assets/68631458/1233bbc4-7032-4f91-9d69-cc44919f13e7)

> I guess the use case of x-default is to provide a language selector to some pages (typically the homepage)

You are correct, the main purpose of `x-default` is link to language selector page. Google said: "it was designed for language selector pages and so it will work best with those."[^1] However, Google didn't deny the fact that it could be used "the `x-default` value for any page"[^1]. This can be understood as fallback pages can also be `x-default` value.

> I wouldn't treat this option like another language code ... (it affects also to the sitemap plugin).

The reason I treat it as another language code (set 'x-default' in `languages` option) so that sitemap plugin can easily generate it. I belive it is the right idea because I check the sitemap plugin and found out that to generate language link list, it use `alternates` var from multilanguages plugin which will be easy if we inject `x-default` in to `alternates` var. On the other hand, `x-default` use the same value with normal hreflang. Isn't it is perfect reason to use it?

> I imagine a variable at page level to indicate that a page has a default lang. For example:
>
> ```yml
> lang: en
> langSelector: /country-selector
> ```

You got a point. How about this:

1. If plugin has config `defaultLanguage` and `langSelector`, then `x-default` value in each page is the `langSelector` page.
2. If plugin has no config `defaultLanguage` and not `langSelector`, then we disable `x-default` feature.
3. If plugin has config `defaultLanguage` and not `langSelector`, then `x-default` value in each page is the fallback page to the default language.
4. If plugin has no config `defaultLanguage` but has `langSelector`, then `x-default` value in each page is the `langSelector` page.

With these cases, we can cover 2 usages (both recommend and side effect). `x-default` will be used as language selector page when `langSelector` involved (no 1, 4).  `x-default` will be used as language fallback page when only `defaultLanguage` involved (no 3). Last one is a bit redundant when we disable `x-default`, because without `defaultLanguage` no bare `index.html` will be generated which users will likely choose one. But, well...

So with that thought, setting `x-default` as default option wasn't very good. I think I should only inject `x-default` after check all the cases carefully.

What do you think?

[^1]: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/specialty/international/localized-versions#xdefault
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
The reason I think including `x-default` in the list of languages is a bad idea is because it's not really a language (there's no `<html lang="x-default">` pages), it only specifies the relationship between two pages.

In this example (view-source:https://newtoninvestment.jp/) of a site in English and Japanese languages, the `x-default` page of the japanese language is the english version, even when english version is not the default language (English pages paths are prefixed with `/en/`).

What do you think about this?

- The `x-default` page can be defined at page level with the `defaultAlternate` variable (name may change).
  - This allows to specify the language selector page globally for all pages, for example in a `_data.yml` file in the root of the project:
    ```yml
    defaultAlternate: /country-selector/
    ```
  - But also allows to customize the language selector for a specific page (overriding the variable in the front matter) or a group of pages (by creating a `_data.yml` file in a subfolder).
- This variable could have a url (like `/country-selector/`) so it links to this url, or a language code (like `defaultAlternate: en`) to link to that language version.
- By having the `defaultAlternate` variable at page level, the sitemap can also use it to generate the links.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> there's no <html lang="x-default"> pages

Yeah, my shortcut isn't worth for the sake of simplicity.

> even when english version is not the default language

I don't think it is strange. They just tried to do fallback language for international visitors. Usually the best usage recommended by Google is for language selector page. But in both cases we saw, [yours](https://newtoninvestment.jp) & [mine](https://www.ancestry.com) are all used it as fallback language page rather than language selector page.

About your solution, I agree, but can we use the name `langX` because the `x-default` is clearly both selector and fallback (so 2 names we came up so far is not really match the purpose).
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
> About your solution, I agree, but can we use the name `langX` because the `x-default` is clearly both selector and fallback (so 2 names we came up so far is not really match the purpose).

Do you mean this?

```yml
lang: en
langX: /country-selector
```

Yeah, is shorter. The reason of suggesting `defaultAlternate` is because all alternate pages are stored in the `alternates` variable, so in the templates you have the `alternates` and `defaultAlternate`. But it's okay to use `langX` because `alternates` is a variable generated by Lume, not defined in the frontmatter.

In summary, it would work in this way:

- The user can define the `langX` variable in the page or _data files.
- The multilanguage plugin will read this variable to generate the `alternateX` variable.
  - If the variable is a URL (starts with `/`), the plugin searches the page with this url and creates the `alternateX` variable with the `data` of the page.
  - If the variable is a language code (like `en`) the `alternateX` is the `data` object of the page in `alternates` with the same language code.
  - If the variable is empty, the `alternateX` variable is empty.
- The multilanguage plugin will add the `x-default` link using the `alternateX` value:
  ```js
  if (page.data.alteranteX) {
    //insert <link rel="alternate" hreflang="x-default" href="...">
  }
  ```
- The `sitemap` plugin will do the same. 

What do you think?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Sounds good, I'll work on the code now.
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body: I didn't close this, it must be because I reset the branch to the latest. I'll reopen it with dummy commit for now :p
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
1. I'm concern about the UX. User could be easily mix up the `defaultLanguage` vs `langX`. Since both of them are about the fallback language. We should update and be clear in the documentation about this problem.
2. Maybe the `langX` should also support `http` string (external link) for better scalability. (user can have a language gateway for multiple lume sites)

Thanks for the review, I'm working on it now.
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
I'm thinking of maybe `langX` is not a good name. What you do think about `noLang`? to be clear that it's the url to follow when the user's language doesn't match with any available language in the site.

If we are going to allow external urls, maybe `alternateX` doesn't have to exist. Multilanguage plugin could just resolve the `noLang` value if its value is one of the available languages (like converting `en` to `/en/...`). And this url is all we need to build the alternate link and sitemap. We don't need the `alternateX` variable with the data object of the page.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> I'm thinking of maybe `langX` is not a good name

Upto now, I agree, it doesn't look good on user pov. But `noLang` could also be confused as well imho. I think about a longer name but more close to html standard: `defaultHrefLang`.

> ... maybe `alternateX` doesn't have to exist ... We don't need the `alternateX` variable with the data object of the page

I think so, in the best case, we don't have to expose a `alternateX` var. But I try to come up a case where developers really need a fully processed link. Maybe they need to display it in language switcher? If so should `alternateX` (which should have new name) be a string or a new type of object that can provide more information?
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body: > But noLang could also be confused as well imho. I think about a longer name but more close to html standard: defaultHrefLang. The problem of having "default" in the name is that it can be confused with the `defaultLanguage` option of the plugin. Maybe `unknownLang`? Or if we want a name more close to the `x-default` attribute, maybe `xDefaultLang`. If the variable only has the url of the page, I'd include the "url" somewhere: `defaultLangUrl`, `unknownLangUrl`, etc. > But I try to come up a case where developers really need a fully processed link. Maybe they need to display it in language switcher? It would be possible. Let's say we use `xDefaultLang` variable: ```html Language picker ``` If you need to access to the page data: ```html {{ set xPage = search.page(`url=${xDefaultLang}`) }} {{ xPage.title }} ``` Having only the URL in this variable, it's possible to use external URLs for the language picker. This can be useful for multidomain sites, where the language picker is, for example (`example.com`) and every language has its own domain (`example.es`, `example.it`) or subdomain (`es.example.com`, `it.example.com`, etc).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> having "default"... confused with the `defaultLanguage` option

That makes sense. It would confuses dev who try to get `defaultLanguage` variable.

> ... if we want a name more close to the x-default attribute, maybe `xDefaultLang`...

We still have default in the end, ain't it? I kinda want to avoid `unknown` word, it could be TypeScript keyword. The word itself wouldn't be very meaningful. The ideal word is fallback, but it could also be `defaultLanguage`. I ask ChatGPT for similar words:

![image](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/assets/68631458/8dc73168-c1a0-4b41-ad60-49f0a016b963)

![image](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/assets/68631458/d651f5a7-29d4-404a-92d3-e06c69c4d919)

I prefer `standbyLangUrl` and `auxiliaryLangUrl` or `auxLangUrl`. "Standby" sound more friendly to everyone and "au**x**iliary" make the sense out of `x-default`. What's about your opinion?
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
Hmmm, naming thinks is complicated. 😄

We agree that the name should contain "lang" (obviously) and "url" (because it has a url, not a language code).
Fallback may make sense (fallbackLangUrl), but it looks like it's the url of the fallback language, which is not correct: there's no any fallback language because there's no language at all.

Maybe the most accurate name is `unmatchedLangUrl`: the url for when the user's language doesn't match with any of the site available language.

Does it make sense to you?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> `unmatchedLangUrl`

That sounds sexy. Let go with this :))
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
Let me do one last confirm for this case. So now we will have:

1. `data.unmatchedLang` is the input which can be lang-code, absolute path, external url
2. `data.unmatchedLangUrl` is the output with proper URL that can be used

Is it ok to you, did I miss anything else?
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
I would use only one variable. It's like `url` variable: the user can define it as relative (`./pathname/`) and Lume resolves it.

`unmatchedLang` is fine (the url part can be omited, I think it's not confused now).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Now that I remember the url option also got rewritten from `_data` to `layout/*`. Ok, we can do the same thing with `unmatchedLangUrl`. But I don't think we should remove suffix "url". Like `url` is rewritten and `*Url` are also rewritten, that will make more sense right?

For now, I'll do with rewritable `unmatchedLangUrl` but still open for suggest any time you have new idea.
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
Fair enough.
I suggested `unmatchedLang` because it's shorter, but `unmatchedLangUrl` is OK to me.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Thanks for your great work!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjcxODYwNDUy.Title: Typescript code conversion
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjcxODYwNDUy.Body:
By popular request, I'm trying to convert Lume to Typescript. I'm not an expert and I'd like to check also how this can affect to performance (the initial checking seems slower).
If anyone want to help here, it will be really appreciated.
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjcxODYwNDUy.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: @oscarotero TypeScript startup speed will _definitely_ be slower but execution speed should be the same or negligible from what I've seen. If you [connect with me on LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/shahidshah/) we can coordinate a quick call or two to discuss where my team and I might be able to help. I've got a lot of Deno-specific TypeScript under my belt and we are heavy users of Hugo with lots of opinionated views about static sites so joining forces makes sense.
Comments.Author: shah

Comments.Body: It looks like you're making good progress here @oscarotero, nice work. If you're interested in a collaborative code review for some comments for stronger type-safety let me know and we can schedule some time to chat via Skype or Discord audio/video. My suggestions will be opinionated and worth a "live" conversation if you think that's useful. I could also show you my latest code where I've added TypeScript in my content to see if that might help inform some of your changes.
Comments.Author: shah

Comments.Body:
@shah There's still much work to do and I'm learning Typescript at the same time, but yes, I've made some progress.
Code review would be great but it's too early right now. Feel free to make PR if you see something that could be improved.
Comments.Author: oscarotero
oscarotero

Comments.Body: @oscarotero sorry, looks like I missed your message from last week. Do you want to try a collaborative session via screen share to show me where you're seeing errors and we can review together? To coordinate drop me an email since I'm part of many GitHub projects and could miss it coming just through GH.
Comments.Author: shah

Comments.Body:
@shah Don't worry. Finally I've managed to convert the library to Typescript progressively, so this branch will be removed.

The collaborative session would be great but I'll be a bit bussy the next 2-3 days. If you want, you can take a look to `master` branch, specially if you search for `// @ts-ignore` comments.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: That's fantastic @oscarotero - I look forward to trying out the next release with initial TS implementation!
Comments.Author: shah

PR_kwDOEYAvFc5WKIGI.Title: Relative URL Plugin Not Work when Pretty URL is disabled.
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5WKIGI.Body:
<!--Please read the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)-->

## Description

Relative URL plugin give wrong path when pretty URL is disabled. URL are always start with `../`. For example when page is at `/index.md` and url is `/styles.css`, resolved url is `../styles.css`.

PR_kwDOEYAvFc6IN9h-.Title: jsx_preact: Add type for `Lume.Data.children`
PR_kwDOEYAvFc6IN9h-.Body:
## Description

The `jsx_preact` plugin adds a type for the `children` field on `Lume.Data`. Unfortunately, this doesn't pass tests because it conflicts with the `children` field added by the `jsx` plugin. Is there a way to move forward with this? My project of course only uses the `jsx_preact` plugin, so I get no type annotations for the `children` field.

### Check List

- [x] Have you read the
      [CODE OF CONDUCT](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [x] Have you read the document
      [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)
  - [x] One pull request per feature. If you want to do more than one thing,
        send multiple pull request.
  - [ ] Write tests.
  - [x] Run deno `fmt` to fix the code format before commit.
  - [ ] Document any change in the `CHANGELOG.md`.
PR_kwDOEYAvFc6IN9h-.Author: 9999years

Comments.Body:
Thanks!
It fails because tests check the types of all plugins at the same time. In a real project only one jsx plugin must be used (either `jsx` or `jsx_preact`).

A solution is to add the `// @ts-ignore - jsx and jsx_preact conflict` declaration before both `children` declarations. This will make ts checker to ignore it and the tests will pass.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Thanks! I'm seeing a failure in `tests/esbuild.test.ts` for the JSX plugin, even though this PR doesn't change that code -- any idea what's going on there? Seems to produce a diff in a huge block of minified code.
Comments.Author: 9999years

Comments.Body: I think the `// @ts-ignore` directive must be added to both `children` declarations. Not only jsx_preact but also to jsx https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/main/plugins/jsx.ts#L135
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: It seems to pass tests locally with the annotation only on the `jsx_preact` plugin, but maybe that makes it depend on module import order... happy to add it to the other one as well though
Comments.Author: 9999years

Comments.Body:
In my end, tests only pass if the anotation is in both places. So maybe it depends on the module order.
Please, add it to jsx plugin and let's see :)
thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I've merged and will include the ignore directive if it keeps failing. 
Thanks for your contribution!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

PR_kwDOEYAvFc5ddvBg.Title: refactor(server): use `Deno.serve()`
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5ddvBg.Body:
<!--Please read the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)-->

## Description

A refactoring of Lume Server that uses the new `Deno.serve()` to simplify structure and improve performance.

> I'm not quite sure how to write a changelog for v2 at the moment, so I left it out.

```diff
import Server from "https:/deno.land/x/lume/core/server.ts";

const server = new Server({
  port: 8000,
  root: `${Deno.cwd()}/_site`,
});

- await server.start();
+ server.start();
+ // optional: Deno.serve({ port: 8000 }, server.handle)

console.log("Listening on http://localhost:8000");
```

It's worth noting that Middleware's `ConnInfo` has changed to `Deno.ServeHandlerInfo`.

refs:

* https://devhd.wordpress.com/2015/07/31/10-ways-to-optimize-your-feed-for-feedly/

PR_kwDOEYAvFc6Iojyp.Author: castaneai

Comments.Body:
I'm okay with adding support for Feedly, but I'd rather to have good defaults instead of adding manually these fields.
What do you think about the following changes?

- Add `xmlns:webfeeds="http://webfeeds.org/rss/1.0"` and `version="2.0" to the XML. No need to configure it.
- Extend the `FeedInfoOptions` interface with `color`,  `image` and `icon` properties. This also matches with [Metas plugin](https://lume.land/plugins/metas/#description) that have these properties.
- Some of these properties are also supported by [JSON Feed](https://www.jsonfeed.org/version/1/), (like `icon` for example), so we should modify the JSON generation to use them.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Thanks, @oscarotero.
That change sounds like a good one! I'm willing to take a little time to adopt those changes.
Comments.Author: castaneai

Comments.Body: Great! Thank you!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: I have rewritten it to three options: image, icon and color.
Comments.Author: castaneai

Comments.Body: It's perfect. Thank you!

PR_kwDOEYAvFc5D7woT.Title: Make filters available as a global variable in Pug
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5D7woT.Body:
## Description This PR makes filters registered by plugins and by the user available as a global variable in Pug templates. Previously, filters are already exposed as Pug filters, which come with the unfortunate downside that all inputs must be in plain text and no dynamic inputs are allowed. With this approach, dynamic variables can be used in filters like other template engines. Example: ```pug h1= filters.date(new Date(), "HUMAN_DATETIME") ``` Output: ```html

November 30th, 2022 at 9:57:16 AM GMT+8

```` ## Related Issues Fixes #320. ### Check List - [X] Have you read the [CODE OF CONDUCT](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) - [X] Have you read the document [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)

## Description

Adds a plugin for [PurgeCSS](https://purgecss.com/). This has several advantages over using PurgeCSS via the PostCSS plugin:

- Scan generated HTML (from plugins, Headless CMS or an API)
- Scan bundled JS dependencies (bootstrap, etc.)
- Only include CSS that is actually necessary (don't include drafts or conditional HTML that does not make it into the build)

PR_kwDOEYAvFc4umXG1.Title: Allow passing sourceMap options to PostCSS
PR_kwDOEYAvFc4umXG1.Body:
PostCSS accepts `boolean | SourceMapOptions` as `options.map`.
This slightly changes the default behavior, as "map: true" will default to inline.

We can change it to `options.sourceMap === true ? {inline: false} : options.sourceMap`, but I'm not sure is worth keeping the extra weirdness. WDYT?
PR_kwDOEYAvFc4umXG1.Author: fserb

Comments.Body:
Mmmm, ok, it makes sense.
Could you change the type in the Options interface?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: like this?
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body: Yes, but no need to replicate the types in the dep file. Postcss provides the types here: https://deno.land/x/postcss@8.3.11/lib/postcss.d.ts#L258
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: I have no idea if this is the proper way to lift a definition from a d.ts. :)
Comments.Author: fserb

PR_kwDOEYAvFc6GIytF.Title: Fix icon plugin for custom catalog
PR_kwDOEYAvFc6GIytF.Body:
<!--Please read the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)-->

## Description

Fixes a small bug I encountered in the `icons` plugin when using a custom catalog (specifically: [Skill Icons](https://skillicons.dev)). See this example:

```js
site.use(icons(
  {
    catalogs: [
      {
        id: "skill-icon",
        src: "https://skillicons.dev/icons?i={name}&theme={variant}",
        variants: [
          "dark",
          "light",
        ],
      },
    ],
  },
));
```

Upon running the website, Lume would return `> Catalog "skill-icon" not found`. 

I traced the issue in the `plugins/icons.ts` to a missing `options` when attempting to get the catalog (line 30). Replace `catalogs` with `options.catalogs` fixes the issue.

## Related Issues

<!--
  Link to the issue that is fixed by this PR (if there is one)
  e.g. Fixes #1234, Addresses #1234, Related to #1234, etc.
-->

> Not sure if baseUrl is mandatory for mdx. If it is, maybe it can changed to:
> 
> ```js
> const baseUrl = toFileUrl(join(this.baseUrl, filename || "")).href;

Changing to this works fine:

```diff
- const baseUrl = toFileUrl(join(this.baseUrl, filename || "")).href;
```

Using an empty string will cause the JSX import to fail.

PR_kwDOEYAvFc5Vj_X2.Title: Parameterize command for Netlify CMS proxy server in netlify_cms plugin
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5Vj_X2.Body:
## Description

I never want to have to install NPM just to run `netlify-cms-proxy-server` to use the netlify_cms plugin locally (I can install it via `deno install npm:netlify-cms-proxy-server` instead). Therefore I added a `proxyCommand` option for the `netlify_cms` plugin and set the default of the current command used (`npx netlify-cms-proxy-server`). It makes it overridable for people like me who want to be rid of NPM from my system entirely.

N/A

Comments.Body:
Please, give me some time to review this PR (and the middleware too). I didn't know the hatsu library and want to understand what this plugin does and what's the problem to solve. 🙏 

Thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> I didn't know the hatsu library and want to understand what this plugin does and what's the problem to solve. 🙏 

The Hatsu plugin gets ActivityPub integration from Hatsu by setting up a static .well-known file and setting an activity+json alternate for matching urls.

https://hatsu.cli.rs/users/redirecting-with-static-files-and-markup.html

On top of this, Hatsu middleware goes a step further and redirects for .well-known and matched activity+json requests to be compatible with more Fediverse software.
Comments.Author: kwaa

Comments.Body:
> What these files can do?

When a user searches for `@user@instance.tld` on Fediverse, the server tries to request `https://instance.tld/.well-known/webfinger?resource=acct:@user@instance.tld`. The Hatsu plugin's `wellKnown` part supports the query by saving the webfinger as a static file.

host-meta is related to webfinger and may be requested by some implementations. Here it is set to the webfinger URL of the Hatsu instance.

> For example, Lume has a mastodon account [fosstodon.org/@lume](https://fosstodon.org/@lume). What benefits provides the hatsu plugin?

The Hatsu plugin only considers integration with Hatsu instances, so there is no benefit.

As for why this isn't a generic Fediverse plugin, it's because Hatsu has a unique inferable AS2 URL feature.

When the pathname matches, the post will set the `application/activity+json` alternate (or automatically redirect the `application/activity+json` request when using middleware):

```html
<!-- generated alternate for https://example.com/foo/bar -->
<link rel="alternate" type="application/activity+json" href="https://hatsu.local/posts/https://example.com/foo/bar" />
```

When a Fediverse user searches for `https://example.com/foo/bar`, they will be redirected to `https://hatsu.local/posts/https://example.com/foo/bar` to display the search results.

> I'm using a web component to show the replies of a mastodon post as comments of posts (see the [comments of this post as an example](https://lume.land/blog/posts/lume-cms/)). Does this plugin make this process easier?

Yes, I have an example in the Hatsu documentation, but this plugin currently doesn't implement that part (since it's available only for theme-simple-blog).

https://hatsu.cli.rs/users/backfeed-based-on-mastodon-comments.html#lume
Comments.Author: kwaa

Comments.Body:
Thanks for the detailed explanation!

If I understand correctly, this plugins does two things:

- It creates a static version of the webfinger file to support requests like `.well-known/webfinger?query_params`. Due the file is static, the query parameters are really not used, it shows always the same content.
  - I understand the middleware in https://github.com/lumeland/lume/pull/632/ does this redirect to the hatsu server.
- It also creates alternate links tags in some pages, which seems to be a special feature of hatsu, right?

What do you thing about split this plugin in two?

For example, a `webfinger` plugin to generate the `/.well-known/webfinger` file (and other files that may be required in the .well-known folder). This plugin could generate the file using static data and/or fetch the data from other services. For example:

```js
site.use(webfinger({
    data: {}, // Data passed directly
    remote: // Url with a remote bridge, like  https://hatsu.local
});
```

Does it make sense to you?
This could allow to generate webfinger files with different methods and would be more flexible.

And `hatsu` plugin could be a different plugin to implement hatsu specific features.
Let me know your thoughts.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
> * It creates a static version of the webfinger file to support requests like `.well-known/webfinger?query_params`. Due the file is static, the query parameters are really not used, it shows always the same content.
>   * I understand the middleware in [feat: new middleware `hatsu` #632](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/pull/632) does this redirect to the hatsu server.

yes.

> * It also creates alternate links tags in some pages, which seems to be a special feature of hatsu, right?

right, I think this is the main feature of this plugin, to provide search discovery on Fediverse for website URLs.

> What do you thing about split this plugin in two?
> 
> For example, a `webfinger` plugin to generate the `/.well-known/webfinger` file (and other files that may be required in the .well-known folder). This plugin could generate the file using static data and/or fetch the data from other services. For example:

The implementation is simple, though it will make configuration more cumbersome.
People who only need webfinger can always simply create a static webfinger file.

> Does it make sense to you? This could allow to generate webfinger files with different methods and would be more flexible.
> 
> And `hatsu` plugin could be a different plugin to implement hatsu specific features. Let me know your thoughts.

I feel necessary to think about the configuration format to try to avoid getting more complicated.
Comments.Author: kwaa

PR_kwDOEYAvFc6iQlPk.Title: add the `stylePropertyNameCase` option to the mdx plugin
PR_kwDOEYAvFc6iQlPk.Body:
<!--Please read the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)-->

## Description

<!-- Write a brief description of the changes introduced by this PR -->

This PR adds the [`stylePropertyNameCase`](https://mdxjs.com/packages/mdx/#:~:text=stylePropertyNameCase) option to the MDX plugin.
Without this option, rehype plugins that depend on the `style` attribute—such as [`@shikijs/rehype`](https://shiki.style/packages/rehype#shikijs-rehype)—receive camel-cased style keys (`backgroundColor`), which break valid CSS in the generated HTML.
The new option lets users choose between `'dom'` (camelCase) and `'css'` (kebab-case), ensuring correct serialization for both client-side React and static HTML/SSR use-cases.

## Related Issues

<!--
  Link to the issue that is fixed by this PR (if there is one)
  e.g. Fixes #1234, Addresses #1234, Related to #1234, etc.
-->

There is no existing issue in this repository that tracks this problem.  
However, https://github.com/shikijs/shiki/issues/633 demonstrates the same style-attribute bug that this option might resolve.

### Check List

- [x] Have you read the
      [CODE OF CONDUCT](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [x] Have you read the document
      [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)
  - [x] One pull request per feature. If you want to do more than one thing,
        send multiple pull request.
  - [ ] Write tests.
  - [x] Run deno `fmt` to fix the code format before commit.
  - [x] Document any change in the `CHANGELOG.md`.
PR_kwDOEYAvFc6iQlPk.Author: s7tya

Comments.Body:
Oh, I didn't know this option even exists and I'm surprised to see that the default value is `dom`. This means that the default configuration of `mdx` doesn't produce valid HTML code.

I think this value should be always `css`, I don't see any reason to change it. Can you edit your PR to hardcode this option and remove the plugin options?

Thanks a lot!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: I’ve just updated the PR. Thanks for your quick response!

Comments.Body: It's perfect. Thank you!

PR_kwDOEYAvFc6gTNuK.Title: Add a try-catch to the Katex plugin
PR_kwDOEYAvFc6gTNuK.Body:
<!--Please read the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)-->

Hello, thank you for a wonderful static site generator. I've been using it for a few years, and I've enjoyed working with it very much. :3

## Description

When one uses the Katex plugin **with delimiters**, sometimes it can crash the whole build/watch process because Katex's `renderMathInElement` is not in a try-catch like `Katex.renderToString` is, so I've added that. (The `throwOnError` option is of little help, since [that only applies to `ParseError`s](https://github.com/KaTeX/KaTeX/issues/3680).)

## Related Issues

None that I could find.

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjEwOTY2ODAz.Title: Update MIME types
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjEwOTY2ODAz.Body:
* Deleted the `charset` parameter from `application/json` because the [default encoding](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7159#section-8.1) for JSON, or the [only one that’s allowed](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8259#section-8.1), is UTF-8 and there’s actually [no that parameter defined](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8259#page-12) for the MIME type.
* Added the `charset` parameter to `image/svg+xml` because it has no such default charset.
* Added the following formats to the list:
  * the RSS and Atom feed
  * [web app manifest](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Manifest)
  * AV1 image format (AVIF) and animated PNG (APNG), both having [some browser support](https://caniuse.com/avif,apng).
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjEwOTY2ODAz.Author: valtlai

Comments.Body: Thanks! 👍
Comments.Author: oscarotero

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0Njc1MDg2MzE0.Title: Allow setting includes directory and non-absolute copy
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0Njc1MDg2MzE0.Body:
This PR adds a `includes` option to change the `_includes` directory and allows `site.copy()` to copy from directories relative to `src` (dest is still absolute).

The rationale behind this is: for one of my projects, I have loads of top level files, I want to keep them in a subdirectory. When I change `src`, I'm kinda forced to change my assets and _include, because they "follow" the src. With this change, I'm able to specify `includes: "../_includes"` and `site.copy('../assets', 'assets')`.

Alternatively, we could make those two (includes and copy) to be relative to the execution, but I'm not sure that's a good idea, because it breaks the "I want to isolate a build" use case.
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0Njc1MDg2MzE0.Author: fserb

Comments.Body:
About the option to configure the `_includes` directory, It's a good idea. But keep in mind that this value is used in several engines and plugins, so you need to change in all places. Take a look here: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/search?q=_includes&type=code

And about the `copy()` change, I'm not sure to understand. You can already do `site.copy('../assets', 'assets')`.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Updated all `_includes`, sorry for that.

Regarding  `copy()`. I don't think that's true, because `join('/', '../x') == '/x'`, I mean, there's even a [test](https://deno.land/std@0.99.0/path/join_test.ts#L42) for that. I've replaced with `join('./', from)` for now.

That said, this is not correct yet, the watch on serve stopped working on assets once I did that. Looking into that now.
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body: Found it.
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body: Fixed it :)
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body: argh. The copy is still not working properly. Now it detects the change, but it doesn't copy the correct files on change.
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body: Fixed. :)
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body: I'm not absolutely convinced about `copy`, but I'll merge and try it.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I've testing the `copy()`change and it generates some issues. For example, on read the file to embed in the html by the `inline` plugin. I'm not confortable with letting to load and process files outside src folder. It looks a bit insecure and inconsistent (for example, the watcher now have to watch all these outside paths, and you forget to add also the _includes folder).

Sorry but I'm going to revert this change. Some ideas:

- You can use a script to copy manually the files and folder outside the src folder. For example:
  ```js
  site.addEventListener("beforeBuild", "cp ../outside-file.txt _dest/new-file.txt");
  ```
- I can include an option to add, manually more paths to the watcher:
  ```js
  site.watch("../outside-file.txt");
  ```

What do you think?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Hmmmmmmmmm. Okey. What do you think about doing the opposite then?

We keep `src` as "the root" of the project and keep all the `join('/', xx)` with the guarantee that we won't touch any file outside it.

And we add a path to be the "page dir" (that by default is `"."`) that decides where we are going to look for pages and what is the base path (on dest) for them?
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body: Mmmm, that's could be complex too. I'm doing some testing with symlinks, so I think it's the most clean and easy way. Just create a symlink in your src folder pointing to external sources. Does it fit to you?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Yes, sure, symlink solves it.

Maybe I could also solve this by either setting a URL data function that removes the top level dir or by changing the URLs on beforeBuild/beforeUpdate?
Comments.Author: fserb

Comments.Body: FWIW, I agree with creating "helper" functions as scripts to do anything complex outside of `src` directory and `symlinks` are ideal ways of bridging any gaps.
Comments.Author: shah
oscarotero
fserb

PR_kwDOEYAvFc6GmMqG.Title: Add integrity hash to hot reload inline script
PR_kwDOEYAvFc6GmMqG.Body:
<!--Please read the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)-->

## Description

I have been working on adding strict CSP rules to my website.

Although the hot reload script is only present during development, the strict CSP rules end up blocking the hot reload script from running at all. Plus, it generates some noise in the developer tools console:

> Refused to execute inline script because it violates the following Content Security Policy directive: "script-src-elem 'self' https://esm.sh". Either the 'unsafe-inline' keyword, a hash ('sha256-C2A/zAnUiwX3gOwj8Fa3vHcIis+esAeDAmnu4aN5OSE='), or a nonce ('nonce-...') is required to enable inline execution.

This diff adds the corresponding integrity hash property to the injected script tag.
