MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjM5OTQ4Mjg0.Title: changed page url generation
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjM5OTQ4Mjg0.Body:
@valtlai After a second thought, I've changed my mind about the possibility to define an url as an object and your idea of using the trailing slash to detect if the url is a pretty-html or a extension-less filename is better.

One of my goals with lume is building something easy to use and with predictable behavior, and the url variable that accepts so many formats (function, string, object) and different behaviors is far from being that.

With this change, the url can be defined using a function or a string:

- `/about-me` Generates a `/about-me` file (without extension)
- `/about-me/` Generates a `/about-me/index.html` file
- `/about-me.html` Generates a `/about-me.html` file

`prettyUrls` setting is used only if the page has not the url variable. `slugifyUrls` is used everywhere.
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjM5OTQ4Mjg0.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Looks good. I’m wondering how should be the `_headers` use case solved. Should one just add `replace: { "_": "_" }` to `slugifyUrls`? Should there possibly be a corresponding `slugifyUrls` variable in pages to disable the slugifier (or maybe to modify its configuration) on particular pages?
Comments.Author: valtlai

Comments.Body:
@valtlai Good question. I have some ideas:

- The variable `slugifyUrls` is good idea, but I'd like to avoid having too many configuration variables in the pages, because they are like global variables in the templates and this polution is not good.
- Other idea is recovering the `url` object for that. But instead `path` and `ext`, something like this:
  ```yml
  url:
    path: _headers
    slugify: false
  ```
The `ext` variable wouldn't exist. So, for a html page, the `path` value should contain also the extension. My only doubt is if we have an option to enable/disable slugify, why don't another to enable/disable pretty urls? In this case, what should do this?

```yml
url:
    path: /about-me/
    pretty: true
```

thoughts?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I like this approach. Most of the time, a string will be enough, but an object allows disabling the slugifier when needed. And with an object, we can have the variable `pretty` as well.

I think your example should produce `/about-me/index.html` regardless of the value of `pretty`.

If there wasn’t a trailing slash in your example and `pretty` was set to `false` or omitted (because it defaults to `false`, right?), `/about-me` would be produced. Otherwise – in the case of `pretty: true` – `/about-me/index.html` would be produced.
Comments.Author: valtlai

Comments.Body:
Fair.
I would defaults `pretty` and `slugify` to the global config `prettyUrls` and `slugifyUrls`. This way, you can override one option without having to set the other.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: But a string would still work as you described in the first comment, right?
Comments.Author: valtlai

Comments.Body: Yes. Strings would work like  in the first comment.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Okay, that’s nice. 🙂
Comments.Author: valtlai

PR_kwDOEYAvFc6AodBO.Title: esbuild plugin: add proper support for lume url rewrites
PR_kwDOEYAvFc6AodBO.Body:
<!--Please read the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)-->

## Description

As a follow up of PR #678. It's actually possible to pass esbuild a [in/out path map](https://esbuild.github.io/api/#entry-points) as `entryPoints`. Esbuild will then rewrite the urls accodingly. I initially thought using that would disable/break `entryNames` but that is not the case. This allows us to get full support for lume url rewrites (`basename` and `url`) for esbuild.

This PR also extends that to the manual url rewriting done by lume when bundling via esbuild is disabled. And extends manual import rewriting to dynamic imports and standalone imports without a `from`.

## Related Issues

Related to #678

PR_kwDOEYAvFc584JxK.Title: fallback to git last modified upon empty git created date
PR_kwDOEYAvFc584JxK.Body:
<!--Please read the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)-->

## Description

<!-- Write a brief description of the changes introduced by this PR -->

`getGitDate("created", ...)` uses `git log --diff-filter=A`
which returns nothing for files where the latest commit had a merge conflict.
Previously lume would use filesystem last modified date as a fallback.
This commit introduces a change to fall back to `git modified` first when using special value `git created` and `getGitDate("created", ...)` returns nothing.
I think `git modified` is more reliable than file system creation time under most CI environments.


## Related Issues

  Link to the issue that is fixed by this PR (if there is one)
  e.g. Fixes #1234, Addresses #1234, Related to #1234, etc.

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjE2MjQyMTE3.Title: Rename SOME operator to ANY
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjE2MjQyMTE3.Body: `SOME` feels like a plural, even though only one match is required. Also, the `ALL` and `SOME` operators are not documented. Are they really supported?
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjE2MjQyMTE3.Author: valtlai

Comments.Body:
Yes. It's an idea but I'm not fully convinced about the syntax and that's is why I didn't document yet.
`ALL` is used for tags (Here: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/helpers/search.js#L83).

I'd like to follow the [css attribute selector syntax](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/CSS/Building_blocks/Selectors/Attribute_selectors) so I guess the `ALL` operator should be `~=` (that is wrongly assigned to "contains" that should be `*=`.

My main question is how differentiate `SOME` from `ALL`. For example, if `tags~=javascript,deno` searches all pages having the two tags (the current ALL), how would be the syntax to search all pages that have at least one of these tags? Maybe `tags|=javascript,deno` ?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I agree the *contains* matching should use `*=`.

I don’t think `~=` really matches `ALL` but more like `SOME`. There’s no such attribute selector in CSS. You would just use multiple attribute selectors, like `[foo=~js][foo=~md]`. I guess if CSS supported something like `[foo~=js,md]`, that would behave like `SOME`.

What about `&=` for `ALL` and `|=` for `SOME`?
Comments.Author: valtlai

Comments.Body: I opened a draft PR for changing the search operators: #69. (Tried to rename the branch, but that didn’t really work.)
Comments.Author: valtlai

Comments.Body: Is this compatible? I've copied the format from the `DATE_ATOM` constant in php (https://www.php.net/manual/en/class.datetimeinterface.php#datetime.constants.atom).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Yes, it is. See the [examples](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4287#page-11) on the spec. Also GitHub uses `Z` in its Atom feeds, such as the one of [Lume’s commits](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/commits/master.atom#:~:text=%3Cupdated%3E,%3C/updated%3E).
Comments.Author: valtlai

Comments.Body: Ok, got it. Thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

PR_kwDOEYAvFc5GIafQ.Title: Resolve file URLs in source-map sources
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5GIafQ.Body:
<!--Please read the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)-->

## Description

Sometimes, a source-map uses stringified file URLs in the "sources" field. Sass does this. On Linux, we get an error when we try to fetch the sources' contents, in order to write the actual source-map file. Somehow, the full posix path is appended to the root file URL. So I made sure we convert file URLs to paths.

For example, say we have a file "src/style.scss" in the working directory "/home/binyamin/project". The source is resolved to "**file:///home/binyamin/project**/home/binyamin/project/src/style.scss".

    the code format before commit.

    `CHANGELOG.md`. (not applicable)

Comments.Body:
Sorry but I don't get it. This function is supposed to return always an URL (https: or file:). The code below always return the path as file urls.

Comments.Body: I did some more testing. It happens to me when using sass & lightning-css, and when the directory includes a space. The space is URL-encoded, and it's not decoded. Maybe switch from `new URL().href` to `fromFileUrl()`.

When you specify a local file as an import map, Lume raises an error even when the specified file is a valid import map.
```
$ lume --serve -- --import-map=./import_map.json
Error: Unable to load the import map file
- mapFile: ./import_map.json
    at getImportMap (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.4.1/ci.ts:53:13)

Caused by TypeError: Invalid URL
    at Object.opSync (deno:core/01_core.js:149:12)
    at opUrlParse (deno:ext/url/00_url.js:47:27)
    at new URL (deno:ext/url/00_url.js:320:20)
    at new Request (deno:ext/fetch/23_request.js:241:27)
    at (deno:ext/fetch/26_fetch.js:396:29)
    at fetch (deno:ext/fetch/26_fetch.js:392:12)
    at getImportMap (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.4.1/ci.ts:48:33)

This PR resolves this.

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjIzNjAxOTcy.Title: Fix date creation
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjIzNjAxOTcy.Body:
The `Date` constructor does not support `undefined` as an argument, so we must specify the default values ourselves.

Also
* allow a lowercase `t` as the date-time separator
* shorten the regexes by using `\d\d` instead of `\d{2}`
* reorder the comparison operators in comments to match the ones in `switch`–`case`.
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjIzNjAxOTcy.Author: valtlai

Comments.Body: The idea was to use the current time values for missing (or undefined) arguments. For example, `new Date(year, month, day)` creates a date with the current time (to hours, minutes and seconds). Your change forces the time to `0:00:00`. Maybe it makes more sense because using the current time is a bit random...
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
Yeah, I think it’s random. If I write `date>=2021-04`, I want a page with `date: 2021-04-01T00:00:00` to always match, regardless of the current date or time.

Hmm, the `Date` constructor doesn’t seem to use the current date time for the missing values, but replaces them with default values instead, like `1` for the day of the month or `0` for hours. (I tested with Chrome, which uses the same V8 JavaScript engine as Deno. See also [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Date/Date#individual_date_and_time_component_values).) Using `undefined` (as we’re doing with `hour && parseInt(hour)`) results `Invalid Date`.
Comments.Author: valtlai

Comments.Body: Yes, you're absolutely right. I don't know why I've assumed that it would take the current value.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: That would be nice 😄

Comments.Body: Thanks for inviting :)
Comments.Author: valtlai

Comments.Body: You're welcome!!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

PR_kwDOEYAvFc5_MBJw.Title: Use esbuild metafile. Add support for 'entryNames' esbuild option.
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5_MBJw.Body:
<!--Please read the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)-->

## Description

Currently (before this PR) the esbuild plugin would always output JS asset files as `options.outdir + replaceExtension(page.data.url)`. Which means The esbuild option [`entryNames`](https://esbuild.github.io/api/#entry-names) would not work.

Additionally the esbuild plugin tries to map output files from esbuild to lume pages (JS eintry points). This looks similar to this:

```js
const page = pages.find((page) => withoutExtension(page.sourcePath) === withoutExtension(outfile.path));
```

When `entryPoints` is set/changed this no longer works. The plugin then can not find a page for the outfile and adds it as a chunk.

When using the `entryNames` option like this:

```js
site.use(esbuild({
  options: {
    entryNames: "js/[name].[hash]",
  },
}));
```

Given a source file `scripts/index.ts` you would get something like the following output files:
- `js/index.WSRTVQIK.js`
  - No src connection
  - Bundled and transformed file
- `scripts/index.ts`
  - Src connection: `scripts/index.ts`
  - No transforms - raw source file

This PR adds support for the `entryPoints` option. It now uses the [esbuild metafile](https://esbuild.github.io/api/#metafile) which contains information about input and output files and their relation. Using this metafile we can properly map esbuild outfiles to lume pages. It now also uses the esbuild outfile filename as final `data.url`:

```js
page.data.url = outfile.path.replaceAll(
  dirname(page.sourcePath),
  dirname(page.data.url),
)
```

The replace is there to support url overwrites via `basename`. Using lume url overwrites in combination with the `entryNames` esbuild option works generally. However as we only write the file to a different location to what esbuild intended, JS imports to this file (e.g. via dynamic imports) are not rewritten! Esbuild currently does not offer any functionality to dynamically rewrite an outfile path per file (as far as I know). So this is as much as we can do.

I have also mostly reverted the changes from commit https://github.com/lumeland/lume/commit/13796a7fe66c007aaca6a23084233247a46235fb as that workaround is no longer necessary.

## Related Issues

None

### Check List

- [x] Have you read the
      [CODE OF CONDUCT](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [x] Have you read the document
      [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)
  - [x] One pull request per feature. If you want to do more than one thing,
        send multiple pull request.
  - [x] Write tests.
  - [x] Run deno `fmt` to fix the code format before commit.
  - [x] Document any change in the `CHANGELOG.md`.
PR_kwDOEYAvFc5_MBJw.Author: into-the-v0id

Comments.Body:
> To me, the most sensible part is the change of the output directory, which can break the code.

Note that the previous implementation completely ignored the esbuild output path and just wrote it to `page.data.url` - which also breaks dynamic imports if we use lume url overwrites via `basename`.
Comments.Author: into-the-v0id

Comments.Body:
> Note that the previous implementation completely ignored the esbuild output path and just wrote it to page.data.url - which also breaks dynamic imports if we use lume url overwrites via basename.

Yeah, to be honest, I hadn't thought about that situation until now! :)
Thanks for your contribution. The esbuild plugin is much stronger now!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDI1NTc4.Title: Support for non-HTML output formats?
MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDI1NTc4.Body:
I was curious if Lume supports non-HTML output formats such as:

* iCal (if we want to generate an iCal dynamically and serve that up)
* RSS (for obvious reasons)
* JSON (for example to serve up a JSON file through generated content, not static)
* MD (in case the final markdown should be served to the client and transformed in the browser)

By way of reference, here the [custom output formats Hugo supports](https://gohugo.io/templates/output-formats/) (we are using iCal and a few others now).
MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDI1NTc4.Author: shah

Comments.Body:
Yes, you can output any format. There are two ways:

- Add a subextension to the file. For example, `styles.css.njk` is a nunjucks file that output the file `styles.css`.
- Or include the `url` variable in the frontmatter or named module. For example:
```ts
export const url = "feed.rss";
```

In base-blog you have examples of both methods:
- Nunjucks template that exports a xml file with the RSS. [Link](https://github.com/lumeland/base-blog/blob/master/sitemap.xml.njk)
- Javascript template that exports a Json Feed. [Link](https://github.com/lumeland/base-blog/blob/master/feed.tmpl.js)
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
In case it's helpful, for those using TypeScript this is necessary if creating a JSON output format: `/api/file.json.tmpl.ts` template will create `/api/file.json` (I did not have a layout and just emitted output of `JSON.stringify(data)`. 

The feature is fantastic and much easier than in other static site generators. Now, we can create complete APIs statically.
Comments.Author: shah

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AdQ5W.Title: Build static site at the edge to dramatically speed up build latency
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AdQ5W.Body:
This may sound like a crazy idea, and I haven't found anybody doing this on the internet. Perhaps my google-foo is failing me, or perhaps it's just a stupid idea. If so, I'd be very interested in knowing why.

I was reading [this Deno Deploy post](https://deno.com/blog/fastest-git-deploys-to-the-edge) where they advertise how great their v8 isolates are because they're already running and how most other CI systems (like GitHub Actions) spend most of their time starting the Docker image and downloading dependencies, and not on doing the actual build. This makes a lot of sense to me.

But while that post seems to advertise this approach for dynamic edge functions (e.g. Deno Fresh), surprisingly there is nowhere a mention of building a static site (e.g. with Lume). More googling brought up [Static Files on Deno Deploy](https://deno.com/blog/deploy-static-files), but there they are using GitHub Actions again to call `deployctl` to upload the files to their CDN (because while you can use `Deno.readFile` etc. to read from the Deno CDN, you apparently cannot write to it that way). Thus you pay the VM startup time again.

Hence my question: has anybody tried building a static site at the edge (e.g. in a CloudFlare worker) and then upload the artefacts to a CDN? Potential obstacles:

- lack of a normal filesystem (but could use a virtual one like the [Origin private file system](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/File_System_API#origin_private_file_system))
- [lack of official REST API](https://community.cloudflare.com/t/does-cloudflare-pages-has-documented-rest-api-for-direct-upload/566364) for uploading to CloudFlare CDN, but perhaps there are other CDNs that have one?

Perhaps the static site generator would need to be rewritten a bit to work on the virtual file system. But assuming that would work: any other show stoppers? Would we really get static site deployments in the one-digit seconds?

Posting this here, as I thought this crowd might have thought about these things already. But happy to be pointed to a place that fits the question better.
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AdQ5W.Author: mb21

Comments.Body:
I'm not sure to understand.
The edge is just a marketing term to define a CDN that not only serve static files but also run some code on the server. Like a CDN, it requires different replicas or nodes around the world in order to process your request as close as possible to your location. This is why Deno Deploy cannot write on the disk, because it would write only in the disk of the current node, but the other nodes wouldn't have these changes. And syncinc changes between all nodes is hard (they have Deno KV for that).

For static sites, the build process is not important, because it's run only once. Then, the static site is replicated in different nodes and this is what make them so fast.

In my experience, services like [netlify](https://answers.netlify.com/t/is-there-a-list-of-where-netlifys-cdn-pops-are-located/855/2) are more performant than Deno deploy if you only have static files, because are specific for this. Only if you need some server-side processing (like providing an API or handle form submits) you need to use "the edge" (and Deno Deploy is a good option here).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AWq1v.Title: Where is `site.logger` in Lume 2?
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AWq1v.Body: Some plugin developers might need to know it as now the `site.logger` in Lume 1 is obsoleted in Lume 2.
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AWq1v.Author: ngdangtu-vn

Comments.Body:
In Lume 1, to print message on console, we tend to use `site.logger`. This feature has breaking change in Lume 2 with it own utils module `lume/core/utils/log.ts`.

**In Lume 1:**

```ts
// We need Site object to use logger
site.logger.log('message')
site.logger.warn('warning')
```

**In Lume 2**:

```ts
// We only have to call it from a utll module
import { log } from 'lume/core/utils/log.ts'

// Come with more flavour
log.info(msg)
log.debug(msg)
log.warning(msg)
log.error(msg)
log.critical(msg)
```
Comments.Author: ngdangtu-vn

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AQZZt.Title: Lume for SSG in Fresh
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AQZZt.Body:
I’m a bit out of my depth there, but could Lume possibly be used to add support for static site generation in the Fresh frontend framework?

Apparently SSG is not currently possible in Fresh, and it’s not straightforward to add it: https://github.com/denoland/fresh/discussions/254
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AQZZt.Author: erlend-sh

Comments.Body:
If you mean creating a site combining Fresh and Lume, I don't see how it can be done.

Lume has it's own way to generate the static files (similar to 11ty, jekyll or Hugo) that is a bit different from Fresh. It has support for JSX (React as primary library but you can use also Preact), so if your site is composed only with static pages, I think it should be easy to port to Lume. But Lume hasn't a built in concept of "islands" to create interactive components in the client side magically, so this part may take a bit more work (or maybe not, not sure).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AU3mX.Title: Is it possible compage current `url` with url resolved by `resolve_url` plugin?
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AU3mX.Author: cawa-93

Comments.Body:
Lume provides the `url` filter out of the box: https://lume.land/plugins/url/#url-filter You can do this: ```jsx export default ({lang, url}, helpers) => { const comparedUrl = helpers.url(`~/index.tsx(lang=${lang})`); const isCurrentPage = url === comparedUrl return
{goHome}
} ```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I see. I probably should use `page.src` ```tsx export default ({lang, url, page}) => { const isCurrentPage = page.src.entry.name === `index.tsx` return
{goHome}
} ```

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AeZ9S.Title: Self-referencing vars in data files
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AeZ9S.Body:
Is it possible to a) reference other values in the same `_data/myData.yml` file? or b) between files? eg `_data/myData.yml` and `_data/pluginData.yml`

If not, is it possible to set a global value in `_config.ts` and use that value in the data files?

Thanks in advance!
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AeZ9S.Author: feamsr00

Comments.Body:
`yml` format is a static format, it can't reference dynamically values from external files. For more advanced cases, you may use `_data/myData.js` or `_data/myData.ts` formats. With js or ts files, you can import or read other values.

In `_config.ts` yo can define global values with `site.data("name", value)`. But _data files cannot see these values.

If you tell me what do you want to do (what problem you want to solve) maybe I can give you a more precise solution.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4ARY8m.Title: How can I build my project offline?
D_kwDOEYAvFc4ARY8m.Body:
I use lume with the following plugins: esbuild, minify_html, multilanguage, sass, source_maps.

Is there a way to vendor (and if that's currently not possible,) to cache all dependencies locally, so I can build my project offline?

It currently says
```
TypeError: error sending request for url (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.12.1/plugins/sass.ts): error trying to connect: dns error: failed to lookup address information: Temporary failure in name resolution
    at file:///home/anick/project/_config.ts:4:18
    at (file:///home/anick/project/_config.ts:4:18)
    | 3 | import multilanguage from "lume/plugins/multilanguage.ts";
    | 4 | import sass from "lume/plugins/sass.ts";
    | ~ | ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^
    | 5 | import source_maps from "lume/plugins/source_maps.ts";

    at async createSite (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.12.1/cli/run.ts:51:17)
    at async build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.12.1/cli/build.ts:28:16)
    at async Command.execute (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.2/command/command.ts:1463:7)
    at async (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.12.1/cli.ts:147:3)
```
when I switch off my internet connection.

Btw. I love lume - it's so much better than everything I used before! Thank you so much for your work!
D_kwDOEYAvFc4ARY8m.Author: anick3

Comments.Body:
Deno cache all dependencies locally, so they are requested only the first time and then use the local cache. It should be enought to run Lume offline.

There's also the `deno vendor` command to store all dependencies locally in a directory (https://deno.land/manual@v1.27.2/tools/vendor). The problem is it doesn't work with `npm:` specifiers yet (it's in the [roadmap](https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/15960) but I don't know if it will be implemented in the short term.

Lume had the `lume vendor` command that runs `deno vendor` internally with additional tweaks to make it work. But this was removed in lume 1.12.0 due problems with npm dependencies. If you really need this, you can downgrade to lume 1.11.4 and use it.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AUCut.Title: Mismatch results in dev with the flag --location
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AUCut.Body:
The code:
```javascript
// _config.ts

import lume from "https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.17.4/mod.ts";

const site = lume({
  location: new URL("http://localhost:8888"),
  server: {
    port: 8888
  }
});

console.log(`site.options.location.href: ${site.options.location.href}`);

export default site;
```

Command A:
```bash
deno run --unstable -A https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.17.4/cli.ts -s -d
```

Output A:
```bash
...
site.options.location.href: http://localhost:3000/
...
```

Command B:
```bash
deno run --unstable -A https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.17.4/cli.ts -s -d --location=http://localhost:8888
```

Output B:
```bash
...
site.options.location.href: http://localhost:8888/
...
```
---
Shouldn't the `--location` flag be just an override of the `location` option? Why the different results?

Thanks.
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AUCut.Author: metacriacoes

Comments.Body: If an explicit `--location` is passed to the CLI, it's supposed that you want to override the default `localhost` domain. For example, to use a different hostname (like `hostname.local`).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AQ0v2.Title: do not understand in the global state
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AQ0v2.Body:
I want to change data from data.xml
suppose
name = "jessie"
and when accessed .md and .tmpl.ts and other files the state has changed to jessie

where should I put this syntax in which file?

site.data("name", "jessie");


on the web: https://lume.land/docs/configuration/data/


thanks
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AQ0v2.Author: bobwatcherx

Comments.Body:
in _config.ts , sorry noob 

thanks
Comments.Author: bobwatcherx

Comments.Body:
In Lume the data is stored in `_data.*` files or `_data/*` folders. And their scope is the directory where the file saved is and subdirectories. There's a better explanation here: https://lume.land/docs/creating-pages/shared-data/

You can create a `_data.yml` file (or use other formats, like json, ts, js) with the data that you want to share with your pages:

```yml
# _data.yml

name: jessie
```

Placing this file in the root folder, will affects to all pages of your site. If you save it in a subdirectory, only affects to the pages in this subdirectory (and sub-subdirectories).

`site.data("name", "jessie")` is equivalent to create a `_data` file in the root folder, but it's more practical for plugins, so they can inject their data in the site, or if you don't want to create a file only to add a variable.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4Ago1v.Title: Advanced Configuration for UnoCSS: Need Guidance
D_kwDOEYAvFc4Ago1v.Author: alecStewart1

Comments.Body:
```
Type 'import(".../$node_modules/.deno/@unocss+core@66.3.3/$node_modules/@unocss/core/dist/index").UserConfig<object>' is not assignable to type 'import(".../$node_modules/.deno/@unocss+core@66.2.0/$node_modules/@unocss/core/dist/index").UserConfig<object>'.
```
I think Deno is mixing different npm versions of unocss with different types. (`@unocss+core@66.3.3` and `@unocss+core@66.2.0`). 
Try to import the version used by Lume, which is 66.2.0 (https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/v3.0.4/deps/unocss.ts)
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4Abwuo.Title: Issues making tailwindcss plugin gen css files for website
D_kwDOEYAvFc4Abwuo.Body:
using the plugin i dont get my website styled, while using the cdn version does. what am i doing wrong or is there a bug?
https://github.com/XodiumSoftware/XodiumSoftware.github.io
D_kwDOEYAvFc4Abwuo.Author: illyrius666

Comments.Body:
This is because the `.html` files are copied (with `site.copyRemainingFiles`) so they are not processed by the tailwind plugin.

You can change the extension `index.html` to `index.vto` to load the files (instead of simply being copied) so they will be processed.

FYI, this is a recurrent issue, and I'm thinking of changing this behavior in Lume v3 (more info: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/issues/571) but I didn't find yet a good alternative.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4ARcKj.Title: Server middleware only on Deno Deploy?
D_kwDOEYAvFc4ARcKj.Body: I see that [on_demand](https://lume.land/plugins/on_demand/) only works on Deno Deploy. By looking at the code I see it relies on server and http modules but nothing specific to Deno Deploy. Does it really only work on Deno Deploy and what prevents it from running the server on any other deno server runtime?
D_kwDOEYAvFc4ARcKj.Author: shishkin

Comments.Body:
Ok, maybe the message is not correct. It only works on Deno runtimes. In fact, it works better in Deno CLI than Deno Deploy (Deno Deploy has some limitations like not supporting dynamic imports).

Besides Deno Deploy, I don't know any hosting service providing a Deno environment, so this is why I added this warning.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4ARwW2.Title: HMR (hot module reloading), a la Vite?

Coming from Vite-based projects loosing page state on reloads feels super no bueno.
D_kwDOEYAvFc4ARwW2.Author: jaymakes11

Comments.Body:
HMR is hard to implement and probably out of the scope of Lume.
It depends on the frontend framework (react, vue, etc) and the bundle library. The library used by Lume is esbuild that doesn't support HMR https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/issues/464
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4Ae6hg.Title: Help understanding use of JS assets in Lume Components
D_kwDOEYAvFc4Ae6hg.Body:
Hi,
I've been studying the documentation on Components, and playing around with an example, and running into errors such that I don't really get what's going on with JS 'assets'

I get that Lume Components are meant for generating static html. CSS behaves as I would expect: it gets merged into `components.css` in the output. I can't get JS to work, however. It seems to be executing in the build side (e.g. I  get the error 'document global is not available in Deno' when I'm writing code intended to be executed in the browser).

Am I misunderstanding how this is supposed to be used? If this isn't how I should use JS Component assets, why provide the mechanism at all? Adding to my confusion is that I need to subsequently include `components.css` and `components.js` elsewhere in my templates, which feels again like it kind of defeats the purpose of encapsulating stuff in components (except of course stripping unused assets at build time).

I've tried several times to use components to isolate pieces of UI with styling and some lightweight JS needs, but keep running up against this issue and it seems easier to just write the JS in a 'global' script instead.

Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AS-4m.Title: Building a site with JSX and MDX plugins
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AS-4m.Author: ootoovak

Comments.Body:
Hi.
Indeed, `post.data.content` contains the raw file content of the page. The rendered content is stored in `post.content`.

But `post.content` not only contains the MDX code rendered but also the layout used to wrap this code. There's similar issue at https://github.com/lumeland/lume/issues/357 regarding to how to access to the rendered content before being merged with the layout, and the only current way is by creating a "proxy" layout to store the content.

Due this is a recurrent issue, I will find a way to integrate this behavior natively on Lume.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Thank you very much for the quick reply @oscarotero! I look forward to the change, and again, thanks for creating and sharing Lume. 🙂
Comments.Author: ootoovak

D_kwDOEYAvFc4Ac9FN.Title: Whats the best approach to modify a theme?
D_kwDOEYAvFc4Ac9FN.Body:
Hello everyone

I found myself using [Top theme](https://github.com/tarugoconf/top). However, I wanna modify or add new blocks. What should be the best approach?

Should I fork the Top theme, then point my project to that one instead? How should I do that in that case. I understand that I could use `--theme`, but not sure if how, since there it not that much documentation about...
D_kwDOEYAvFc4Ac9FN.Author: jesusfj710

Comments.Body:
Hi Jesús.

I need to add some documentation to the TOP theme and probably make some changes to make the customization easier.

For now, this is what we have:
- If you want to customize the styles, just edit the file `styles/theme.css` to override the default styles and configure the theme variables (colors, typography, etc).
- If you want to change the html code generated by an existing block, you have to download the template file in local and change it. For example, to edit the faq block, create locally the file `_includes/blocks/faq/faq.vto` and edit it. Lume will use this file instead of the remote file of the theme.
- If you want to add new blocks, the theme is not ready for that yet. It's possible to create the new block folder in `_includes/blocks/` but there's no way (for now) to configure the CMS to show the new block in the block selector. So the only way is forking the theme and add the new block there. Once the block is created, you have to do the following to use it:
  - Update the _cms.ts config to load the new block: https://github.com/tarugoconf/top/blob/main/_cms.ts#L21
  - Update the main styles file to import the styles of the new block: https://github.com/tarugoconf/top/blob/main/src/styles/main.css#L19
  - If you think the new block can be useful for more people, please do a Pull request :)
  - That's all, I have to implement a way to load new blocks dinamically without need to clone the source repo, but for now it's the only way.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDU3NDky.Title: import lume failed (Netlify deploy)
MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDU3NDky.Body:
I am getting this error message: 
```
Error: Import 'https://deno.land/x/lume@v0.25.3/deps/yaml.js' failed: 404 Not Found
8:18:24 PM:     at file:///opt/build/repo/admin/config.tmpl.js#1626146304012:1:0
```
My `config.tmpl.js` file can be viewed [here](https://github.com/Isaac-Tait/macadamGrinding_denoStyle/blob/main/admin/config.tmpl.js).

I am not certain how to go about clearing the error so Netlify can build/deploy my site. Any pointers would be appreciated. Cheers!
MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDU3NDky.Author: Isaac-Tait

Comments.Body:
Hi.
Lume is using typescript now, so you have to update your imported files from `*.js` to `*.ts`.
In this file it should be:

```ts
import { stringify } from "lume/deps/yaml.ts";
```

`_config.js` is still working for now with `*.js` but I encourage to change to `*.ts`, to prevent future errors when javascript files are removed.
Thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Thank you @oscarotero I made that small fix and the error is gone and the Netlify deploy was built. Cheers!
Comments.Author: Isaac-Tait

[I am new to Lume so this may be a stupid/obvious question]

There is a really nice Partials API in Deno Fresh that has a "nav partials" feature that you apply to the app router with one line of code and then every page turns into a partial, so you can change pages without triggering a browser reload. 

Does something like this exist in Lume?
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AhaTC.Author: knotbin

Comments.Body:
Lume is not a framework for frontend, so it doesn't include this feature out the box.
But you can use any of the many libraries that implement something similar, like [Barba](https://barba.js.org/), [Turbo](https://turbo.hotwired.dev/) or [Page-loader](https://github.com/oom-components/page-loader) (this one was created by me).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4ARP7l.Body:
I built a category and tag page and faced a conflict error with the tag and category page. On my website, I have more than 150 posts on it. ``` Error: A page will overwrite another page. Use distinct `url` values to resolve the conflict. - page: /tag[49].tmpl.js - previousPage: /tag[43].tmpl.js - destination: /tag/npm-cli/index.html at Writer.savePage (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.12.1/core/writer.ts:84:15) at async (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.12.1/core/writer.ts:43:13) at async concurrent (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.12.1/core/utils.ts:173:3) at async Writer.savePages (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.12.1/core/writer.ts:40:5) at async Site.build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.12.1/core/site.ts:486:19) at async build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.12.1/cli/build.ts:36:3) at async Command.execute (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.2/command/command.ts:1463:7) at async (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.12.1/cli.ts:147:3) ``` I used `search.tags()` inbuilt function to genrate a tag ``` // tag.tmpl.js export const layout = "layouts/tag.njk"; export default function* ({ search }) { for (const tag of search.tags()) { yield { url: `/tag/${tag}/`, title: `Tagged “${tag}”`, type:"tag", tag, }; } } ``` The tag page shows a specific category, myblog.com/tag/html ``` // tag.njk --- layout: layouts/base.njk title: Tags ---

Tags

{% for page in search.pages("type=tag","tag") %} {{ page.data.tag }} {% endfor %}
``` To build a category page, I use the `search.values("category")` function to get all categories. The categories page shows a list of all categories. ``` // categories.njk --- layout: layouts/base.njk title: Categories ---

Categories page

{% for category in search.values("category") %} {{ category }} {% endfor %}
``` --- The category page shows a specific category, `myblog.com/category/html` ``` // category.tmpl.js export const layout = "layouts/category.njk"; export default function* (props) { console.log(props, ' props inside category '); const { search }= props for (const category of search.values("category") ) { yield { url: `/category/${category}/`, title: `Categoryed “${category}”`, type: "category", category, }; } } ``` I know my
D_kwDOEYAvFc4ARP7l.Author: officialrajdeepsingh

Comments.Body:
Looks like you have two tags that outputs the same url, so they produce this error.
This check is done case insensitive, so `/tags/NPM-cli/` and `/tags/npm-cli/` are considered the same url, even if they are generated from two different tags. Probably you have the same tag with different cases.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body:
I recently solved it in my blog, but currently, I am working with 15 posts. If I am doing 150+ posts, it is tough to solve all tags. I find this behaviour is a bug. Because in my case, this error occurs due to two tags 
1. lume does not recognise both `/tags/Npm-cli/` and  `/tags/npm-cli/` tags are the same
2. The second in the other  `/tags/npm ` tags has space, and lume also considers a different tag.
Comments.Author: officialrajdeepsingh

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AYH_n.Title: Restart server on changes in non-reloadable files
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AYH_n.Body:
## The Problem

When using live-reload unfriendly components like ESM modules (`.ts`) or JSX, you have to reload the server manually for every single changes. This is a lot of work.

adding `--watch` option also does not work:

`echo \"import 'lume/cli.ts'\" | deno run --watch=_includes/foo.ts --unstable -A -`

## Suggested Changes

allow users to configure server so the server would completely restart on file changes such as `.ts` or `.tsx`.
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AYH_n.Author: scarf005

Comments.Body:
Yes, this is a limitation of Deno. It doesn't have any mecanism to invalidate an import and reload it, without restarting completely the script.

You can skip this limiation by using Lume components (files in _components folder) because they are not imported as esm (with `import name from "url"`). js/ts layers don't have this limitation neither because they are loaded using the same technique as components.
Does this fix your problem?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Nice! Here's a paraphrase for searchability, since it took me a long time to find this: when using code imported in `_config.ts`, such as your own plugins, live reload is not triggered. The above provides a workaround, using Deno's `--experimental-hmr` flag, where the entire server is restarted, which is at least an improvement over having to kill/restart the process by hand. More detail is provided in [this comment](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/issues/590#issuecomment-2031648988), which works for me on Deno 2.1.4 and Lume 2.4.3 (I presume this workaround is **not** made obsolete by `watcher.include` added in 2.3.1?)

D_kwDOEYAvFc4APcsM.Title: Blog posts in folders (with assets)/nested shortcodes
D_kwDOEYAvFc4APcsM.Body:
Hi all!

After playing with Lume I am blown away by its flexibility. Excellent work, guys!

I would like to migrate a number of rather large scale Hugo and Eleventy projects to Lume and I have a few questions:

- Is it/how is it possible to use a Hugo-like folder structure for a blog?
- Is it possible to copy assets (and process images with Imagick) from within these folders?

```
## In

blog/
├─ 2022-05-01_first-post/
│  ├─ index.md
│  ├─ an-image.png
│  └─ a-pdf-file.pdf
├─ 2022-05-02_second-post.md
└─ 2022-05-03_third-post/
   ├─ index.md
   └─ another-image.png

## Out

blog/
├─ first-post/
│  ├─ index.html
│  └─ an-image.png
├─ second-post.html
└─ third-post/
   ├─ index.html
   └─ another-image.png

```

- Is there an equivalent of Eleventy’s *paired shortcodes.* This is an easy example of what I would like to do:

```
site.addHelper('blockquote', blockquote, { body: 'true' });

{% blockquote author="XY", link="https…" %}
This is a quote formatted with **Markdown**.

Another line of text.
{% endblockquote %}

Thank you very much!
D_kwDOEYAvFc4APcsM.Author: Brixy

Comments.Body:
I've created an issue to support dates in directories: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/issues/198

But I have some questions?:
- It's required the file name to be "index.md" (`posts/2022-05-01_first-post/index.md`) or it works with any filename? (like `posts/2022-05-01_first-post/first-post.md`)
- Does it works if the date is in a parent directory? (like `posts/2022-05-01_first-post/first-post/index.md`)

This week I'd like to release a new Lume version and want to include this.
Thanks!
Comments.Author: oscarotero
Brixy

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AVfks.Title: Example of using TSX (or JSX) for a component?
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AVfks.Author: bbugh

Comments.Body:
The `_components` folder is not intended for client-side components, but only to be used by the templates at building time. They are similar to `_includes` but with a couple of differences:

- They not only render HTML but also can export CSS and JavaScript
- They are preloaded so are accesible from the `comp` variable.

If you want interactive client-side components (with `onClick` callbaks, etc), you need the esbuild plugin to compile your js code. But the code architecture is up to you (Lume is not a frontend framework).

If you only want to use JSX to generate static HTML, then you can use the [JSX](https://lume.land/plugins/jsx/) or [JSX Preact](https://lume.land/plugins/jsx_preact/) plugins and the `_components/test_button.tsx` will work.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AXA4I.Title: How to handle interactive elements in posts when using the feed plugin
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AXA4I.Body:
Hi, I have a site set up using `theme-simple-blog` and I occasionally want to create posts that have interactive elements in them, for example a web map. However, these interactive elements are not supported in RSS feeds. Is there a way to strip out `script` tags and/or provide image fallbacks for them? 

Here is the specific article on my site that I'm speaking to: https://zeigert.com/posts/where-to-find-obsidian-colors-at-glass-buttes/

It contains a `<div>` and a `<script>` tag that builds the web map in that div on the client. It builds and renders fine on the web and there are no errors when building the feed. However, when I view the feed it's clear that the feed includes some unsupported content:

https://github.com/lumeland/lume/assets/7832935/5139ca59-b380-4712-8268-c06e88e08908

This might be be more broad than Lume, but thanks anyway for any suggestions. :heart:
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AXA4I.Author: ajzeigert

Comments.Body:
A solution is to place this code in the head of the page (using the `extra_head` value of the post).

I guess you're already using this value for your custom styles, so if you set it again in the post, the previous value is overridden.

I'm thinking this value should have the [`stringArray` merge strategy](https://lume.land/docs/core/merged-keys/#stringarray-mode): instead of overriding the value, it creates an array with the two values (the same as how tags work). Now we only had to edit the layout to iterate this array and print all values.

what do you think?
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4Aenzc.Title: Any plans on publishing Lume to JSR?
D_kwDOEYAvFc4Aenzc.Body:
Right now, if I want to publish a package that depends on Lume to JSR, then it’s not possible. See here for an example:

```sh
deno publish --dry-run
Checking for slow types in the public API...
error[invalid-external-import]: invalid import to a non-JSR 'https' specifier
 --> /Users/kimchi/dev/remote/github.com/yeskunall/lume-umami/mod.ts:1:29
  |
1 | import type { Plugin } from "lume/core/site.ts";
  |                             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ the specifier
  |
  = hint: replace this import with one from jsr or npm, or vendor the dependency into your package

  info: the import was resolved to 'https://deno.land/x/lume@v2.5.2/core/site.ts'
  info: this specifier is not allowed to be imported on jsr
  info: jsr only supports importing `jsr:`, `npm:`, `data:`, `bun:`, and `node:` specifiers
  docs: https://jsr.io/go/invalid-external-import

error: Found 1 problem
```

That’s because [HTTP modules are supported in Deno, but not in JSR](https://jsr.io/docs/migrate-x-to-jsr#https-modules-supported-in-deno-not-in-jsr-packages).

In the same doc they state that deno.land/x will not be discontinued of shut down*:

> There are no plans to discontinue or shut down deno.land/x. Module authors can continue to publish and update modules there, and Deno code using it will continue to function.

However, by not publishing to JSR we miss out on their [DX features](https://jsr.io/docs/why#outstanding-developer-experience), the 2 main ones (for me) being:

- Automatic API documentation generation from source code
- Zero-config [publishing from GitHub Actions](https://jsr.io/docs/publishing-packages#publishing-from-github-actions)

###### \*Personally, I have come to not trust these statements. They are akin to “forever” (end of time) or “unlimited” plans, which frankly, don’t exist. But that’s a _me_ problem and I get that.

On top of that, deno.land/x has some concerns for which [JSR was created](https://jsr.io/docs/other-registries#denolandx), a big one being its lack of SemVer enforcement.

I’m opening this as a discussion to get the conversation started, but I understand if this is not a priority for Lume (as of yet).

As always, thanks for your work on Lume! 💖
D_kwDOEYAvFc4Aenzc.Author: yeskunall

Comments.Body:
Hi @yeskunall 

There are no plans to move Lume to JSR, at least in the medium term, until some changes are made in JSR. I don't feel confortable with some of its design decisions.

- The `exports` key make the package not compatible with the filesystem and HTTP. I had to create [this utility to minimize this issue](https://github.com/oscarotero/jsr-pub). This key also make the JSR packages incompatible with standard ES modules (see this issue: https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/28139)
- JSR is not compatible with import maps. You have more context here: https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/23504
- It's not easy to test a package without publishing it. Lume allows to use a `dev` version based on the commit hash. Even NPM ecosystem has something like this https://github.com/stackblitz-labs/pkg.pr.new
- Despite JSR claims to be "TypeScript first package registry", there are a lot of TypeScript features that don't work in JSR (JSX support, implicit types are marked as "slow types", etc).
- Dynamic imports in JSR are not fully supported. There are many issues with that, specially if you want to import local files (something that Lume does). I think it's not a good idea that JSR modify your code before publishing.
- It's vendor lock-in. Once you are in JSR it's not easy to exit. It's a non-standard centralized solution. For example, it's not easy to move your packages to a different instance in the future.

HTTP imports are a superior solution to me (despite their flaws, I know they are not perfect). They don't have any of these problems.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDA5NDIw.Title: Theming strategy?
MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDA5NDIw.Body:
I was curious what the approach should be to translate a theme from Hugo to Lume. Specifically, we built out a Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS) based Hugo theme that we'd like to start with but I did not see any theming opinions in the Lume documentation. 

How should we go about porting our [SLDS Hugo Theme](https://github.com/netspective-studios/hugo-theme-sf-lightning) over to Lume? There's nothing special about our theme, it's basically SLDS components make accessible through Hugo and we'd want to do the same thing with Lume.

I get the sense that [Lume Layouts](https://lumeland.github.io/creating-pages/layouts/) is where we'd build our "theme" or is there another strategy that the community suggests?
MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDA5NDIw.Author: shah

Comments.Body: Lume is unopinionated and doesn't have a "standard" theming system. This Hugo theme could be ported by translating the layouts files to nunjucks (or other template system available) and store them in `_includes`. The other files are just static files so it can be copied as is.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AaOC5.Title: Directory-Breadcrumb mapping
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AaOC5.Body:
Would it be possible to have a directory called `about` that is mapped to "About Us" when the breadcrumb is rendered? I couldn't figure out a way to do this. For example, the Simple Wiki theme uses `item.slug` for the directory when rendering the breadcrumb, so you end up with an unaesthetic breadcrumb:

My Site / about /

What I would like to do is have a breakcrumb that looks like this:

My Site / About Us /

and the "About Us" page links to /about/ (not /about-us/).
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AaOC5.Author: thdoan

Comments.Body:
The theme uses `item.slug` if there isn't any `index.html` file in the directory. Otherwise it uses the page title.
If your url structure is, for example:

```
/
/about/
/about/team/
/about/team/laura/
```

The breadcrumb should display the title page of every directory. But if there are intermediate directories, without pages, like

```
/
/about/
/about/team/laura/
```

The folder `/about/team/` doesn't have a page, so the breadcrumb doesn't know what to show and use the slug. See [the documentation for more info](https://lume.land/plugins/nav/#menu).
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: @oscarotero what you explained makes sense -- in my initial testing I didn't create an index page for `/about/`.
Comments.Author: thdoan

D_kwDOEYAvFc4Aaqs_.Title: Expanding scope of Picture Plugin or making it composable
D_kwDOEYAvFc4Aaqs_.Body: Hello @oscarotero ! I have been studying the picture plugin the last few days, and come to the conclusion (correct me if I'm wrong) that it supports one of multiple Responsive Images use cases: a. resolution-switching, i.e. telling the browser about different _sizes_ of images to use with media queries (**not supported**; needs an `` with `srcset` and `sizes` but the plugin outputs `` elements) b. multiple filetypes and pixel densities to allow the browser to choose the best one (**supported**) c. art direction, i.e. directing the browser to use different _versions_ of images with media queries, e.g. a cropped version on narrow screens (**not supported**; needs a `media` attribute on the `` element in `` but the plugin doesn't support that); (see [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/HTML/Multimedia_and_embedding/Responsive_images) for an overview of these use-cases). What I did for my immediate use-case ((a) above) was create a new plugin copying lots of the code, but only outputting ``s. I also had to add a shared data file as well as another processing step to add the `sizes` property to images before my plugin ran and to handle some conditional cases. All in all, it was quite straightforward to adapt, and the flexibility of Lume's processing mechanism is very nice. However, there is a bunch of helper code in the Picture plugin which it would be a pity to re-implement (creating the sources and transforms and such). I was wondering: 1. would it be an idea to extend the Picture plugin to handle more use-cases? I would be willing to help with that, as time allowed. 2. alternatively, could some of the internal functions and interfaces get exported, so that another plugin for resolution switching could make use of that? Cheers! Hans

Comments.Body: Hi @hpfast The picture plugin outputs `` or ``, it depends on the number of `` elements needed. If the image is required only in one format, instead of creating a `` with just one ``, it outputs a `` with `srcset` attribute. [Here's the code](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/main/plugins/picture.ts#L160). In short, the plugin only handles the combination of different formats and pixel densities. It doesn't handle media queries because it would be complicated and tricky (there are infinite combinations). But I had a similar request the other day [on discord](https://discord.com/channels/794537085641818124/1022269532619952180/threads/1262834811924381856) and I'm thinking on implementing support for `` elements in order to be able to do this: ```html ``` Supporting this, you could create a `` for each different media query you want to support (which would cover the art direction use case) and the plugin would complete it with the remaining `source` elements to support formats and/or pixel density. What do you think? If you like the idea and want ho help with that, I'll appreciate a lot.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: The more I study it, the more it seems like my use-case should already be supported. Either I've found a bug or I'm doing something wrong. I'd like to extract this from my project and make an isolated test case, alongside a reference implementation of ​ and ​, but I need to do some other things first. I'll get back with this soon On Tuesday, July 30th, 2024 at 12:49, Óscar Otero ***@***.***> wrote: > Sizes attribute is already supported (see PR here: [#482](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/pull/482)) > > I like the idea of making srcset optional in the (taking the value of the as default). So the input code of my example would be simpler: > > < > > picture > >> > > < > > source > > media > > =" > > (min-width: 1920px) > > " > > transform-images > > =" > > webp avif > > " > >> > > < > > source > > media > > =" > > (min-width: 1280px) > > " > > transform-images > > =" > > webp avif 1280 > > " > >> > > < > > source > > media > > =" > > (min-width: 480px) > > " > > transform-images > > =" > > webp avif 480 > > " > >> > > < > > img > > alt > > =" > > … > > " > > src > > =" > > /img/img.jpg > > " > >> > > > picture > >> > > I rather only one attribute (transform-images) instead of adding additional attributes because it's clearer, specially if you want to override them. > > This attribute can handle values of different types that the plugin understand. For example in avif webp ***@***.***, avif and webp are formats and 300 is a image size (adding @2 to create a version for 2x density). Maybe we can include [number]w syntax to generate images of a specific width, without taking into account the density. I.e. avif webp 300w. > > — > Reply to this email directly, [view it on GitHub](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/discussions/637#discussioncomment-10189431), or [unsubscribe](https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AA35I2W6KD6W36UEZRVMVQDZO5VUHAVCNFSM6AAAAABLUDM2Y6VHI2DSMVQWIX3LMV43URDJONRXK43TNFXW4Q3PNVWWK3TUHMYTAMJYHE2DGMI). > You are receiving this because you were mentioned.Message ID: ***@***.***>

MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDEzNzEz.Title: Was performance the primary reason to choose JS vs. TypeScript?
MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDEzNzEz.Body: I was curious why JavaScript, instead of TypeScript, was chosen as the language for implementation. As the project grows and will scale to more developers, will not having type-safety through TypeScript become an issue? I assume JS was chosen for startup performance reasons? Or was there something else about TypeScript in Deno that was not a good idea?
MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDEzNzEz.Author: shah

Comments.Body:
I have been asked about TypeScript a lot of times! The reason is just a matter of taste. I'm not a fan of TypeScript because it's too verbose and harder to debug. I know the benefits of working with types (I'd love that javascript had native support for that) but to me, typescript is just another layer that needs to be compiled and maintained to make it work. I remember when Deno developers decided to move all internal TS code to JS: https://github.com/denoland/deno/pull/6793

Anyway, I know that a Deno project not written in Typescript is weird to the community, and I have planned to implement Typescript, so other developers can extend Lume with other plugings easily. The version v1.0.0 will be 100% Typescript.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4ASGBc.Title: Imagick output
D_kwDOEYAvFc4ASGBc.Body:
Following [the docs here](https://lume.land/plugins/imagick/), I have Imagick creating several sizes and formats. However, the output doesn't seem to generate a `picture` element to actually use the optimized sizes.

Does the plugin not generate a default `picture` like the [11ty Image plugin](https://www.11ty.dev/docs/plugins/image/#asynchronous-shortcode)? 

Could you give an example of how to modify the html output of optimized images?
D_kwDOEYAvFc4ASGBc.Author: simplerethink

Comments.Body:
Nope, Imagick plugin doesn't modify the HTML, only process the image files and output different versions (that you can use in HTML, CSS files or just as file to download).

Maybe this is an opportunity to create a `picture` plugin 🤔
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4ASC7a.Title: 11ty Image to Lume Imagick
D_kwDOEYAvFc4ASC7a.Author: simplerethink

Comments.Body:
Lume has the [imagick](https://lume.land/plugins/imagick/) plugin that can transform an image and outputs different versions. For example you can create a `_data.yml` in your images folder with the following data:

```yml
imagick:
  - resize: [450]
    format: [webp, jpeg, avif]
    suffix: -450
  - resize: [550]
    format: [webp, jpeg, avif]
    suffix: -550
  - resize: [800]
    format: [webp, jpeg, avif]
    suffix: -800
```

Or if you don't want to keep this configuration manually, you can create a  `_data.ts` file that generate this data for you:

```ts
const formats = ["webp", "jpeg", "avif"];
const sizes = [450, 550, 800];

export const imagick = sizes.map((size) => {
  return {
    resize: [size],
    formats,
    suffix: `-${size}`
  };
})
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Not sure it has any meaningful impact to you at this point, but just an FYI, since I see Netlify CMS mentioned throughout your project.

MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDQ0NzM0.Title: Does compiling browser-side JS from TS make sense?
MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDQ0NzM0.Body:
I am using Lume to build my site and it's going well (great job @oscarotero!). I am wondering about the following:

* I have a `lib/prime.js` JS file which are browser-side utility JS functions/classes
* I use config.copy('lib') to copy the prime.js library of scripts to my site
* I use `<script type="module">import * as lib from "./lib/prime.js" ... </script>` and my JS works fine in the HTML client

All the above works great but I'd love to create my browser-side code in TS and have it transpiled to JS as part of the Lume transformations. Is this something possible? Does it even make sense for Lume to do this or should it be out of scope? 

I think the above functionality might be implemented using the bundler plugin `https://lumeland.github.io/plugins/bundler/` but wasn't sure it was for Deno-specific bundling or for the browser.
MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDQ0NzM0.Author: shah

Comments.Body: I think I answered my own question -- using `bundler` plugin it converts all `*.ts` files into `*.js`. This does almost exactly what I need (now I have some TS-specific typing problems).
Comments.Author: shah

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AYUaX.Title: Using Nunjucks to generate Open Graph images
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AYUaX.Author: cgwrench

Comments.Body:
The advantage of Satori is it can output SVG using simple CSS and HTML, which is easier than generating the SVG code using a text-based engine like Nunjucks. But maybe this plugins should allow both options. I'll work on that for next versions.
Meanwhile, you can reuse the existing nunjucks environment:

```js
const format = site.formats.search(".njk"); // Get the configuration for the .njk file extension
const env = format.engines[0]; // Get the engine for Nunjucks
```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4ASR8Y.Title: How to access postcss filter in JSX component?
D_kwDOEYAvFc4ASR8Y.Author: shishkin

Comments.Body: Do you mean Lume components? (components stored in the `_components` folder). If so, the postcss plugin already process the css code because Lume create a css file with the CSS code generates by the used components (by default `/components.css`), and this file is processed as any other CSS file.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4ARkCv.Title: How to use prism.js plugin
D_kwDOEYAvFc4ARkCv.Body: I need some additional[ prism.js plugins](https://prismjs.com/#plugins) for my blog. But I can't find a way to install it.
D_kwDOEYAvFc4ARkCv.Author: officialrajdeepsingh

Comments.Body:
Prism is the only Lume dependency loaded from `esm.sh` instead of `npm:`. This makes difficult to install plugins ATM. The reason for using `esm.sh` is it fails on `npm:` on loading languages dynamically (see https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/15826).

I need to make some breaking changes in the plugin to switch to npm. Probably in 1.14.0.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDA5MTkw.Title: Migrating large Hugo site curiosity
MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDA5MTkw.Body:
Hi, we have a large site (over 100k pages which we build many times a day) that Hugo builds within a couple of minutes. We're very happy with Hugo performance and base functionality but we add on many Deno and shell scripts to do a lot of pre and post processing in Hugo which seems possible with Lume's plugins. 

For example, all our pages' markdown are generated from SQL data but all that work happens outside of Hugo in Deno scripts because Hugo has no hooks capability or ability to generate pages from PostgreSQL. 

I know this question would be difficult to answer but I was curious what the HTML build performance might be for over 100k markdown files in Lume and would love to hear some general opinions and wild guesses. :-)

This is a fantastic library, great work!

We would happy to be a trial site for a large Lume project if there's interest.
MDEwOkRpc2N1c3Npb24zNDA5MTkw.Author: shah

Comments.Body:
Hi, @shah 
Thanks for your interest in Lume. In terms of performance, I think Hugo is impossible to beat (any compiled language like Go will be always faster than a scripting language such javascript). Maybe using some wasm/rust libraries that can work on Deno could increase the performance.

I did some benchmarking some time ago here: https://github.com/lumeland/benchmark and Lume takes (more or less) the double of time to build the same site. If you think the ability of Lume to be extended with hooks and integrated with databases worths the performance reduction, I'd give it a try.

I'm open to PR and suggestions to improve performance.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AayhZ.Title: What makes mermaid.js cannot work on server-side, while other visualization libs can?
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AayhZ.Body:
On #465 there are 2 alternative options for Mermaid:
- Serea plugin: https://github.com/idris-maps/serea
- OI Lume Viz plugin: https://open-innovations.github.io/oi-lume-viz/

Why can they work while Mermaid can't? In general, I don't understand why:
- Mermaid should be run server-side here,
- A browser lib cannot be run server-side 
- _Two_ visualization libs just for this generator? Why can't one be enough?
- Do they have the same syntax with Mermaid? 

Any insight?
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AayhZ.Author: ooker777

Comments.Body:
Mermaid is a browser-side library and use some APIs not available in server side, like DOM, CSS, etc.
Other options are designed for server-side and don't use these APIs.
Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Hey, I don’t mean to go off topic, but [Pintora](https://github.com/hikerpig/pintora) is another alternative to Mermaid too.
Comments.Author: binhtran432k

D_kwDOEYAvFc4ARacj.Title: Enable commenting on lume with utteranc.es
D_kwDOEYAvFc4ARacj.Body:
Lume enables commenting on the site with [https://utteranc.es/](https://utteranc.es/). The utteranc is an open-source comment system base on github.

The function to detect the config file is this: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/main/core/utils/lume_config.ts#L55

For profiling, I use to change the `lume/` import map to my local clone:

```diff
"imports": {

Why to you think this is a problem? it's expected to load the `_config.ts` file.

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AbulK.Title: Component syntax in Vento
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AbulK.Body:
Hello,

I'm having a bit of trouble implementing components in Vento using the Lume docs. I'm trying to do this purely with Vento/Front Matter/YAML without any JS.

I can import a menu component using the `{{ include "_components/menu.vto" }}` syntax but with many components this is tedious and doesn't read well. But when trying to use the component syntax e.g. `{{ comp Menu }}{{ \comp}}` OR `{{ comp.menu() }}` I receive errors that the variable is undefined.

I'm also not sure about how to write the component file in Vento. For example, if I want to create a component called **Menu**, where in the file `menu.vto` should I be naming the component? Or is it based on the file name. Both the Lume and Vento documentation is a bit ambiguous about this.

Thanks!
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AbulK.Author: smcjustin

Comments.Body:
Hi! The appropiate way to use vento components is with `{{ comp.menu() }}`, or `{{ comp.menu }} here the content {{ /comp }}`. If you have errors about an undefined variable, maybe you're using a non existing variable in the component. To create vento components, just create a `.vto` file inside the `_components` folder. For example, `_components/menu.vto` and inside the file: ```html
    {{ for link of links }}
  • {{ link.text }}
    • {{ /for }}
``` To use the component, remember to pass the data: ```html {{ comp.menu({ links: [...] }) }} ```
Comments.Author: oscarotero

D_kwDOEYAvFc4ASC8q.Title: Get page hierarchical structure for breadcrumbs
D_kwDOEYAvFc4ASC8q.Body:
I have a file structure similar to this: ``` └── blog/ ├── parent-category1/ │ ├── post1.md │ └── child-category2/ │ └── post2.md ├── parent-category3/ │ └── ... └── ... ``` How can I generate breadcrumbs in Lume? For example, in `post2.md` I'd like to have output like: ```
  1. Parent Category1
  2. Child Category2
``` For `post1.md` I'd like to have output: ```
  1. Parent Category1
``` Basically, I'm looking to fetch the hierarchical structure of a page based on the position within the file structure. I have dozens of categories, so I'm looking for a dynamic way of doing this within the template.
D_kwDOEYAvFc4ASC8q.Author: simplerethink

D_kwDOEYAvFc4AWU6W.Title: Process filename on render?
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AWU6W.Body:
Hi Lume,

Thank you for beautiful software.  Really liking it so far.

So, I'm looking for a way to adjust filenames on render.  I'd like to programmatically include a version number, ideally from my site's `_data.yml` file.

To explain, I am using a somewhat strange piece of software that allows me to host individual static assets, but they're not versioned.  I'd like to know at a glance whether the assets I've uploaded are up to date.  Hence the manual versioning.

Any suggestion how this might be accomplished?

For example, something like this might work (but doesn't because it's meant for extensions):

```javascript
site.preprocess(
  ["*mah_folder_name*"],
  (page) => page.data.filename = page.src.path + "v1.2.3" + page.src.ext,
);
```

No worries if not, I'll write my own external postprocessor.  But it would be cool to manage it all in one place.

Thanks.
-Allan
D_kwDOEYAvFc4AWU6W.Author: allanberry

Comments.Author: oscarotero

Comments.Body: Whoa, that's perfect.  Simpler than expected by far.  Thank you!
Comments.Author: allanberry

Comments.Body: In version 3, @oscarotero perhaps this method is no longer supported?  I can't get it to work using a `_data.js` file.  The `url()` function doesn't seem to be called at runtime.
Comments.Author: allanberry

Comments.Body:
@allanberry It's supported but there are some changes in the `page.src` object.

`page.src.slug` no longer exist. You can use the `page.data.basename` variable.
Comments.Author: oscarotero
